  {
    "start_timestamp": "2026-03-07T22:01:19.927159Z",
    "stop_timestamp": "2026-03-07T22:02:40.802302Z",
    "flags": null,
    "type": "text",
    "text": "\n\n# Photodissociation Regions (PDRs) \u2014 Let's Build Something New\n\nGreat ambition! Let's start from the physics ground up, then identify the gaps, and then sketch out what a next-generation 3D PDR model could look like.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The Physics of PDRs\n\nA **Photodissociation Region** (or Photon-Dominated Region) is any zone of the interstellar medium where far-ultraviolet (FUV) photons (6 eV < h\u03bd < 13.6 eV) drive the chemistry, heating, and structure of the gas. They're ubiquitous \u2014 they exist at the surfaces of molecular clouds, protoplanetary disks, planetary nebulae, and anywhere neutral gas is exposed to UV radiation from hot stars.\n\n### 1.1 The Layered Structure\n\nThe classical picture is a stratified slab illuminated on one side by a FUV field (measured in units of the Habing field, G\u2080):\n\n**Surface (A_V ~ 0):** Fully ionized hydrogen, atomic carbon (C\u207a), atomic oxygen. Heating dominated by photoelectric effect on dust grains.\n\n**Transition zone (A_V ~ 1\u20132):** H \u2192 H\u2082 transition. Carbon goes from C\u207a \u2192 C\u2070 \u2192 CO. This is where the [CII] 158 \u03bcm and [OI] 63 \u03bcm fine-structure lines originate \u2014 the dominant coolants.\n\n**Deep interior (A_V > 3\u20135):** Fully molecular. CO is the main carbon carrier. Chemistry becomes rich \u2014 complex molecules form. Cooling shifts to CO rotational lines and dust thermal emission.\n\n### 1.2 Key Physical Processes\n\n**Heating mechanisms:**\n- Photoelectric effect on PAHs and small dust grains (dominant at the surface)\n- H\u2082 formation on grain surfaces (releases ~4.5 eV per molecule)\n- H\u2082 photodissociation pumping (UV pumping \u2192 cascade \u2192 collisional de-excitation)\n- Cosmic ray ionization heating (important in shielded regions)\n- Gas-grain collisional heating/cooling (deep regions, couples gas to dust temperature)\n\n**Cooling mechanisms:**\n- [CII] 158 \u03bcm fine-structure line (dominant at low A_V)\n- [OI] 63 \u03bcm and 145 \u03bcm lines\n- CO rotational ladder (dominant at high A_V)\n- H\u2082 ro-vibrational lines\n- [CI] 609/370 \u03bcm lines\n- Ly\u03b1 emission, dust continuum\n\n**Chemistry:**\n- H\u2082 formation on grain surfaces (rate depends on grain size distribution, temperature, surface properties)\n- CO formation via multiple pathways (OH + C, CH + O, etc.) and destruction by photodissociation\n- Self-shielding of H\u2082 and CO \u2014 line absorption rather than continuum, so shielding depends on column density of the molecule *itself*, not just dust\n- PAH chemistry \u2014 charge state, hydrogenation, affects photoelectric heating efficiency\n- Sulfur, nitrogen, and other networks for tracers like CS, HCN, N\u2082H\u207a\n\n**Radiative transfer:**\n- FUV continuum attenuation by dust (straightforward exponential with A_V)\n- H\u2082 line self-shielding (requires tracking ~5000 Lyman/Werner lines)\n- CO self-shielding and mutual shielding by H\u2082 (isotopologue-dependent)\n- Line emission in the cooling lines (escape probability or full transfer)\n- Dust continuum emission (modified blackbody, depends on grain properties and temperature)\n\n### 1.3 The Thermal and Chemical Balance\n\nThe steady-state assumption means solving, at every point:\n\n**Thermal balance:** \u0393(T, n, G\u2080, A_V) = \u039b(T, n, abundances)\n\n**Chemical balance:** Formation rate = Destruction rate for each species\n\nThese are coupled \u2014 temperature affects chemistry, chemistry affects cooling, cooling affects temperature. In practice you iterate to convergence.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Existing Models & Where The Gaps Are\n\n### Existing Codes\n\n| Model | Geometry | Key Features |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Cloudy** | 1D slab/sphere | Comprehensive microphysics, H II + PDR, huge chemical network |\n| **Meudon PDR** | 1D plane-parallel | Excellent H\u2082 treatment, careful self-shielding, benchmarked |\n| **KOSMA-\u03c4** | \"3D\" (clumpy ensemble) | Clumpy PDR via superposition of 1D spherical clumps; not true 3D RT |\n| **3D-PDR** (Bisbas+) | 3D (Healpix rays) | True 3D geometry on AMR/SPH, but simplified chemistry |\n| **UCL_PDR** | 1D | Time-dependent chemistry, good for dynamical contexts |\n\n### The Real Gaps\n\nHere's where the science is genuinely incomplete:\n\n**Gap 1 \u2014 Geometry and turbulence coupling.** Real molecular clouds are fractal, turbulent, filamentary. No current PDR code self-consistently couples MHD turbulence with PDR chemistry and full FUV radiative transfer. KOSMA-\u03c4 fakes 3D with clump ensembles. 3D-PDR does geometry but with reduced chemistry. Nobody does full 3D RT + full chemistry + turbulent dynamics together.\n\n**Gap 2 \u2014 Time-dependent, dynamical PDRs.** Most codes assume chemical and thermal steady state. But PDR fronts move, clouds are compressed by winds and radiation pressure, and turbulent mixing continually re-exposes molecular gas. The chemical timescales for CO formation (~10\u2075\u201310\u2076 yr) can be comparable to or longer than dynamical timescales in many environments.\n\n**Gap 3 \u2014 Grain physics fidelity.** The photoelectric heating efficiency depends sensitively on PAH abundance, size distribution, and charge state \u2014 all of which evolve *within* the PDR. Grain growth, shattering, and ice mantle formation/destruction aren't well coupled to the gas chemistry. H\u2082 formation rates on realistic grain surfaces (amorphous carbon vs silicate, temperature-dependent sticking) remain uncertain.\n\n**Gap 4 \u2014 Multi-scale problem.** The H/H\u2082 and C\u207a/C/CO transitions happen over tiny spatial scales (~10\u207b\u00b3 pc at high density), but clouds are pc-scale and emission is integrated over kpc-scale beams in extragalactic observations. Bridging this is an unsolved problem.\n\n**Gap 5 \u2014 Magnetic fields.** B-fields affect cloud structure, turbulence dissipation, and cosmic ray propagation. No PDR model includes magnetized structure self-consistently.\n\n**Gap 6 \u2014 Cosmic ray transport.** CRs are usually treated as a uniform background rate \u03b6. But CR attenuation, focusing, and exclusion by magnetic fields mean \u03b6 varies spatially in complex ways \u2014 this matters enormously for the deep chemistry.\n\n**Gap 7 \u2014 Benchmarking against resolved observations.** ALMA and JWST are now resolving PDR structures at sub-0.01 pc scales (e.g., the Orion Bar, NGC 7023). Models haven't kept pace with generating synthetic observables at this resolution from 3D models.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Designing a Next-Generation 3D PDR Model\n\nLet me propose an architecture that addresses the most tractable and impactful gaps:\n\n### Working Name: **PRISM-3D** *(PDR Radiative transfer with Integrated Structure and Microphysics)*\n\n### Core Design Principles\n\n**3D adaptive geometry** on unstructured meshes (Voronoi, like AREPO) or octree AMR. This naturally handles the multi-scale problem \u2014 refine at chemical transition fronts, coarsen in uniform regions.\n\n**Hybrid chemistry approach:**\n- Full network (~200 species, ~3000 reactions) solved at \"anchor points\" or on a coarser grid\n- Reduced network (~30 species) solved at full resolution using a machine-learned reduction (trained against the full network)\n- This is the key innovation \u2014 you get the speed of a reduced network with the accuracy of a full one, verified on-the-fly\n\n**Monte Carlo + ray-tracing RT hybrid:**\n- Use long-characteristics ray-tracing for the FUV continuum (fast, accurate for attenuation)\n- Use Monte Carlo for line emission (handles complex geometries naturally)\n- H\u2082 self-shielding via pre-computed shielding functions on column density (Draine & Bertoldi or updated Wolcott-Green tables), but evaluated along multiple sightlines per cell\n\n**Time-dependent option:**\n- Operator-split: advection (hydro) \u2192 chemistry \u2192 thermal \u2192 RT\n- Can be coupled to an external hydro code (e.g., AREPO, FLASH, PLUTO) as a post-processing or on-the-fly module\n- For steady-state applications, iterate to convergence as usual\n\n### Novel Components\n\n**1. ML-accelerated chemistry.** Train a neural ODE or graph neural network on the full chemical network to predict abundances given (n, T, G\u2080, A_V, \u03b6_CR, metallicity). Use the full network to periodically validate and retrain. This could give 100\u20131000\u00d7 speedup, making 3D feasible.\n\n**2. Self-consistent grain evolution module.** Track a discretized grain size distribution (e.g., 10\u201320 bins) that evolves due to shattering, coagulation, and sputtering. Compute photoelectric heating from the *actual* local grain distribution, not a fixed MRN. PAH abundance is a prediction, not a parameter.\n\n**3. Cosmic ray attenuation.** Implement column-density-dependent CR attenuation (Padovani+ 2009, 2018 prescriptions) along multiple sightlines, analogous to FUV shielding. This breaks the \"uniform \u03b6\" assumption cheaply.\n\n**4. Synthetic observation pipeline built in.** Every model run natively outputs:\n- Line intensity cubes for [CII], [OI], CO(1-0) through CO(15-14), [CI], H\u2082 S(0)\u2013S(7)\n- Dust continuum SEDs (using DustEM or similar, fed by the local grain distribution)\n- Column density maps of all species\n- Properly convolved to any telescope beam\n\n**5. GPU acceleration.** The RT (embarrassingly parallel over rays) and chemistry (embarrassingly parallel over cells) both map beautifully to GPU architectures. Target CUDA/HIP with a Python driver layer.\n\n### Architecture Sketch\n\n```\n\u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n\u2502              PRISM-3D PIPELINE              \u2502\n\u251c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2524\n\u2502                                             \u2502\n\u2502  INPUT: 3D density field                    \u2502\n\u2502    (from hydro sim, analytic, or observed)  \u2502\n\u2502                                             \u2502\n\u2502  \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510  \u2502   \u2502\n\u2502 What This Would Enable Scientifically\n\n- **Interpret JWST PDR observations** (like the PDRs4All program on the Orion Bar) with models that actually match the observed 3D complexity\n- **Predict CO-dark H\u2082 fractions** in 3D turbulent clouds \u2014 crucial for correcting the X_CO factor in galaxy observations\n- **Model extragalactic PDRs** with sub-grid prescriptions trained on resolved 3D runs, replacing the simplistic 1D slab or KOSMA-\u03c4 clump ensembles\n- **Study dynamical PDRs** \u2014 cloud evaporation, feedback, triggered star formation \u2014 with proper chemistry\n\n---\n\n## Where Should We Go From Here?\n\nThere are several directions we could dig into next. What interests you most?\n\n- Deep dive into the ML-accelerated chemistry approach (architecture, training data, validation strategy)\n- The radiative transfer algorithm design (ray-tracing schemes, H\u2082 self-shielding implementation)\n- Grain physics module design\n- Benchmarking strategy (against Meudon, 3D-PDR, and observations)\n- The software engineering side (language choice, parallelization, code structure)\n- Writing a science case / white paper for the model\n\nWhat's your background \u2014 are you coming at this from the astrophysics side, the computational side, or both?",

      "path": "/home/claude/prism3d/README.md",
      "file_text": "# PRISM-3D\n\n**PDR Radiative transfer with Integrated Structure and Microphysics**\n\nA next-generation 3D Photodissociation Region (PDR) code for modeling the physics\nand chemistry of UV-illuminated interstellar gas.\n\n## Key Features\n\n- **True 3D geometry** on octree AMR grids with adaptive refinement at chemical fronts\n- **Hybrid chemistry**: Full network (~100 species) + ML-reduced fast network (~30 species)\n- **Multi-ray FUV radiative transfer** using HEALPix-based ray tracing\n- **Self-consistent grain physics**: Evolving size distribution, PAH charge states\n- **Cosmic ray attenuation**: Column-density dependent \u03b6_CR along multiple sightlines\n- **Time-dependent option**: Operator-split hydro \u2192 chemistry \u2192 thermal \u2192 RT\n- **Synthetic observation pipeline**: Line cubes, dust SEDs, column density maps\n- **GPU-ready architecture**: Embarrassingly parallel chemistry and RT\n\n## Architecture\n\n```\nprism3d/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 core/              # Grid, cell data structures, main solver loop\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 grid.py        # 3D AMR octree grid\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 cell.py        # Cell data container\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 solver.py      # Main iteration driver\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 chemistry/         # Chemical network and solvers\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 network.py     # Reaction network definition\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 rates.py       # Rate coefficient calculations\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 solver.py      # ODE integrator for chemistry\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 species.py     # Species definitions and properties\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 radiative_transfer/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 fuv_rt.py      # FUV continuum ray tracing\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 shielding.py   # H2 and CO self-shielding functions\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 line_rt.py     # Line radiative transfer (escape probability)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 grains/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 size_dist.py   # Grain size distribution evolution\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 photoelectric.py # PE heating from realistic grain populations\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 h2_formation.py  # H2 formation on grain surfaces\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 thermal/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 heating.py     # All heating processes\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 cooling.py     # All cooling processes\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 balance.py     # Thermal equilibrium solver\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cosmic_rays/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 attenuation.py # Column-density dependent CR rate\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 observations/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 synthetic.py   # Synthetic line/continuum maps\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 convolution.py # Beam convolution utilities\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 utils/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 constants.py   # Physical constants in CGS\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 io.py          # I/O utilities\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tests/             # Unit and integration tests\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 examples/          # Example model setups\n```\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```python\nfrom prism3d.core.grid import OctreeGrid\nfrom prism3d.core.solver import PDRSolver\nfrom prism3d.examples.orion_bar import setup_orion_bar\n\n# Create a model of the Orion Bar PDR\ngrid = setup_orion_bar(n_base=64)\nsolver = PDRSolver(grid)\nsolver.run(max_iterations=200, convergence=1e-3)\n\n# Generate synthetic [CII] 158 \u03bcm map\nfrom prism3d.observations.synthetic import make_line_cube\ncii_cube = make_line_cube(grid, line='CII_158')\n```\n\n## Physics References\n\n- Tielens & Hollenbach (1985) \u2014 Foundational PDR model\n- Draine & Bertoldi (1996) \u2014 H2 self-shielding\n- Wolfire+ (2003) \u2014 Neutral ISM heating/cooling\n- Le Petit+ (2006) \u2014 Meudon PDR code\n- R\u00f6llig+ (2007) \u2014 PDR benchmark comparison\n- Bisbas+ (2012) \u2014 3D-PDR code\n- Padovani+ (2018) \u2014 Cosmic ray attenuation\n"

FUV continuum ray tracing\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 shielding.py   # H2 and CO self-shielding functions\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 line_rt.py     # Line radiative transfer (escape probability)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 grains/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 size_dist.py   # Grain size distribution evolution\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 photoelectric.py # PE heating from realistic grain populations\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 h2_formation.py  # H2 formation on grain surfaces\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 thermal/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 heating.py     # All heating processes\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 cooling.py     # All cooling processes\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 balance.py     # Thermal equilibrium solver\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cosmic_rays/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 attenuation.py # Column-density dependent CR rate\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 observations/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 synthetic.py   # Synthetic line/continuum maps\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 convolution.py # Beam convolution utilities\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 utils/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 constants.py   # Physical constants in CGS\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 io.py          # I/O utilities\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tests/             # Unit and integration tests\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 examples/          # Example model setups\n```\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```python\nfrom prism3d.core.grid import OctreeGrid\nfrom prism3d.core.solver import PDRSolver\nfrom prism3d.examples.orion_bar import setup_orion_bar\n\n# Create a model of the Orion Bar PDR\ngrid = setup_orion_bar(n_base=64)\nsolver = PDRSolver(grid)\nsolver.run(max_iterations=200, convergence=1e-3)\n\n# Generate synthetic [CII] 158 \u03bcm map\nfrom prism3d.observations.synthetic import make_line_cube\ncii_cube = make_line_cube(grid, line='CII_158')\n```\n\n## Physics References\n\n- Tielens & Hollenbach (1985) \u2014 Foundational PDR model\n- Draine & Bertoldi (1996) \u2014 H2 self-shielding\n- Wolfire+ (2003) \u2014 Neutral ISM heating/cooling\n- Le Petit+ (2006) \u2014 Meudon PDR code\n- R\u00f6llig+ (2007) \u2014 PDR benchmark comparison\n- Bisbas+ (2012) \u2014 3D-PDR code\n- Padovani+ (2018) \u2014 Cosmic ray attenuation\n",

Planck constant [erg\u00b7s]\nk_boltz = 1.380649e-16         # Boltzmann constant [erg/K]\nm_proton = 1.67262192e-24      # Proton mass [g]\nm_electron = 9.10938370e-28    # Electron mass [g]\ne_charge = 4.80320451e-10      # Electron charge [esu]\nG_grav = 6.67430e-8            # Gravitational constant [cm\u00b3/g/s\u00b2]\nsigma_sb = 5.670374e-5         # Stefan-Boltzmann [erg/cm\u00b2/s/K\u2074]\na_rad = 4.0 * sigma_sb / c_light  # Radiation constant\n\n# Atomic / molecular\nm_H = 1.6735575e-24            # Hydrogen atom mass [g]\nm_H2 = 2.0 * m_H               # H2 mass [g]\nm_He = 6.6464764e-24            # Helium mass [g]\nm_C = 1.9944235e-23             # Carbon mass [g]\nm_O = 2.6567629e-23             # Oxygen mass [g]\n\n# Energy conversions\neV_to_erg = 1.602176634e-12     # 1 eV in erg\neV_to_K = 1.160451812e4         # 1 eV in Kelvin\nRy_to_eV = 13.605693            # 1 Rydberg in eV\n\n# Astronomical units\npc_cm = 3.0856776e18            # Parsec in cm\nau_cm = 1.49597871e13           # AU in cm\nMsun_g = 1.98892e33             # Solar mass in g\nLsun_erg = 3.828e33             # Solar luminosity in erg/s\nyr_s = 3.15576e7                # Year in seconds\n\n# ISM parameters\nmu_H = 1.4                     # Mean molecular weight per H nucleus (with He)\nX_H = 0.7154                   # Hydrogen mass fraction (solar)\nY_He = 0.2703                  # Helium mass fraction (solar)\n\n# FUV radiation field\n# Habing field: G0 = 1 corresponds to 1.6e-3 erg/cm\u00b2/s integrated 6-13.6 eV\n# Draine field: chi = 1 corresponds to 2.7e-3 erg/cm\u00b2/s\nG0_to_flux = 1.6e-3            # G0=1 flux [erg/cm\u00b2/s] (Habing 1968)\nchi_to_flux = 2.7e-3           # chi=1 flux [erg/cm\u00b2/s] (Draine 1978)\nG0_to_chi = G0_to_flux / chi_to_flux  # ~0.59\n\n# Dust properties\nsigma_dust_FUV = 1.8e-21       # FUV dust cross section per H [cm\u00b2] (at ~1000 \u00c5)\n# A_V / N_H = 5.34e-22 mag cm\u00b2 (Bohlin+ 1978)\nAV_per_NH = 5.34e-22           # Visual extinction per H column [mag/cm\u207b\u00b2]\nNH_per_AV = 1.0 / AV_per_NH   # H column per A_V [cm\u207b\u00b2 / mag]\n# R_V = A_V / E(B-V), typically 3.1\nR_V = 3.1\n# FUV extinction relative to visual: A_FUV / A_V\ntau_FUV_per_AV = 1.8           # ~1.8 for standard ISM dust\n\n# Standard solar abundances (by number, relative to H)\n# Asplund+ (2009) values\nsolar_abundances = {\n    'H':  1.0,\n    'He': 8.51e-2,\n    'C':  2.69e-4,\n    'N':  6.76e-5,\n    'O':  4.90e-4,\n    'Ne': 8.51e-5,\n    'Mg': 3.98e-5,\n    'Si': 3.24e-5,\n    'S':  1.32e-5,\n    'Fe': 3.16e-5,\n}\n\n# Fine-structure line data\n# Format: (wavelength_um, frequency_Hz, Einstein_A [s\u207b\u00b9], E_upper/k [K], g_upper, g_lower)\nfine_structure_lines = {\n    'CII_158':  (157.741e-4, 1.9005e12, 2.321e-6, 91.21, 4, 2),\n    'OI_63':    (63.184e-4,  4.7448e12, 8.910e-5, 227.71, 3, 5),\n    'OI_145':   (145.525e-4, 2.0604e12, 1.750e-5, 326.58, 1, 3),\n    'CI_609':   (609.135e-4, 4.9231e11, 7.880e-8, 23.62, 3, 1),\n    'CI_370':   (370.415e-4, 8.0929e11, 2.650e-7, 62.46, 5, 3),\n}\n\n# CO rotational line data\n# CO B_rot = 57635.968 MHz, first few transitions\nCO_B_rot = 57.635968e9  # Hz\nCO_dipole = 0.1098e-18  # Debye in esu\u00b7cm\n\n# H2 parameters\nH2_dissociation_energy = 4.478  # eV\nH2_binding_energy_grain = 0.04  # eV (physisorption on silicate)\n\n\ndef mean_molecular_weight(x_H2, x_HI, x_e):\n    \"\"\"\n    Mean molecular weight per particle given H2, HI fractions and electron fraction.\n    x_H2 = n(H2)/n_H, x_HI = n(HI)/n_H, x_e = n(e)/n_H\n    \"\"\"\n    # Total particles per H nucleus: x_HI + x_H2 (each H2 = 1 particle) + He/H + x_e\n    He_per_H = solar_abundances['He']\n    n_particles_per_nH = x_HI + x_H2 + He_per_H + x_e\n    # Mean mass per H: (1 + 4*He/H) * m_H\n    mean_mass = (1.0 + 4.0 * He_per_H) * m_H\n    mu = mean_mass / (n_particles_per_nH * m_H)\n    return mu\n",

G0=1 flux [erg/cm\u00b2/s] (Habing 1968)\nchi_to_flux = 2.7e-3           # chi=1 flux [erg/cm\u00b2/s] (Draine 1978)\nG0_to_chi = G0_to_flux / chi_to_flux  # ~0.59\n\n# Dust properties\nsigma_dust_FUV = 1.8e-21       # H column per A_V [cm\u207b\u00b2 / mag]\n# R_V = A_V / E(B-V), typically 3.1\nR_V = 3.1\n# FUV extinction relative to visual: A_FUV / A_V\ntau_FUV_per_AV = 1.8           # ~1.8 for standard ISM dust\n\n# Standard solar abundances (by number, relative to H)\n# Asplund+ (2009) values\nsolar_abundances = {\n    'H':  1.0,\n    'He': 8.51e-2,\n    'C':  2.69e-4,\n    'N':  6.76e-5,\n    'O':  4.90e-4,\n    'Ne': 8.51e-5,\n    'Mg': 3.98e-5,\n    'Si': 3.24e-5,\n    'S':  1.32e-5,\n    'Fe': 3.16e-5,\n}\n\n# Fine-structure line data\n# Format: (wavelength_um, frequency_Hz, Einstein_A [s\u207b\u00b9], E_upper/k [K], g_upper, g_lower)\nfine_structure_lines = {\n    'CII_158':  (157.741e-4, 1.9005e12, 2.321e-6, 91.21, 4, 2),\n    'OI_63':    (63.184e-4,  4.7448e12, 8.910e-5, 227.71, 3, 5),\n    'OI_145':   (145.525e-4, 2.0604e12, 1.750e-5, 326.58, 1, 3),\n    'CI_609':   (609.135e-4, 4.9231e11, 7.880e-8, 23.62, 3, 1),\n    'CI_370':   (370.415e-4, 8.0929e11, 2.650e-7, 62.46, 5, 3),\n}\n\n# CO rotational line data\n# CO B_rot = 57635.968 MHz, first few transitions\nCO_B_rot = 57.635968e9  # Hz\nCO_dipole = 0.1098e-18  # Debye in esu\u00b7cm\n\n# H2 parameters\nH2_dissociation_energy = 4.478  # eV\nH2_binding_energy_grain = 0.04  # eV (physisorption on silicate)\n\n\ndef mean_molecular_weight(x_H2, x_HI, x_e):\n    \"\"\"\n    Mean molecular weight per particle given H2, HI fractions and electron fraction.\n    x_H2 = n(H2)/n_H, x_HI = n(HI)/n_H, x_e = n(e)/n_H\n    \"\"\"\n    # Total particles per H nucleus: x_HI + x_H2 (each H2 = 1 particle) + He/H + x_e\n    He_per_H = solar_abundances['He']\n    n_particles_per_nH = x_HI + x_H2 + He_per_H + x_e\n    # Mean mass per H: (1 + 4*He/H) * m_H\n    mean_mass = (1.0 + 4.0 * He_per_H) * m_H\n    mu = mean_mass / (n_particles_per_nH * m_H)\n    return mu\n",

      "file_text": "\"\"\"\n3D Adaptive Mesh Refinement (AMR) Octree Grid for PRISM-3D.\n\nSupports:\n- Uniform and adaptively refined grids\n- Cell neighbor finding for gradient calculations\n- HEALPix-based ray casting directions\n- Refinement criteria based on chemical gradients\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Optional, List, Tuple\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass CellData:\n    \"\"\"\n    Physical state data for a single grid cell.\n    All quantities in CGS unless noted.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Gas properties\n    n_H: float = 1e3              # Total H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T_gas: float = 100.0          # Gas temperature [K]\n    T_dust: float = 20.0          # Dust temperature [K]\n    v_turb: float = 1e5           # Microturbulent velocity [cm/s] (1 km/s)\n\n    # Radiation field\n    G0: float = 1.0               # FUV field in Habing units\n    A_V: float = 0.0              # Visual extinction from illuminating source [mag]\n\n    # Cosmic ray ionization rate\n    zeta_CR: float = 2e-16        # CR ionization rate [s\u207b\u00b9]\n\n    # Chemical abundances (relative to n_H)\n    # These are the key species tracked at full resolution\n    x_e: float = 1e-4             # Electron fraction\n    x_HI: float = 1.0             # Atomic H\n    x_H2: float = 0.0             # Molecular H2\n    x_Cp: float = 0.0             # C+\n    x_C: float = 0.0              # C\n    x_CO: float = 0.0             # CO\n    x_O: float = 0.0              # O\n    x_OH: float = 0.0             # OH\n    x_H2O: float = 0.0            # H2O\n    x_CHx: float = 0.0            # CH, CH2 (lumped)\n    x_OHx: float = 0.0            # OH, H2O (lumped in reduced network)\n    x_Sp: float = 0.0             # S+\n    x_S: float = 0.0              # S\n    x_Si: float = 0.0             # Si\n    x_Sip: float = 0.0            # Si+\n    x_Fe: float = 0.0             # Fe\n    x_Fep: float = 0.0            # Fe+\n    x_HCOp: float = 0.0           # HCO+\n    x_Hp: float = 0.0             # H+\n    x_Hep: float = 0.0            # He+\n    x_Mp: float = 0.0             # Generic metal ion (for charge balance)\n\n    # Heating / cooling rates [erg/cm\u00b3/s] (diagnostic)\n    Gamma_total: float = 0.0      # Total heating rate\n    Lambda_total: float = 0.0     # Total cooling rate\n\n    # Grain properties\n    G_PE_efficiency: float = 0.05 # Photoelectric heating efficiency\n    R_H2_form: float = 3e-17     # H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n\n    # Column densities along primary ray [cm\u207b\u00b2] (for shielding)\n    N_H_total: float = 0.0\n    N_H2: float = 0.0\n    N_CO: float = 0.0\n\n    # Convergence tracking\n    converged: bool = False\n    delta_T: float = 1e10         # Last temperature change\n    delta_x: float = 1e10         # Max abundance change\n\n    def get_abundances_array(self):\n        \"\"\"Return abundances as numpy array for vectorized operations.\"\"\"\n        return np.array([\n            self.x_e, self.x_HI, self.x_H2, self.x_Cp, self.x_C,\n            self.x_CO, self.x_O, self.x_OH, self.x_H2O, self.x_CHx,\n            self.x_OHx, self.x_Sp, self.x_S, self.x_Si, self.x_Sip,\n            self.x_Fe, self.x_Fep, self.x_HCOp, self.x_Hp, self.x_Hep,\n            self.x_Mp\n        ])\n\n    def set_abundances_from_array(self, arr):\n        \"\"\"Set abundances from numpy array.\"\"\"\n        (self.x_e, self.x_HI, self.x_H2, self.x_Cp, self.x_C,\n         self.x_CO, self.x_O, self.x_OH, self.x_H2O, self.x_CHx,\n         self.x_OHx, self.x_Sp, self.x_S, self.x_Si, self.x_Sip,\n         self.x_Fe, self.x_Fep, self.x_HCOp, self.x_Hp, self.x_Hep,\n         self.x_Mp) = arr\n\n    def n_total(self):\n        \"\"\"Total particle number density [cm\u207b\u00b3].\"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n        He_per_H = solar_abundances['He']\n        return self.n_H * (self.x_HI + self.x_H2 + He_per_H + self.x_e)\n\n\n# Species index mapping\nSPECIES_NAMES = [\n    'e', 'HI', 'H2', 'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'O', 'OH', 'H2O', 'CHx',\n    'OHx', 'S+', 'S', 'Si', 'Si+', 'Fe', 'Fe+', 'HCO+', 'H+', 'He+', 'M+'\n]\nSPECIES_INDEX = {name: i for i, name in enumerate(SPECIES_NAMES)}\nN_SPECIES = len(SPECIES_NAMES)\n\n\nclass OctreeNode:\n    \"\"\"\n    Single node in the octree. Can be a leaf (contains data) or internal\n    (has 8 children).\n    \"\"\"\n    __slots__ = ['level', 'index', 'center', 'size', 'children', 'data',\n                 'parent', 'is_leaf']\n\n    def __init__(self, center, size, level=0, parent=None):\n        self.center = np.array(center, dtype=np.float64)  # [cm]\n        self.size = float(size)                             # cell edge length [cm]\n        self.level = level\n        self.parent = parent\n        self.children = None   # None = leaf, list of 8 = internal\n        self.data = CellData()\n        self.is_leaf = True\n\n    @property\n    def volume(self):\n        return self.size ** 3\n\n    def contains(self, point):\n        half = self.size / 2.0\n        return all(abs(point[i] - self.center[i]) <= half for i in range(3))\n\n    def refine(self):\n        \"\"\"Split this cell into 8 children.\"\"\"\n        if not self.is_leaf:\n            return\n        self.children = []\n        self.is_leaf = False\n        quarter = self.size / 4.0\n        child_size = self.size / 2.0\n        for i in range(2):\n            for j in range(2):\n                for k in range(2):\n                    offset = np.array([\n                        (2*i - 1) * quarter,\n                        (2*j - 1) * quarter,\n                        (2*k - 1) * quarter\n                    ])\n                    child = OctreeNode(\n                        self.center + offset,\n                        child_size,\n                        level=self.level + 1,\n                        parent=self\n                    )\n                    # Inherit parent data\n                    child.data = CellData()\n                    child.data.n_H = self.data.n_H\n                    child.data.T_gas = self.data.T_gas\n                    child.data.T_dust = self.data.T_dust\n                    child.data.G0 = self.data.G0\n                    child.data.A_V = self.data.A_V\n                    child.data.zeta_CR = self.data.zeta_CR\n                    child.data.set_abundances_from_array(\n                        self.data.get_abundances_array()\n                    )\n                    self.children.append(child)\n        # Clear parent data (no longer a leaf)\n        self.data = None\n\n\nclass OctreeGrid:\n    \"\"\"\n    3D AMR octree grid for PDR calculations.\n\n    The grid covers a cubic domain. Cells can be adaptively refined\n    based on physical criteria (chemical gradients, density jumps, etc.)\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    box_size : float\n        Physical size of the domain [cm]\n    n_base : int\n        Base resolution (number of cells per dimension at level 0).\n        Must be a power of 2.\n    center : array-like\n        Center of the domain [cm]. Default: origin.\n    max_level : int\n        Maximum refinement level. Each level doubles resolution.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, box_size, n_base=32, center=None, max_level=5):\n        self.box_size = float(box_size)\n        self.n_base = n_base\n        self.max_level = max_level\n\n        if center is None:\n            center = np.zeros(3)\n        self.center = np.array(center, dtype=np.float64)\n\n        self.base_cell_size = self.box_size / self.n_base\n\n        # Build base grid\n        self.root_nodes = []\n        half_box = self.box_size / 2.0\n        half_cell = self.base_cell_size / 2.0\n\n        for i in range(n_base):\n            for j in range(n_base):\n                for k in range(n_base):\n                    cell_center = self.center + np.array([\n                        -half_box + half_cell + i * self.base_cell_size,\n                        -half_box + half_cell + j * self.base_cell_size,\n                        -half_box + half_cell + k * self.base_cell_size,\n                    ])\n                    node = OctreeNode(cell_center, self.base_cell_size, level=0)\n                    self.root_nodes.append(node)\n\n        self._leaf_cache = None\n        self._leaf_cache_valid = False\n\n    @property\n    def n_cells(self):\n        \"\"\"Total number of leaf cells.\"\"\"\n        return len(self.get_leaves())\n\n    def get_leaves(self):\n        \"\"\"Get all leaf nodes (cells with data).\"\"\"\n        if self._leaf_cache_valid and self._leaf_cache is not None:\n            return self._leaf_cache\n\n        leaves = []\n        stack = list(self.root_nodes)\n        while stack:\n            node = stack.pop()\n            if node.is_leaf:\n                leaves.append(node)\n            else:\n                stack.extend(node.children)\n\n        self._leaf_cache = leaves\n        self._leaf_cache_valid = True\n        return leaves\n\n    def invalidate_cache(self):\n        self._leaf_cache_valid = False\n\n    def find_cell(self, point):\n        \"\"\"Find the leaf cell containing a given point.\"\"\"\n        point = np.asarray(point)\n        # Find which root node\n        half_box = self.box_size / 2.0\n        idx = np.floor(\n            (point - self.center + half_box) / self.base_cell_size\n        ).astype(int)\n        idx = np.clip(idx, 0, self.n_base - 1)\n        root_idx = idx[0] * self.n_base**2 + idx[1] * self.n_base + idx[2]\n\n        if root_idx < 0 or root_idx >= len(self.root_nodes):\n            return None\n\n        node = self.root_nodes[root_idx]\n        while not node.is_leaf:\n            # Determine which octant\n            octant = 0\n            if point[0] >= node.center[0]: octant += 4\n            if point[1] >= node.center[1]: octant += 2\n            if point[2] >= node.center[2]: octant += 1\n            node = node.children[octant]\n        return node\n\n    def refine_by_gradient(self, quantity='x_H2', threshold=0.1):\n        \"\"\"\n        Refine cells where the gradient of a quantity exceeds threshold.\n        Uses neighbor differences as a proxy for gradients.\n        \"\"\"\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n        to_refine = []\n\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            if leaf.level >= self.max_level:\n                continue\n            val = getattr(leaf.data, quantity)\n            # Check neighbors along each axis\n            for axis in range(3):\n                for direction in [-1, 1]:\n                    offset = np.zeros(3)\n                    offset[axis] = direction * leaf.size\n                    neighbor_pos = leaf.center + offset\n                    neighbor = self.find_cell(neighbor_pos)\n                    if neighbor is not None and neighbor.is_leaf:\n                        neighbor_val = getattr(neighbor.data, quantity)\n                        if abs(val - neighbor_val) > threshold:\n                            to_refine.append(leaf)\n                            break\n                else:\n                    continue\n                break\n\n        for node in to_refine:\n            node.refine()\n\n        if to_refine:\n            self.invalidate_cache()\n\n        return len(to_refine)\n\n    def get_cell_arrays(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Extract all cell data as structure-of-arrays for vectorized computation.\n        Returns dict of numpy arrays.\n        \"\"\"\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n        n = len(leaves)\n\n        arrays = {\n            'centers': np.zeros((n, 3)),\n            'sizes': np.zeros(n),\n            'n_H': np.zeros(n),\n            'T_gas': np.zeros(n),\n            'T_dust': np.zeros(n),\n            'G0': np.zeros(n),\n            'A_V': np.zeros(n),\n            'zeta_CR': np.zeros(n),\n            'abundances': np.zeros((n, N_SPECIES)),\n        }\n\n        for i, leaf in enumerate(leaves):\n            arrays['centers'][i] = leaf.center\n            arrays['sizes'][i] = leaf.size\n            arrays['n_H'][i] = leaf.data.n_H\n            arrays['T_gas'][i] = leaf.data.T_gas\n            arrays['T_dust'][i] = leaf.data.T_dust\n            arrays['G0'][i] = leaf.data.G0\n            arrays['A_V'][i] = leaf.data.A_V\n            arrays['zeta_CR'][i] = leaf.data.zeta_CR\n            arrays['abundances'][i] = leaf.data.get_abundances_array()\n\n        return arrays\n\n    def set_cell_arrays(self, arrays):\n        \"\"\"Write structure-of-arrays data back to cell objects.\"\"\"\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n        for i, leaf in enumerate(leaves):\n            leaf.data.n_H = arrays['n_H'][i]\n            leaf.data.T_gas = arrays['T_gas'][i]\n            leaf.data.T_dust = arrays['T_dust'][i]\n            leaf.data.G0 = arrays['G0'][i]\n            leaf.data.A_V = arrays['A_V'][i]\n            leaf.data.zeta_CR = arrays['zeta_CR'][i]\n            leaf.data.set_abundances_from_array(arrays['abundances'][i])\n\n    def setup_1d_slab(self, n_H, G0_surface, N_cells=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize as a 1D plane-parallel slab (for benchmarking).\n        Illuminated from the -x face.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float or callable\n            Gas density [cm\u207b\u00b3]. If callable, n_H(x) where x in [0, box_size].\n        G0_surface : float\n            FUV field at the illuminated surface in Habing units.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import AV_per_NH\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            # Distance from illuminated face\n            x = leaf.center[0] - (self.center[0] - self.box_size/2.0)\n\n            if callable(n_H):\n                leaf.data.n_H = n_H(x)\n            else:\n                leaf.data.n_H = float(n_H)\n\n            # Column density to illuminated face (simple integral)\n            N_H_col = leaf.data.n_H * x  # Approximate\n            leaf.data.A_V = N_H_col * AV_per_NH\n            leaf.data.G0 = G0_surface * np.exp(-1.8 * leaf.data.A_V)\n            leaf.data.N_H_total = N_H_col\n\n            # Initialize chemistry: surface = atomic, deep = molecular\n            if leaf.data.A_V < 0.5:\n                # Atomic surface\n                leaf.data.x_HI = 1.0\n                leaf.data.x_H2 = 0.0\n                leaf.data.x_Cp = 2.69e-4\n                leaf.data.x_C = 0.0\n                leaf.data.x_CO = 0.0\n                leaf.data.x_O = 4.90e-4\n                leaf.data.T_gas = 500.0\n            else:\n                # Molecular interior\n                fmol = min(1.0, leaf.data.A_V / 5.0)\n                leaf.data.x_HI = 1.0 - fmol\n                leaf.data.x_H2 = fmol / 2.0\n                leaf.data.x_Cp = 2.69e-4 * (1.0 - fmol)\n                leaf.data.x_C = 2.69e-4 * fmol * 0.3\n                leaf.data.x_CO = 2.69e-4 * fmol * 0.7\n                leaf.data.x_O = 4.90e-4 - leaf.data.x_CO\n                leaf.data.T_gas = 500.0 - 400.0 * fmol\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return (f\"OctreeGrid(box={self.box_size:.2e} cm, \"\n                f\"n_base={self.n_base}, leaves={self.n_cells})\")\n",

      "code": "\"\"\"\n3D Adaptive Mesh Refinement (AMR) Octree Grid for PRISM-3D.\n\nSupports:\n- Uniform and adaptively refined grids\n- Cell neighbor finding for gradient calculations\n- HEALPix-based ray casting directions\n- Refinement criteria based on chemical gradients\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Optional, List, Tuple\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass CellData:\n    \"\"\"\n    Physical state data for a single grid cell.\n    All quantities in CGS unless noted.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Gas properties\n    n_H: float = 1e3              # Total H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T_gas: float = 100.0          # Gas temperature [K]\n    T_dust: float = 20.0          # Dust temperature [K]\n    v_turb: float = 1e5           # Microturbulent velocity [cm/s] (1 km/s)\n\n    # Radiation field\n    G0: float = 1.0               # FUV field in Habing units\n    A_V: float = 0.0              # Visual extinction from illuminating source [mag]\n\n    # Cosmic ray ionization rate\n    zeta_CR: float = 2e-16        # CR ionization rate [s\u207b\u00b9]\n\n    # Chemical abundances (relative to n_H)\n    # These are the key species tracked at full resolution\n    x_e: float = 1e-4             # Electron fraction\n    x_HI: float = 1.0             # Atomic H\n    x_H2: float = 0.0             # Molecular H2\n    x_Cp: float = 0.0             # C+\n    x_C: float = 0.0              # C\n    x_CO: float = 0.0             # CO\n    x_O: float = 0.0              # O\n    x_OH: float = 0.0             # OH\n    x_H2O: float = 0.0            # H2O\n    x_CHx: float = 0.0            # CH, CH2 (lumped)\n    x_OHx: float = 0.0            # OH, H2O (lumped in reduced network)\n    x_Sp: float = 0.0             # S+\n    x_S: float = 0.0              # S\n    x_Si: float = 0.0             # Si\n    x_Sip: float = 0.0            # Si+\n    x_Fe: float = 0.0             # Fe\n    x_Fep: float = 0.0            # Fe+\n    x_HCOp: float = 0.0           # HCO+\n    x_Hp: float = 0.0             # H+\n    x_Hep: float = 0.0            # He+\n    x_Mp: float = 0.0             # Generic metal ion (for charge balance)\n\n    # Heating / cooling rates [erg/cm\u00b3/s] (diagnostic)\n    Gamma_total: float = 0.0      # Total heating rate\n    Lambda_total: float = 0.0     # Total cooling rate\n\n    # Grain properties\n    G_PE_efficiency: float = 0.05 # Photoelectric heating efficiency\n    R_H2_form: float = 3e-17     # H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n\n    # Column densities along primary ray [cm\u207b\u00b2] (for shielding)\n    N_H_total: float = 0.0\n    N_H2: float = 0.0\n    N_CO: float = 0.0\n\n    # Convergence tracking\n    converged: bool = False\n    delta_T: float = 1e10         # Last temperature change\n    delta_x: float = 1e10         # Max abundance change\n\n    def get_abundances_array(self):\n        \"\"\"Return abundances as numpy array for vectorized operations.\"\"\"\n        return np.array([\n            self.x_e, self.x_HI, self.x_H2, self.x_Cp, self.x_C,\n            self.x_CO, self.x_O, self.x_OH, self.x_H2O, self.x_CHx,\n            self.x_OHx, self.x_Sp, self.x_S, self.x_Si, self.x_Sip,\n            self.x_Fe, self.x_Fep, self.x_HCOp, self.x_Hp, self.x_Hep,\n            self.x_Mp\n        ])\n\n    def set_abundances_from_array(self, arr):\n        \"\"\"Set abundances from numpy array.\"\"\"\n        (self.x_e, self.x_HI, self.x_H2, self.x_Cp, self.x_C,\n         self.x_CO, self.x_O, self.x_OH, self.x_H2O, self.x_CHx,\n         self.x_OHx, self.x_Sp, self.x_S, self.x_Si, self.x_Sip,\n         self.x_Fe, self.x_Fep, self.x_HCOp, self.x_Hp, self.x_Hep,\n         self.x_Mp) = arr\n\n    def n_total(self):\n        \"\"\"Total particle number density [cm\u207b\u00b3].\"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n        He_per_H = solar_abundances['He']\n        return self.n_H * (self.x_HI + self.x_H2 + He_per_H + self.x_e)\n\n\n# Species index mapping\nSPECIES_NAMES = [\n    'e', 'HI', 'H2', 'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'O', 'OH', 'H2O', 'CHx',\n    'OHx', 'S+', 'S', 'Si', 'Si+', 'Fe', 'Fe+', 'HCO+', 'H+', 'He+', 'M+'\n]\nSPECIES_INDEX = {name: i for i, name in enumerate(SPECIES_NAMES)}\nN_SPECIES = len(SPECIES_NAMES)\n\n\nclass OctreeNode:\n    \"\"\"\n    Single node in the octree. Can be a leaf (contains data) or internal\n    (has 8 children).\n    \"\"\"\n    __slots__ = ['level', 'index', 'center', 'size', 'children', 'data',\n                 'parent', 'is_leaf']\n\n    def __init__(self, center, size, level=0, parent=None):\n        self.center = np.array(center, dtype=np.float64)  # [cm]\n        self.size = float(size)                             # cell edge length [cm]\n        self.level = level\n        self.parent = parent\n        self.children = None   # None = leaf, list of 8 = internal\n        self.data = CellData()\n        self.is_leaf = True\n\n    @property\n    def volume(self):\n        return self.size ** 3\n\n    def contains(self, point):\n        half = self.size / 2.0\n        return all(abs(point[i] - self.center[i]) <= half for i in range(3))\n\n    def refine(self):\n        \"\"\"Split this cell into 8 children.\"\"\"\n        if not self.is_leaf:\n            return\n        self.children = []\n        self.is_leaf = False\n        quarter = self.size / 4.0\n        child_size = self.size / 2.0\n        for i in range(2):\n            for j in range(2):\n                for k in range(2):\n                    offset = np.array([\n                        (2*i - 1) * quarter,\n                        (2*j - 1) * quarter,\n                        (2*k - 1) * quarter\n                    ])\n                    child = OctreeNode(\n                        self.center + offset,\n                        child_size,\n                        level=self.level + 1,\n                        parent=self\n                    )\n                    # Inherit parent data\n                    child.data = CellData()\n                    child.data.n_H = self.data.n_H\n                    child.data.T_gas = self.data.T_gas\n                    child.data.T_dust = self.data.T_dust\n                    child.data.G0 = self.data.G0\n                    child.data.A_V = self.data.A_V\n                    child.data.zeta_CR = self.data.zeta_CR\n                    child.data.set_abundances_from_array(\n                        self.data.get_abundances_array()\n                    )\n                    self.children.append(child)\n        # Clear parent data (no longer a leaf)\n        self.data = None\n\n\nclass OctreeGrid:\n    \"\"\"\n    3D AMR octree grid for PDR calculations.\n\n    The grid covers a cubic domain. Cells can be adaptively refined\n    based on physical criteria (chemical gradients, density jumps, etc.)\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    box_size : float\n        Physical size of the domain [cm]\n    n_base : int\n        Base resolution (number of cells per dimension at level 0).\n        Must be a power of 2.\n    center : array-like\n        Center of the domain [cm]. Default: origin.\n    max_level : int\n        Maximum refinement level. Each level doubles resolution.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, box_size, n_base=32, center=None, max_level=5):\n        self.box_size = float(box_size)\n        self.n_base = n_base\n        self.max_level = max_level\n\n        if center is None:\n            center = np.zeros(3)\n        self.center = np.array(center, dtype=np.float64)\n\n        self.base_cell_size = self.box_size / self.n_base\n\n        # Build base grid\n        self.root_nodes = []\n        half_box = self.box_size / 2.0\n        half_cell = self.base_cell_size / 2.0\n\n        for i in range(n_base):\n            for j in range(n_base):\n                for k in range(n_base):\n                    cell_center = self.center + np.array([\n                        -half_box + half_cell + i * self.base_cell_size,\n                        -half_box + half_cell + j * self.base_cell_size,\n                        -half_box + half_cell + k * self.base_cell_size,\n                    ])\n                    node = OctreeNode(cell_center, self.base_cell_size, level=0)\n                    self.root_nodes.append(node)\n\n        self._leaf_cache = None\n        self._leaf_cache_valid = False\n\n    @property\n    def n_cells(self):\n        \"\"\"Total number of leaf cells.\"\"\"\n        return len(self.get_leaves())\n\n    def get_leaves(self):\n        \"\"\"Get all leaf nodes (cells with data).\"\"\"\n        if self._leaf_cache_valid and self._leaf_cache is not None:\n            return self._leaf_cache\n\n        leaves = []\n        stack = list(self.root_nodes)\n        while stack:\n            node = stack.pop()\n            if node.is_leaf:\n                leaves.append(node)\n            else:\n                stack.extend(node.children)\n\n        self._leaf_cache = leaves\n        self._leaf_cache_valid = True\n        return leaves\n\n    def invalidate_cache(self):\n        self._leaf_cache_valid = False\n\n    def find_cell(self, point):\n        \"\"\"Find the leaf cell containing a given point.\"\"\"\n        point = np.asarray(point)\n        # Find which root node\n        half_box = self.box_size / 2.0\n        idx = np.floor(\n            (point - self.center + half_box) / self.base_cell_size\n        ).astype(int)\n        idx = np.clip(idx, 0, self.n_base - 1)\n        root_idx = idx[0] * self.n_base**2 + idx[1] * self.n_base + idx[2]\n\n        if root_idx < 0 or root_idx >= len(self.root_nodes):\n            return None\n\n        node = self.root_nodes[root_idx]\n        while not node.is_leaf:\n            # Determine which octant\n            octant = 0\n            if point[0] >= node.center[0]: octant += 4\n            if point[1] >= node.center[1]: octant += 2\n            if point[2] >= node.center[2]: octant += 1\n            node = node.children[octant]\n        return node\n\n    def refine_by_gradient(self, quantity='x_H2', threshold=0.1):\n        \"\"\"\n        Refine cells where the gradient of a quantity exceeds threshold.\n        Uses neighbor differences as a proxy for gradients.\n        \"\"\"\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n        to_refine = []\n\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            if leaf.level >= self.max_level:\n                continue\n            val = getattr(leaf.data, quantity)\n            # Check neighbors along each axis\n            for axis in range(3):\n                for direction in [-1, 1]:\n                    offset = np.zeros(3)\n                    offset[axis] = direction * leaf.size\n                    neighbor_pos = leaf.center + offset\n                    neighbor = self.find_cell(neighbor_pos)\n                    if neighbor is not None and neighbor.is_leaf:\n                        neighbor_val = getattr(neighbor.data, quantity)\n                        if abs(val - neighbor_val) > threshold:\n                            to_refine.append(leaf)\n                            break\n                else:\n                    continue\n                break\n\n        for node in to_refine:\n            node.refine()\n\n        if to_refine:\n            self.invalidate_cache()\n\n        return len(to_refine)\n\n    def get_cell_arrays(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Extract all cell data as structure-of-arrays for vectorized computation.\n        Returns dict of numpy arrays.\n        \"\"\"\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n        n = len(leaves)\n\n        arrays = {\n            'centers': np.zeros((n, 3)),\n            'sizes': np.zeros(n),\n            'n_H': np.zeros(n),\n            'T_gas': np.zeros(n),\n            'T_dust': np.zeros(n),\n            'G0': np.zeros(n),\n            'A_V': np.zeros(n),\n            'zeta_CR': np.zeros(n),\n            'abundances': np.zeros((n, N_SPECIES)),\n        }\n\n        for i, leaf in enumerate(leaves):\n            arrays['centers'][i] = leaf.center\n            arrays['sizes'][i] = leaf.size\n            arrays['n_H'][i] = leaf.data.n_H\n            arrays['T_gas'][i] = leaf.data.T_gas\n            arrays['T_dust'][i] = leaf.data.T_dust\n            arrays['G0'][i] = leaf.data.G0\n            arrays['A_V'][i] = leaf.data.A_V\n            arrays['zeta_CR'][i] = leaf.data.zeta_CR\n            arrays['abundances'][i] = leaf.data.get_abundances_array()\n\n        return arrays\n\n    def set_cell_arrays(self, arrays):\n        \"\"\"Write structure-of-arrays data back to cell objects.\"\"\"\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n        for i, leaf in enumerate(leaves):\n            leaf.data.n_H = arrays['n_H'][i]\n            leaf.data.T_gas = arrays['T_gas'][i]\n            leaf.data.T_dust = arrays['T_dust'][i]\n            leaf.data.G0 = arrays['G0'][i]\n            leaf.data.A_V = arrays['A_V'][i]\n            leaf.data.zeta_CR = arrays['zeta_CR'][i]\n            leaf.data.set_abundances_from_array(arrays['abundances'][i])\n\n    def setup_1d_slab(self, n_H, G0_surface, N_cells=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize as a 1D plane-parallel slab (for benchmarking).\n        Illuminated from the -x face.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float or callable\n            Gas density [cm\u207b\u00b3]. If callable, n_H(x) where x in [0, box_size].\n        G0_surface : float\n            FUV field at the illuminated surface in Habing units.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import AV_per_NH\n        leaves = self.get_leaves()\n\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            # Distance from illuminated face\n            x = leaf.center[0] - (self.center[0] - self.box_size/2.0)\n\n            if callable(n_H):\n                leaf.data.n_H = n_H(x)\n            else:\n                leaf.data.n_H = float(n_H)\n\n            # Column density to illuminated face (simple integral)\n            N_H_col = leaf.data.n_H * x  # Approximate\n            leaf.data.A_V = N_H_col * AV_per_NH\n            leaf.data.G0 = G0_surface * np.exp(-1.8 * leaf.data.A_V)\n            leaf.data.N_H_total = N_H_col\n\n            # Initialize chemistry: surface = atomic, deep = molecular\n            if leaf.data.A_V < 0.5:\n                # Atomic surface\n                leaf.data.x_HI = 1.0\n                leaf.data.x_H2 = 0.0\n                leaf.data.x_Cp = 2.69e-4\n                leaf.data.x_C = 0.0\n                leaf.data.x_CO = 0.0\n                leaf.data.x_O = 4.90e-4\n                leaf.data.T_gas = 500.0\n            else:\n                # Molecular interior\n                fmol = min(1.0, leaf.data.A_V / 5.0)\n                leaf.data.x_HI = 1.0 - fmol\n                leaf.data.x_H2 = fmol / 2.0\n                leaf.data.x_Cp = 2.69e-4 * (1.0 - fmol)\n                leaf.data.x_C = 2.69e-4 * fmol * 0.3\n                leaf.data.x_CO = 2.69e-4 * fmol * 0.7\n                leaf.data.x_O = 4.90e-4 - leaf.data.x_CO\n                leaf.data.T_gas = 500.0 - 400.0 * fmol\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return (f\"OctreeGrid(box={self.box_size:.2e} cm, \"\n                f\"n_base={self.n_base}, leaves={self.n_cells})\")\n",

PDR-specific additions.\n\nThe network covers:\n- H/H2 chemistry (formation, destruction, self-shielding)\n- C+/C/CO chemistry (main carbon network)\n- O/OH/H2O oxygen chemistry\n- S, Si, Fe ionization balance\n- Key molecular ions: HCO+, CH+, OH+, H3+\n- Charge balance with PAH/grain charging\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import k_boltz, eV_to_K\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Species definitions\n# ============================================================\n\nclass Species:\n    \"\"\"A chemical species with its properties.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, name, mass_amu, charge=0, n_atoms=1, is_grain=False):\n        self.name = name\n        self.mass_amu = mass_amu\n        self.charge = charge\n        self.n_atoms = n_atoms\n        self.is_grain = is_grain\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"Species({self.name})\"\n\n\n# Define all species in the network\nSPECIES_CATALOG = {\n    # Atoms and ions\n    'H':     Species('H',     1.008,  0, 1),\n    'H+':    Species('H+',    1.008,  1, 1),\n    'H-':    Species('H-',    1.008, -1, 1),\n    'He':    Species('He',    4.003,  0, 1),\n    'He+':   Species('He+',   4.003,  1, 1),\n    'C':     Species('C',    12.011,  0, 1),\n    'C+':    Species('C+',   12.011,  1, 1),\n    'O':     Species('O',    15.999,  0, 1),\n    'O+': Species('CO',   28.010,  0, 2),\n    'OH':    Species('OH',   17.007,  0, 2),\n    'OH+':   Species('OH+', 17.007,  1, 2),\n    'H2O':   Species('H2O', 18.015,  0, 3),\n    'H2O+':  Species('H2O+',18.015,  1, 3),\n    'H3O+':  Species('H3O+',19.023,  1, 4),\n    'CH':    Species('CH',   13.019,  0, 2),\n    'CH+':   Species('CH+', 13.019,  1, 2),\n    'CH2':   Species('CH2', 14.027,  0, 3),\n    'CH2+':  Species('CH2+',14.027,  1, 3),\n    'CO+':   Species('CO+', 28.010,  1, 2),\n    'HCO+':  Species('HCO+',29.018,  1, 3),\n    'HOC+':  Species('HOC+',29.018,  1, 3),\n 1, is_grain=True),\n    'PAH+':  Species('PAH+', 100.0,  1, 1, is_grain=True),\n    'PAH-':  Species('PAH-', 100.0, -1, 1, is_grain=True),\n}\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Reaction types\n# ============================================================\n\nclass ReactionType:\n    \"\"\"Enumeration of reaction types.\"\"\"\n    TWO_BODY = 'two_body'               # A + B \u2192 C + D\n    PHOTODISSOCIATION = 'photodiss'     # A + h\u03bd \u2192 B + C\n    PHOTOIONIZATION = 'photoion'         # A + h\u03bd \u2192 A+ + e-\n    COSMIC_RAY = 'cosmic_ray'           # A + CR \u2192 products\n    CR_INDUCED_PHOTO = 'cr_photo'       # A + CR_photon \u2192 products\n    GRAIN_SURFACE = 'grain_surface'     # A + B + grain \u2192 C + grain\n    RECOMBINATION = 'recomb'            # A+ + e- \u2192 A + h\u03bd (radiative)\n    GRAIN_RECOMB = 'grain_recomb'       # A+ + e- + grain \u2192 A + grain\n    CHARGE_EXCHANGE = 'charge_exchange' # A+ + B \u2192 A + B+\n    THREE_BODY = 'three_body'           # A + B + M \u2192 C + M\n\n\nclass Reaction:\n    \"\"\"\n    A single chemical reaction with its rate coefficient.\n\n    Rate coefficient is parameterized as:\n        k(T) = alpha * (T/300)^beta * exp(-gamma/T)\n    for two-body reactions, or:\n        k = alpha * G0 * exp(-gamma * A_V)\n    for photoreactions, or:\n        k = alpha * zeta_CR\n    for cosmic ray reactions.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, reactants, products, alpha, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n                 reaction_type=ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n                 T_range=(10, 41000), name=None):\n        self.reactants = list(reactants)\n        self.products = list(products)\n        self.alpha = alpha\n        self.beta = beta\n        self.gamma = gamma\n        self.reaction_type = reaction_type\n        self.T_range = T_range\n        self.name = name or self._auto_name()\n\n    def _auto_name(self):\n        r = ' + '.join(self.reactants)\n        p = ' + '.join(self.products)\n        return f\"{r} \u2192 {p}\"\n\n    def rate(self, T=None, G0=None, A_V=None, zeta_CR=None, n_H=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute rate coefficient.\n\n        For two-body: returns k [cm\u00b3/s]\n        For photo: returns k [s\u207b\u00b9] (already includes G0 and shielding)\n        For CR: returns k [s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.reaction_type in (ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n                                   ReactionType.RECOMBINATION,\n                                   ReactionType.CHARGE_EXCHANGE):\n            T_clamped = np.clip(T, self.T_range[0], self.T_range[1])\n            return self.alpha * (T_clamped / 300.0)**self.beta * np.exp(-self.gamma / T_clamped)\n\n        elif self.reaction_type in (ReactionType.PHOTODISSOCIATION,\n                                     ReactionType.PHOTOIONIZATION):\n            return self.alpha * G0 * np.exp(-self.gamma * A_V)\n\n        elif self.reaction_type == ReactionType.COSMIC_RAY:\n            return self.alpha * zeta_CR\n\n        elif self.reaction_type == ReactionType.CR_INDUCED_PHOTO:\n            # CR-induced UV photons, rate ~ alpha * zeta_CR / (1 - omega)\n            return self.alpha * zeta_CR\n\n        elif self.reaction_type == ReactionType.THREE_BODY:\n            T_clamped = np.clip(T, self.T_range[0], self.T_range[1])\n            return self.alpha * (T_clamped / 300.0)**self.beta * np.exp(-self.gamma / T_clamped) * n_H\n\n        elif self.reaction_type == ReactionType.GRAIN_SURFACE:\n            # H2 formation on grains is handled separately\n            return self.alpha * (T / 300.0)**self.beta\n\n        elif self.reaction_type == ReactionType.GRAIN_RECOMB:\n            T_clamped = np.clip(T, self.T_range[0], self.T_range[1])\n            return self.alpha * (T_clamped / 300.0)**self.beta\n\n        return 0.0\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"Reaction({self.name}, \u03b1={self.alpha:.2e})\"\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Build the PDR chemical network\n# ============================================================\n\ndef build_pdr_network():\n    \"\"\"\n    Construct the standard PRISM-3D chemical network.\n\n    Returns a list of Reaction objects. Rate coefficients are from\n    UMIST 2012 / KIDA 2014 where available, supplemented with\n    Glover+ (2010) for H2 chemistry and Visser+ (2009) for CO.\n\n    This is a reduced but complete PDR network covering the key\n    species and transitions needed for thermal and chemical balance.\n    \"\"\"\n    reactions = []\n    R = Reaction  # shorthand\n\n    # =============================================\n    # H2 formation and destruction\n    # =============================================\n\n    # H + H + grain \u2192 H2 + grain (Jura 1975, Cazaux & Tielens 2004)\n    # Rate ~ 3e-17 * sqrt(T/100) * n_H * x_HI * n_H  [per H nucleus]\n    # The actual rate is R_H2 * n(H) * n_H where R_H2 ~ 3e-17 cm\u00b3/s\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H', 'H'], ['H2'],\n        alpha=3.0e-17, beta=0.5, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.GRAIN_SURFACE,\n        name='H2_formation_grain'\n    ))\n\n    # H2 + h\u03bd \u2192 H + H (photodissociation, Solomon process)\n    # Base rate 3.3e-11 s\u207b\u00b9 in unshielded Draine field\n    # Self-shielding handled separately\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H2'], ['H', 'H'],\n        alpha=3.3e-11, beta=0.0, gamma=3.74,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.PHOTODISSOCIATION,\n        name='H2_photodiss'\n    ))\n\n    # H2 + CR \u2192 H + H (+ e)\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H2'], ['H', 'H+', 'e-'],\n        alpha=1.0, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.COSMIC_RAY,\n        name='H2_CR_ionization'\n    ))\n\n    # H2 + CR \u2192 H2+ + e-\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H2'], ['H2+', 'e-'],\n        alpha=2.0, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.COSMIC_RAY,\n        name='H2_CR_ionization2'\n    ))\n\n    # =============================================\n    # H ionization / recombination\n    # =============================================\n\n    # H + CR \u2192 H+ + e-\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H'], ['H+', 'e-'],\n        alpha=0.46, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.COSMIC_RAY,\n        name='H_CR_ionization'\n    ))\n\n    # H+ + e- \u2192 H + h\u03bd (radiative recombination, case B)\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H+', 'e-'], ['H'],\n        alpha=2.753e-14, beta=-0.745, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.RECOMBINATION,\n        name='H_recombination'\n    ))\n\n    # H + e- \u2192 H- + h\u03bd\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H', 'e-'], ['H-'],\n        alpha=1.4e-18, beta=0.928, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n        name='H_minus_formation'\n    ))\n\n    # H- + H \u2192 H2 + e- (gas-phase H2 formation)\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H-', 'H'], ['H2', 'e-'],\n        alpha=4.0e-9, beta=-0.02, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n        name='H2_gasphase_Hminus'\n    ))\n\n    # =============================================\n    # CO+ reactions\n    # =============================================\n\n    # CO+ + H \u2192 CO + H+\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['CO+', 'H'], ['CO', 'H+'],\n        alpha=7.5e-10, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n        name='COp_H'\n    ))\n\n    # CO+ + H2 \u2192 HCO+ + H\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['CO+', 'H2'], ['HCO+', 'H'],\n        alpha=7.5e-10, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n        name='COp_H2_HCOp'\n    ))\n\n    # =============================================\n    # He chemistry\n    # =============================================\n\n    # He + CR \u2192 He+ + e-\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['He'], ['He+', 'e-'],\n        alpha=0.5, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.COSMIC_RAY,\n        name='He_CR_ion'\n    ))\n\n    # He+ + CO \u2192 C+ + O + He\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['He+', 'CO'], ['C+', 'O', 'He'],\n        alpha=1.6e-9, beta=0.0, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n        name='Hep_CO_destruct'\n    ))\n\n    # He+ + e- \u2192 He + h\u03bd\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['He+', 'e-'], ['He'],\n        alpha=1.0e-12, beta=-0.64, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.RECOMBINATION,\n        name='Hep_recomb'\n    ))\n\n    # =============================================\n    # PAH / grain-assisted recombination\n    # =============================================\n\n    # C+ + PAH- \u2192 C + PAH (grain-assisted C+ recombination)\n    # Rate coefficient ~ 4.4e-12 * sqrt(T/100) cm\u00b3/s (Wolfire+ 2003)\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['C+', 'PAH-'], ['C', 'PAH'],\n        alpha=4.4e-12, beta=0.5, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.GRAIN_RECOMB,\n        name='Cp_PAH_recomb'\n    ))\n\n    # S+ + PAH- \u2192 S + PAH\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['S+', 'PAH-'], ['S', 'PAH'],\n        alpha=4.4e-12, beta=0.5, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.GRAIN_RECOMB,\n        name='Sp_PAH_recomb'\n    ))\n\n    # H+ + PAH- \u2192 H + PAH\n    reactions.append(R(\n        ['H+', 'PAH-'], ['H', 'PAH'],\n        alpha=4.4e-12, beta=0.5, gamma=0.0,\n        reaction_type=ReactionType.GRAIN_RECOMB,\n        name='Hp_PAH_recomb'\n    ))\n\n    return reactions\n\n\nclass ChemicalNetwork:\n    \"\"\"\n    Container for the chemical network.\n    Provides methods for computing formation/destruction rates.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, reactions=None):\n        if reactions is None:\n            reactions = build_pdr_network()\n        self.reactions = reactions\n\n        # Build species list from reactions\n        species_set = set()\n        for rxn in reactions:\n            species_set.update(rxn.reactants)\n            species_set.update(rxn.products)\n        self.species_names = sorted(species_set)\n        self.species_index = {s: i for i, s in enumerate(self.species_names)}\n        self.n_species = len(self.species_names)\n\n    def compute_rates(self, abundances, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                      f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute formation and destruction rates for all species.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        abundances : dict\n            Species name \u2192 abundance relative to n_H\n        n_H : float\n            H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        A_V : float\n            Visual extinction [mag]\n        zeta_CR : float\n            Cosmic ray ionization rate [s\u207b\u00b9]\n        f_shield_H2 : float\n            H2 self-shielding factor (0 to 1)\n        f_shield_CO : float\n            CO self-shielding factor (0 to 1)\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        dndt : dict\n            Species name \u2192 time derivative of abundance [s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        dndt = {s: 0.0 for s in self.species_names}\n\n        for rxn in self.reactions:\n            # Get rate coefficient\n            k = rxn.rate(T=T, G0=G0, A_V=A_V, zeta_CR=zeta_CR, n_H=n_H)\n\n            # Apply self-shielding\n            if rxn.name == 'H2_photodiss':\n                k *= f_shield_H2\n            elif rxn.name == 'CO_photodiss':\n                k *= f_shield_CO\n\n            # Compute reaction rate [cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9 / n_H]\n            if rxn.reaction_type in (ReactionType.TWO_BODY,\n                                      ReactionType.RECOMBINATION,\n                                      ReactionType.CHARGE_EXCHANGE,\n                                      ReactionType.GRAIN_RECOMB):\n                # Rate = k * n(A) * n(B) = k * x_A * x_B * n_H\u00b2\n                # but we want dx/dt, so divide by n_H\n                x_A = abundances.get(rxn.reactants[0], 0.0)\n                x_B = abundances.get(rxn.reactants[1], 0.0)\n                rate = k * x_A * x_B * n_H\n\n            elif rxn.reaction_type in (ReactionType.PHOTODISSOCIATION,\n                                        ReactionType.PHOTOIONIZATION,\n                                        ReactionType.COSMIC_RAY,\n                                        ReactionType.CR_INDUCED_PHOTO):\n                # Unimolecular: rate = k * n(A) \u2192 dx/dt = k * x_A\n                x_A = abundances.get(rxn.reactants[0], 0.0)\n                rate = k * x_A\n\n            elif rxn.reaction_type == ReactionType.GRAIN_SURFACE:\n                # H2 formation: special case\n                x_H = abundances.get('H', 0.0)\n                rate = k * x_H * n_H * 0.5  # Factor 0.5: 2 H atoms per H2\n\n            elif rxn.reaction_type == ReactionType.THREE_BODY:\n                x_A = abundances.get(rxn.reactants[0], 0.0)\n                x_B = abundances.get(rxn.reactants[1], 0.0)\n                rate = k * x_A * x_B * n_H\n\n            else:\n                rate = 0.0\n\n            # Update formation/destruction\n            for r in rxn.reactants:\n                if r in dndt:\n                    dndt[r] -= rate\n            for p in rxn.products:\n                if p in dndt:\n                    dndt[p] += rate\n\n        return dndt\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return (f\"ChemicalNetwork({len(self.reactions)} reactions, \"\n                f\"{self.n_species} species)\")\n",
      "path": "/home/claude/prism3d/chemistry/network.py"
