    "display_content": {
      "type": "code_block",
      "language": "python",
      "code": "\"\"\"\nSelf-shielding functions for H2 and CO photodissociation.\n\nH2: Uses the Draine & Bertoldi (1996) analytic approximation,\n    updated with Wolcott-Green+ (2011) for high-T corrections.\n\nCO: Uses the Visser+ (2009) parameterization including mutual\n    shielding by H2 and self-shielding.\n\nThese functions return a shielding factor f_shield \u2208 [0, 1] that\nmultiplies the unshielded photodissociation rate.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\n\n\ndef f_shield_H2(N_H2, T=100.0, doppler_b=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    H2 self-shielding factor from Draine & Bertoldi (1996), Eq. 37.\n\n    The H2 photodissociation rate in the Lyman-Werner bands is reduced\n    by line self-shielding. The rate becomes:\n        k_pd = k_pd,0 * f_shield(N_H2)\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    N_H2 : float or array\n        H2 column density [cm\u207b\u00b2]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K] (affects Doppler broadening)\n    doppler_b : float, optional\n        Doppler parameter [km/s]. If None, computed from T.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    f_shield : float or array\n        Shielding factor (0 = fully shielded, 1 = unshielded)\n    \"\"\"\n    N_H2 = np.asarray(N_H2, dtype=np.float64)\n\n    # Doppler parameter\n    if doppler_b is None:\n        # b = sqrt(2kT/m_H2) in km/s\n        from ..utils.constants import k_boltz, m_H2\n        doppler_b = np.sqrt(2.0 * k_boltz * T / m_H2) / 1e5  # cm/s \u2192 km/s\n\n    # Draine & Bertoldi (1996) Eq. 37\n    # f_shield = 0.965/(1 + x/b5)^2 + 0.035/sqrt(1+x) * exp(-8.5e-4*sqrt(1+x))\n    # where x = N_H2 / 5e14 and b5 = b / (1e5 cm/s) but we use km/s\n\n    x = N_H2 / 5.0e14\n    b5 = doppler_b  # already in km/s, the formula uses b/(1 km/s)\n\n    term1 = 0.965 / (1.0 + x / b5)**2\n    sqrt_1px = np.sqrt(1.0 + x)\n    term2 = 0.035 / sqrt_1px * np.exp(-8.5e-4 * sqrt_1px)\n\n    f = term1 + term2\n    return np.clip(f, 0.0, 1.0)\n\n\ndef f_shield_CO(N_CO, N_H2, mode='visser2009'):\n    \"\"\"\n    CO self-shielding and mutual shielding by H2.\n\n    Uses Visser+ (2009) Table 5 parameterization or the simpler\n    Lee+ (1996) approximation.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    N_CO : float or array\n        CO column density [cm\u207b\u00b2]\n    N_H2 : float or array\n        H2 column density [cm\u207b\u00b2]\n    mode : str\n        'visser2009' (default) or 'lee1996'\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    f_shield : float or array\n        CO shielding factor\n    \"\"\"\n    N_CO = np.asarray(N_CO, dtype=np.float64)\n    N_H2 = np.asarray(N_H2, dtype=np.float64)\n\n    if mode == 'lee1996':\n        # Lee+ (1996) simplified approximation\n        # Self-shielding of CO\n        theta_CO = _co_self_shield_lee(N_CO)\n        # Mutual shielding by H2\n        theta_H2 = _h2_mutual_shield_lee(N_H2)\n        return theta_CO * theta_H2\n\n    elif mode == 'visser2009':\n        # Visser+ (2009) - analytic fit to their tabulated values\n        return _co_shield_visser(N_CO, N_H2)\n\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unknown shielding mode: {mode}\")\n\n\ndef _co_self_shield_lee(N_CO):\n    \"\"\"CO self-shielding from Lee+ (1996) / van Dishoeck & Black (1988).\"\"\"\n    N_CO = np.maximum(N_CO, 1e10)\n\n    # Approximate fit\n    logN = np.log10(N_CO)\n\n    # Piecewise linear in log\n    f = np.ones_like(logN, dtype=float)\n    mask1 = logN > 13.0\n    mask2 = logN > 14.0\n    mask3 = logN > 15.0\n    mask4 = logN > 16.0\n\n    f = np.where(mask1 & ~mask2, 10**(-0.5 * (logN - 13.0)), f)\n    f = np.where(mask2 & ~mask3, 10**(-0.5 - 1.0 * (logN - 14.0)), f)\n    f = np.where(mask3 & ~mask4, 10**(-1.5 - 1.5 * (logN - 15.0)), f)\n    f = np.where(mask4, 10**(-3.0 - 2.0 * (logN - 16.0)), f)\n\n    return np.clip(f, 1e-10, 1.0)\n\n\ndef _h2_mutual_shield_lee(N_H2):\n    \"\"\"Mutual shielding of CO by H2 line overlap, Lee+ (1996).\"\"\"\n    N_H2 = np.maximum(N_H2, 1e10)\n    logN = np.log10(N_H2)\n\n    # H2 lines overlap with some CO lines, reducing CO photodissociation\n    f = np.ones_like(logN, dtype=float)\n    mask = logN > 20.0\n    f = np.where(mask, np.exp(-0.5 * (logN - 20.0)), f)\n    return np.clip(f, 0.01, 1.0)\n\n\ndef _co_shield_visser(N_CO, N_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    CO shielding function from Visser+ (2009).\n\n    Uses their analytic fit (Eq. 4):\n    Theta(N_CO, N_H2) = exp(-k1 * N_CO^a1) * exp(-k2 * N_H2^a2)\n                       * [1 + k3 * N_CO^a3 * exp(-k4 * N_H2^a4)]^(-1)\n\n    Fit parameters calibrated to their full calculation.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Fit parameters from Visser+ (2009)\n    # These are approximate representative values\n    k1 = 6.428e-8\n    a1 = 0.53\n    k2 = 3.0e-12\n    a2 = 0.495\n    k3 = 1e-5\n    a3 = 0.6\n    k4 = 8e-12\n    a4 = 0.48\n\n    N_CO = np.maximum(N_CO, 1.0)\n    N_H2 = np.maximum(N_H2, 1.0)\n\n    # Self-shielding term\n    term1 = np.exp(-k1 * N_CO**a1)\n\n    # Mutual shielding by H2\n    term2 = np.exp(-k2 * N_H2**a2)\n\n    # Cross-term\n    term3 = 1.0 / (1.0 + k3 * N_CO**a3 * np.exp(-k4 * N_H2**a4))\n\n    f = term1 * term2 * term3\n\n    return np.clip(f, 1e-10, 1.0)\n\n\ndef effective_shielding_multiray(N_H2_rays, N_CO_rays, T=100.0):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute effective shielding factor averaged over multiple ray directions.\n\n    For 3D geometry, the shielding seen by a cell depends on direction.\n    We compute the mean photodissociation rate by averaging the shielded\n    rates over all directions.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    N_H2_rays : ndarray, shape (n_rays,)\n        H2 column density along each ray [cm\u207b\u00b2]\n    N_CO_rays : ndarray, shape (n_rays,)\n        CO column density along each ray [cm\u207b\u00b2]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K]\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    f_H2_eff : float\n        Effective H2 shielding factor\n    f_CO_eff : float\n        Effective CO shielding factor\n    \"\"\"\n    # Compute shielding along each ray\n    f_H2 = f_shield_H2(N_H2_rays, T=T)\n    f_CO = f_shield_CO(N_CO_rays, N_H2_rays)\n\n    # Average (equal solid angle weights assumed)\n    f_H2_eff = np.mean(f_H2)\n    f_CO_eff = np.mean(f_CO)\n\n    return f_H2_eff, f_CO_eff\n",

      "description": "Heating processes for PDRs",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nHeating processes for PRISM-3D PDR models.\n\nImplements all major heating mechanisms:\n1. Photoelectric heating on dust grains and PAHs\n2. H2 photodissociation heating\n3. H2 formation heating\n4. H2 UV pumping (vibrational de-excitation)\n5. Cosmic ray heating\n6. Gas-grain collisional heating\n7. Carbon photoionization heating\n8. Chemical heating (exothermic reactions)\n\nAll rates returned in erg/cm\u00b3/s.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import (k_boltz, eV_to_erg, G0_to_flux, m_H,\n                                 solar_abundances, h_planck, c_light)\n\n\ndef photoelectric_heating(n_H, T, G0, x_e, grain_param=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Photoelectric (PE) heating on dust grains and PAHs.\n\n    Uses the Bakes & Tielens (1994) / Weingartner & Draine (2001)\n    formulation. The PE heating rate depends on the grain charging\n    parameter psi = G0 * sqrt(T) / (n_e), which controls the\n    balance between UV photoemission and electron recapture.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K]\n    G0 : float\n        FUV field [Habing units]\n    x_e : float\n        Electron fraction n_e/n_H\n    grain_param : dict, optional\n        Grain parameters. If None, uses standard ISM values.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma_PE : float\n        PE heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    if G0 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    if n_e <= 0:\n        n_e = 1e-10 * n_H  # floor\n\n    # Charging parameter\n    psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n\n    # Bakes & Tielens (1994) Eq. 41-42\n    # Heating efficiency epsilon(psi)\n    epsilon = 0.049 / (1.0 + (psi / 1925.0)**0.73) \\\n            + 0.037 * (T / 1e4)**0.7 / (1.0 + psi / 5000.0)\n\n    # Total PE heating rate\n    # Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H\n\n    return Gamma_PE\n\n\ndef photoelectric_heating_wd01(n_H, T, G0, x_e, R_V=3.1):\n    \"\"\"\n    PE heating from Weingartner & Draine (2001) - more accurate than BT94.\n\n    Uses their fit for the standard ISM grain size distribution.\n    \"\"\"\n    if G0 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_e = max(x_e * n_H, 1e-10 * n_H)\n\n    # WD01 charging parameter (their Eq. 2)\n    phi_pah = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / (n_e * 1.0)  # for PAH fraction = 1\n\n    # WD01 heating rate (their Eq. 1 and Table 2 for R_V=3.1)\n    C0 = 5.22e-26  # erg/s\n    C1 = 3.46e-2\n    C2 = 5.11e-1\n    C3 = 1.83e4\n    C4 = 1.39e1\n    C5 = 6.14e3\n    C6 = 1.36e-3\n\n    epsilon = C1 / (1.0 + C2 * phi_pah**C3) + C4 / (1.0 + C5 * phi_pah) + C6\n\n    Gamma = C0 * epsilon * G0 * n_H\n\n    return max(Gamma, 0.0)\n\n\ndef h2_photodissociation_heating(n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 photodissociation.\n\n    Each H2 dissociation event deposits ~0.4 eV of kinetic energy\n    (the excess above the dissociation energy).\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    x_H2 : float\n        H2 abundance relative to n_H\n    G0 : float\n        FUV field [Habing units]\n    A_V : float\n        Visual extinction [mag]\n    f_shield_H2 : float\n        H2 self-shielding factor\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma : float\n        Heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    # H2 photodissociation rate\n    k_pd = 3.3e-11 * G0 * np.exp(-3.74 * A_V) * f_shield_H2  # s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    # Energy deposited per dissociation\n    E_heat = 0.4 * eV_to_erg  # erg\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    return k_pd * n_H2 * E_heat\n\n\ndef h2_formation_heating(n_H, x_HI, T, R_H2=3e-17):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 formation on grain surfaces.\n\n    Formation releases 4.48 eV. A fraction goes to the grain,\n    a fraction to kinetic energy, and a fraction to H2 internal states.\n    Following Tielens & Hollenbach (1985): ~1/3 each.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    R_H2 : float\n        H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    # Formation rate = R_H2 * n(H) * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    formation_rate = R_H2 * np.sqrt(T / 100.0) * n_HI * n_H  # cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    # Kinetic energy fraction: ~1.5 eV per formation\n    E_kin = 1.5 * eV_to_erg\n\n    return formation_rate * E_kin\n\n\ndef h2_uv_pumping_heating(n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2, T, n_cr=1e4):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 UV fluorescent pumping.\n\n    FUV photons excite H2 to excited electronic states, which cascade\n    back producing vibrationally excited H2 in the ground state.\n    Collisional de-excitation of these levels heats the gas.\n\n    The efficiency depends on whether the gas is above or below the\n    critical density for vibrational de-excitation (~1e4 cm\u207b\u00b3).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pumping rate (similar to photodissociation but ~10x more absorptions)\n    k_pump = 3.3e-10 * G0 * np.exp(-3.74 * A_V) * f_shield_H2  # s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    # Energy per de-excitation event: ~2 eV if collisionally de-excited\n    E_vib = 2.0 * eV_to_erg\n\n    # Fraction that goes to heating (depends on density vs critical density)\n    f_heat = n_H / (n_H + n_cr)\n\n    return k_pump * n_H2 * E_vib * f_heat\n\n\ndef cosmic_ray_heating(n_H, x_H2, x_HI, zeta_CR):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating by cosmic ray ionization.\n\n    Each CR ionization deposits ~20 eV to the gas through the\n    primary electron and its secondary ionizations.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    zeta_CR : float\n        Primary CR ionization rate of H [s\u207b\u00b9]\n    \"\"\"\n    # Energy deposited per ionization: ~20 eV (Glassgold+ 2012)\n    # In molecular gas, H2 ionization dominates\n    E_CR = 20.0 * eV_to_erg\n\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    # CR ionization rate for H2 is ~2x that of H\n    Gamma = zeta_CR * (n_HI * E_CR + 2.0 * n_H2 * E_CR)\n\n    return Gamma\n\n\ndef gas_grain_heating(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    Gas-grain collisional heating (or cooling if T_gas > T_dust).\n\n    Gas particles collide with dust grains and exchange thermal energy.\n    Important in dense, shielded regions.\n\n    From Hollenbach & McKee (1989).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Accommodation coefficient\n    alpha_T = 0.35\n\n    # Grain cross section per H\n    sigma_d = 1e-21  # cm\u00b2 per H\n\n    # Mean thermal speed\n    from ..utils.constants import m_H\n    mu = 2.0 if x_H2 > 0.5 else 1.0  # mean molecular weight\n    v_th = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T_gas / (np.pi * mu * m_H))\n\n    # Heating rate (positive when T_dust > T_gas)\n    Gamma_gg = 2.0 * alpha_T * sigma_d * n_H**2 * v_th * k_boltz * (T_dust - T_gas)\n\n    return Gamma_gg\n\n\ndef carbon_photoionization_heating(n_H, x_C, G0, A_V):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from photoionization of neutral carbon.\n\n    Each C photoionization releases ~1 eV of kinetic energy\n    (photon energy - IP of 11.26 eV, typical photon ~12.3 eV).\n    \"\"\"\n    k_ion = 3.0e-10 * G0 * np.exp(-3.0 * A_V)  # s\u207b\u00b9\n    E_kin = 1.0 * eV_to_erg\n    n_C = x_C * n_H\n    return k_ion * n_C * E_kin\n\n\ndef total_heating_rate(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                        x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C, x_Cp,\n                        f_shield_H2=1.0, R_H2=3e-17):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute total heating rate.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    All standard cell properties.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma_total : float\n        Total heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    components : dict\n        Individual heating rate components\n    \"\"\"\n    components = {}\n\n    components['PE'] = photoelectric_heating(n_H, T_gas, G0, x_e)\n    components['H2_photodiss'] = h2_photodissociation_heating(\n        n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2)\n    components['H2_formation'] = h2_formation_heating(n_H, x_HI, T_gas, R_H2)\n    components['H2_pumping'] = h2_uv_pumping_heating(\n        n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2, T_gas)\n    components['CR'] = cosmic_ray_heating(n_H, x_H2, x_HI, zeta_CR)\n    components['gas_grain'] = gas_grain_heating(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2)\n    components['C_photoion'] = carbon_photoionization_heating(n_H, x_C, G0, A_V)\n\n    Gamma_total = sum(components.values())\n    return Gamma_total, components\n",

      "code": "\"\"\"\nHeating processes for PRISM-3D PDR models.\n\nImplements all major heating mechanisms:\n1. Photoelectric heating on dust grains and PAHs\n2. H2 photodissociation heating\n3. H2 formation heating\n4. H2 UV pumping (vibrational de-excitation)\n5. Cosmic ray heating\n6. Gas-grain collisional heating\n7. Carbon photoionization heating\n8. Chemical heating (exothermic reactions)\n\nAll rates returned in erg/cm\u00b3/s.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import (k_boltz, eV_to_erg, G0_to_flux, m_H,\n                                 solar_abundances, h_planck, c_light)\n\n\ndef photoelectric_heating(n_H, T, G0, x_e, grain_param=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Photoelectric (PE) heating on dust grains and PAHs.\n\n    Uses the Bakes & Tielens (1994) / Weingartner & Draine (2001)\n    formulation. The PE heating rate depends on the grain charging\n    parameter psi = G0 * sqrt(T) / (n_e), which controls the\n    balance between UV photoemission and electron recapture.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K]\n    G0 : float\n        FUV field [Habing units]\n    x_e : float\n        Electron fraction n_e/n_H\n    grain_param : dict, optional\n        Grain parameters. If None, uses standard ISM values.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma_PE : float\n        PE heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    if G0 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    if n_e <= 0:\n        n_e = 1e-10 * n_H  # floor\n\n    # Charging parameter\n    psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n\n    # Bakes & Tielens (1994) Eq. 41-42\n    # Heating efficiency epsilon(psi)\n    epsilon = 0.049 / (1.0 + (psi / 1925.0)**0.73) \\\n            + 0.037 * (T / 1e4)**0.7 / (1.0 + psi / 5000.0)\n\n    # Total PE heating rate\n    # Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H\n\n    return Gamma_PE\n\n\ndef photoelectric_heating_wd01(n_H, T, G0, x_e, R_V=3.1):\n    \"\"\"\n    PE heating from Weingartner & Draine (2001) - more accurate than BT94.\n\n    Uses their fit for the standard ISM grain size distribution.\n    \"\"\"\n    if G0 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_e = max(x_e * n_H, 1e-10 * n_H)\n\n    # WD01 charging parameter (their Eq. 2)\n    phi_pah = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / (n_e * 1.0)  # for PAH fraction = 1\n\n    # WD01 heating rate (their Eq. 1 and Table 2 for R_V=3.1)\n    C0 = 5.22e-26  # erg/s\n    C1 = 3.46e-2\n    C2 = 5.11e-1\n    C3 = 1.83e4\n    C4 = 1.39e1\n    C5 = 6.14e3\n    C6 = 1.36e-3\n\n    epsilon = C1 / (1.0 + C2 * phi_pah**C3) + C4 / (1.0 + C5 * phi_pah) + C6\n\n    Gamma = C0 * epsilon * G0 * n_H\n\n    return max(Gamma, 0.0)\n\n\ndef h2_photodissociation_heating(n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 photodissociation.\n\n    Each H2 dissociation event deposits ~0.4 eV of kinetic energy\n    (the excess above the dissociation energy).\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    x_H2 : float\n        H2 abundance relative to n_H\n    G0 : float\n        FUV field [Habing units]\n    A_V : float\n        Visual extinction [mag]\n    f_shield_H2 : float\n        H2 self-shielding factor\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma : float\n        Heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    # H2 photodissociation rate\n    k_pd = 3.3e-11 * G0 * np.exp(-3.74 * A_V) * f_shield_H2  # s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    # Energy deposited per dissociation\n    E_heat = 0.4 * eV_to_erg  # erg\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    return k_pd * n_H2 * E_heat\n\n\ndef h2_formation_heating(n_H, x_HI, T, R_H2=3e-17):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 formation on grain surfaces.\n\n    Formation releases 4.48 eV. A fraction goes to the grain,\n    a fraction to kinetic energy, and a fraction to H2 internal states.\n    Following Tielens & Hollenbach (1985): ~1/3 each.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    R_H2 : float\n        H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    # Formation rate = R_H2 * n(H) * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    formation_rate = R_H2 * np.sqrt(T / 100.0) * n_HI * n_H  # cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    # Kinetic energy fraction: ~1.5 eV per formation\n    E_kin = 1.5 * eV_to_erg\n\n    return formation_rate * E_kin\n\n\ndef h2_uv_pumping_heating(n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2, T, n_cr=1e4):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 UV fluorescent pumping.\n\n    FUV photons excite H2 to excited electronic states, which cascade\n    back producing vibrationally excited H2 in the ground state.\n    Collisional de-excitation of these levels heats the gas.\n\n    The efficiency depends on whether the gas is above or below the\n    critical density for vibrational de-excitation (~1e4 cm\u207b\u00b3).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pumping rate (similar to photodissociation but ~10x more absorptions)\n    k_pump = 3.3e-10 * G0 * np.exp(-3.74 * A_V) * f_shield_H2  # s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    # Energy per de-excitation event: ~2 eV if collisionally de-excited\n    E_vib = 2.0 * eV_to_erg\n\n    # Fraction that goes to heating (depends on density vs critical density)\n    f_heat = n_H / (n_H + n_cr)\n\n    return k_pump * n_H2 * E_vib * f_heat\n\n\ndef cosmic_ray_heating(n_H, x_H2, x_HI, zeta_CR):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating by cosmic ray ionization.\n\n    Each CR ionization deposits ~20 eV to the gas through the\n    primary electron and its secondary ionizations.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    zeta_CR : float\n        Primary CR ionization rate of H [s\u207b\u00b9]\n    \"\"\"\n    # Energy deposited per ionization: ~20 eV (Glassgold+ 2012)\n    # In molecular gas, H2 ionization dominates\n    E_CR = 20.0 * eV_to_erg\n\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    # CR ionization rate for H2 is ~2x that of H\n    Gamma = zeta_CR * (n_HI * E_CR + 2.0 * n_H2 * E_CR)\n\n    return Gamma\n\n\ndef gas_grain_heating(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    Gas-grain collisional heating (or cooling if T_gas > T_dust).\n\n    Gas particles collide with dust grains and exchange thermal energy.\n    Important in dense, shielded regions.\n\n    From Hollenbach & McKee (1989).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Accommodation coefficient\n    alpha_T = 0.35\n\n    # Grain cross section per H\n    sigma_d = 1e-21  # cm\u00b2 per H\n\n    # Mean thermal speed\n    from ..utils.constants import m_H\n    mu = 2.0 if x_H2 > 0.5 else 1.0  # mean molecular weight\n    v_th = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T_gas / (np.pi * mu * m_H))\n\n    # Heating rate (positive when T_dust > T_gas)\n    Gamma_gg = 2.0 * alpha_T * sigma_d * n_H**2 * v_th * k_boltz * (T_dust - T_gas)\n\n    return Gamma_gg\n\n\ndef carbon_photoionization_heating(n_H, x_C, G0, A_V):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from photoionization of neutral carbon.\n\n    Each C photoionization releases ~1 eV of kinetic energy\n    (photon energy - IP of 11.26 eV, typical photon ~12.3 eV).\n    \"\"\"\n    k_ion = 3.0e-10 * G0 * np.exp(-3.0 * A_V)  # s\u207b\u00b9\n    E_kin = 1.0 * eV_to_erg\n    n_C = x_C * n_H\n    return k_ion * n_C * E_kin\n\n\ndef total_heating_rate(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                        x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C, x_Cp,\n                        f_shield_H2=1.0, R_H2=3e-17):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute total heating rate.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    All standard cell properties.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma_total : float\n        Total heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    components : dict\n        Individual heating rate components\n    \"\"\"\n    components = {}\n\n    components['PE'] = photoelectric_heating(n_H, T_gas, G0, x_e)\n    components['H2_photodiss'] = h2_photodissociation_heating(\n        n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2)\n    components['H2_formation'] = h2_formation_heating(n_H, x_HI, T_gas, R_H2)\n    components['H2_pumping'] = h2_uv_pumping_heating(\n        n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2, T_gas)\n    components['CR'] = cosmic_ray_heating(n_H, x_H2, x_HI, zeta_CR)\n    components['gas_grain'] = gas_grain_heating(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2)\n    components['C_photoion'] = carbon_photoionization_heating(n_H, x_C, G0, A_V)\n\n    Gamma_total = sum(components.values())\n    return Gamma_total, components\n",

      "description": "Cooling processes for PDRs",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nCooling processes for PRISM-3D PDR models.\n\nImplements all major cooling mechanisms:\n1. [CII] 158 \u03bcm fine-structure line\n2. [OI] 63 \u03bcm and 145 \u03bcm fine-structure lines\n3. [CI] 609 \u03bcm and 370 \u03bcm lines\n4. CO rotational lines (full ladder J=1-0 through J=40-39)\n5. H2 ro-vibrational lines\n6. Lyman-alpha cooling\n7. Gas-grain collisional cooling\n8. Recombination cooling\n9. Metastable line cooling (e.g., [SiII], [FeII])\n\nAll rates returned in erg/cm\u00b3/s.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import (k_boltz, h_planck, c_light, m_H, m_proton,\n                                 fine_structure_lines, eV_to_erg)\n\n\ndef cii_158_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by [CII] 158 \u03bcm \u00b2P\u2083/\u2082 \u2192 \u00b2P\u2081/\u2082 transition.\n\n    This is the dominant coolant in the FUV-illuminated surface layers.\n    Computed using a two-level atom with collisional de-excitation\n    rates for e\u207b, H, and H2 collisions.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K]\n    x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp : float\n        Abundances relative to n_H\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Lambda_CII : float\n        Cooling rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    _, freq, A_ul, T_ul, g_u, g_l = fine_structure_lines['CII_158']\n\n    # Collision rate coefficients [cm\u00b3/s]\n    # Electron collisions (Blum & Pradhan 1992)\n    gamma_e = 8.63e-6 / (g_l * np.sqrt(T)) * 2.15 * np.exp(-T_ul / T)\n\n    # H collisions (Barinovs+ 2005)\n    gamma_H = 8.0e-10 * (T / 100.0)**0.07\n\n    # H2 collisions (Wiesenfeld & Goldsmith 2014)\n    gamma_H2 = 4.9e-10 * (T / 100.0)**0.12\n\n    # Total de-excitation rate\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_Cp = x_Cp * n_H\n\n    R_ul = A_ul + gamma_e * n_e + gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2\n\n    # Two-level population\n    # n_u/n_l = (g_u/g_l) * [gamma_e*n_e + gamma_H*n_HI + gamma_H2*n_H2] / R_ul * exp(-T_ul/T)\n    R_lu = (g_u / g_l) * (gamma_e * n_e + gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2) * np.exp(-T_ul / T)\n\n    n_u = n_Cp * R_lu / (R_lu + R_ul)\n\n    # Cooling rate\n    E_ul = h_planck * freq\n    Lambda = n_u * A_ul * E_ul  # erg/cm\u00b3/s (optically thin)\n\n    return Lambda\n\n\ndef oi_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_O):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by [OI] 63 \u03bcm and 145 \u03bcm transitions.\n\n    Three-level atom: \u00b3P\u2082 (ground), \u00b3P\u2081, \u00b3P\u2080.\n    63 \u03bcm: \u00b3P\u2081 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2082 (dominant)\n    145 \u03bcm: \u00b3P\u2080 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2081\n\n    Collisional rates from Abrahamsson+ (2007) and Jaquet+ (1992).\n    \"\"\"\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_O = x_O * n_H\n\n    # 63 \u03bcm transition\n    _, freq_63, A_63, T_63, g_u_63, g_l_63 = fine_structure_lines['OI_63']\n\n    # H collision rate for 63 \u03bcm (Abrahamsson+ 2007)\n    gamma_H_63 = 9.2e-12 * T**0.67\n    gamma_H2_63 = 4.5e-12 * T**0.64\n    gamma_e_63 = 1.4e-8 * (T / 100.0)**0.39\n\n    R_lu_63 = (g_u_63 / g_l_63) * (\n        gamma_H_63 * n_HI + gamma_H2_63 * n_H2 + gamma_e_63 * n_e\n    ) * np.exp(-T_63 / T)\n    R_ul_63 = A_63 + gamma_H_63 * n_HI + gamma_H2_63 * n_H2 + gamma_e_63 * n_e\n\n    n_u_63 = n_O * R_lu_63 / (R_lu_63 + R_ul_63)\n    Lambda_63 = n_u_63 * A_63 * h_planck * freq_63\n\n    # 145 \u03bcm transition\n    _, freq_145, A_145, T_145, g_u_145, g_l_145 = fine_structure_lines['OI_145']\n\n    gamma_H_145 = 4.0e-12 * T**0.60\n    gamma_H2_145 = 2.0e-12 * T**0.58\n    gamma_e_145 = 5.0e-9 * (T / 100.0)**0.39\n\n    R_lu_145 = (g_u_145 / g_l_145) * (\n        gamma_H_145 * n_HI + gamma_H2_145 * n_H2 + gamma_e_145 * n_e\n    ) * np.exp(-T_145 / T)\n    R_ul_145 = A_145 + gamma_H_145 * n_HI + gamma_H2_145 * n_H2 + gamma_e_145 * n_e\n\n    n_u_145 = n_O * R_lu_145 / (R_lu_145 + R_ul_145)\n    Lambda_145 = n_u_145 * A_145 * h_planck * freq_145\n\n    return Lambda_63 + Lambda_145\n\n\ndef ci_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by [CI] 609 \u03bcm (\u00b3P\u2081 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2080) and 370 \u03bcm (\u00b3P\u2082 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2081).\n    \"\"\"\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_C = x_C * n_H\n\n    Lambda_total = 0.0\n\n    for line_name in ['CI_609', 'CI_370']:\n        _, freq, A_ul, T_ul, g_u, g_l = fine_structure_lines[line_name]\n\n        # Collision rates (Schroder+ 1991, Johnson+ 1987)\n        gamma_H = 1.0e-10 * (T / 100.0)**0.3\n        gamma_H2 = 5.0e-11 * (T / 100.0)**0.3\n        gamma_e = 2.0e-7 * (T / 100.0)**(-0.5)\n\n        R_lu = (g_u / g_l) * (\n            gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2 + gamma_e * n_e\n        ) * np.exp(-T_ul / T)\n        R_ul = A_ul + gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2 + gamma_e * n_e\n\n        n_u = n_C * R_lu / (R_lu + R_ul)\n        Lambda_total += n_u * A_ul * h_planck * freq\n\n    return Lambda_total\n\n\ndef co_rotational_cooling(n_H, T, x_H2, x_CO, N_CO=0.0):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by CO rotational lines.\n\n    Sums over the CO rotational ladder up to J=40.\n    Uses escape probability for optical depth effects.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    N_CO : float\n        CO column density [cm\u207b\u00b2] for escape probability calculation.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ..utils.constants import CO_B_rot, CO_dipole\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_CO = x_CO * n_H\n\n    if n_CO <= 0 or T < 5:\n        return 0.0\n\n    Lambda_CO = 0.0\n\n    # CO properties\n    B_rot = 57.635968e9  # Hz (rotational constant)\n    mu_d = 0.1098e-18    # Debye in esu\u00b7cm\n\n    J_max = min(40, int(5.0 * np.sqrt(T / 2.77)))\n\n    for J_u in range(1, J_max + 1):\n        J_l = J_u - 1\n\n        # Transition frequency\n        freq = 2.0 * B_rot * J_u  # Hz\n\n        # Energy of upper level\n        E_u = h_planck * B_rot * J_u * (J_u + 1)\n        T_u = E_u / k_boltz\n\n        if T_u > 5.0 * T:\n            break\n\n        # Einstein A coefficient\n        A_ul = (64.0 * np.pi**4 * freq**3 * mu_d**2 * J_u) / \\\n               (3.0 * h_planck * c_light**3 * (2*J_u + 1))\n\n        # Statistical weights\n        g_u = 2 * J_u + 1\n        g_l = 2 * J_l + 1\n\n        # CO-H2 collision rate (Flower 2001, approximate)\n        gamma_H2 = 3.3e-11 * (T / 100.0)**0.5 * (1.0 + 0.1 * J_u)\n\n        # Excitation rate\n        R_lu = (g_u / g_l) * gamma_H2 * n_H2 * np.exp(-T_u / T)\n        R_ul = A_ul + gamma_H2 * n_H2\n\n        # Population of upper level\n        n_u = n_CO * R_lu / (R_lu + R_ul)\n\n        # Escape probability (large velocity gradient approximation)\n        if N_CO > 0:\n            tau_line = A_ul * c_light**3 * n_u / (8.0 * np.pi * freq**3) \\\n                      * (n_CO * g_u / (n_u * g_l) - 1.0) * N_CO / n_CO \\\n                      if n_u > 0 else 0.0\n            tau_line = abs(tau_line)\n            if tau_line > 0.01:\n                beta_esc = (1.0 - np.exp(-tau_line)) / tau_line\n            else:\n                beta_esc = 1.0\n        else:\n            beta_esc = 1.0\n\n        Lambda_CO += n_u * A_ul * h_planck * freq * beta_esc\n\n    return Lambda_CO\n\n\ndef h2_rovibrational_cooling(n_H, T, x_H2, x_HI):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by H2 ro-vibrational lines.\n\n    Important at T > 500 K in the warm PDR surface layers.\n    Uses the Glover & Abel (2008) fit.\n    \"\"\"\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n\n    if T < 100 or n_H2 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    # Low-density cooling function \u039b\u2080(T) [erg/s per H2 molecule]\n    # Glover & Abel (2008) fit for H-H2 and H2-H2 collisions\n    logT = np.log10(T)\n    logT = np.clip(logT, 2.0, 4.5)\n\n    # H-H2 cooling (Glover & Abel 2008)\n    log_LH = -24.311 + 3.5692 * logT - 11.332 * logT**2 \\\n             + 15.738 * logT**3 - 10.581 * logT**4 \\\n             + 3.5803 * logT**5 - 0.48365 * logT**6\n\n    # H2-H2 cooling (ibid)\n    log_LH2 = -24.311 + 4.6585 * logT - 14.272 * logT**2 \\\n              + 19.779 * logT**3 - 13.255 * logT**4 \\\n              + 4.4840 * logT**5 - 0.60504 * logT**6\n\n    L_H = 10**log_LH    # erg/s per H2\n    L_H2 = 10**log_LH2  # erg/s per H2\n\n    # LTE cooling function (high density limit)\n    log_LTE = -19.703 + 0.5 * logT\n\n    L_LTE = 10**log_LTE  # erg/s per H2\n\n    # Effective cooling rate with density interpolation\n    L0 = L_H * n_HI + L_H2 * n_H2\n    Lambda_H2 = n_H2 * L0 * L_LTE / (L0 + L_LTE) if (L0 + L_LTE) > 0 else 0.0\n\n    return Lambda_H2\n\n\ndef lyman_alpha_cooling(n_H, T, x_HI, x_e):\n    \"\"\"\n    Lyman-alpha cooling (collisional excitation of H by electrons).\n\n    Important at T > 8000 K.\n    \"\"\"\n    if T < 3000:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n\n    # Collisional excitation rate coefficient (Osterbrock & Ferland 2006)\n    T4 = T / 1e4\n    q_lu = 2.41e-6 / np.sqrt(T) * 0.486 * np.exp(-1.18e5 / T)  # cm\u00b3/s\n\n    E_lya = 10.2 * eV_to_erg\n\n    return n_HI * n_e * q_lu * E_lya\n\n\ndef gas_grain_cooling(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    Gas-grain collisional cooling.\n    Same as heating but with opposite sign when T_gas > T_dust.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .heating import gas_grain_heating\n    Gamma_gg = gas_grain_heating(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2)\n    # gas_grain_heating returns positive when T_dust > T_gas (heating)\n    # Cooling is when T_gas > T_dust, so cooling = -heating when heating < 0\n    return max(-Gamma_gg, 0.0)\n\n\ndef recombination_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_Cp, x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep):\n    \"\"\"\n    Radiative recombination cooling.\n\n    Energy lost when ions recombine and emit recombination photons.\n    \"\"\"\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n\n    Lambda = 0.0\n\n    # C+ recombination (kT per recombination)\n    alpha_Cp = 4.67e-12 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.6)\n    Lambda += x_Cp * n_H * n_e * alpha_Cp * k_boltz * T\n\n    # Other ions (smaller contribution)\n    alpha_Sp = 3.9e-11 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.63)\n    Lambda += x_Sp * n_H * n_e * alpha_Sp * k_boltz * T\n\n    alpha_Sip = 4.26e-12 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.62)\n    Lambda += x_Sip * n_H * n_e * alpha_Sip * k_boltz * T\n\n    alpha_Fep = 3.7e-12 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.65)\n    Lambda += x_Fep * n_H * n_e * alpha_Fep * k_boltz * T\n\n    return Lambda\n\n\ndef total_cooling_rate(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_e, x_HI, x_H2,\n                        x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n                        x_Sp=0, x_Sip=0, x_Fep=0, N_CO=0.0):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute total cooling rate.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Lambda_total : float\n        Total cooling rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    components : dict\n        Individual cooling rate components\n    \"\"\"\n    components = {}\n\n    components['CII_158'] = cii_158_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp)\n    components['OI'] = oi_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_O)\n    components['CI'] = ci_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C)\n    components['CO_rot'] = co_rotational_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_H2, x_CO, N_CO)\n    components['H2_rovib'] = h2_rovibrational_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_H2, x_HI)\n    components['Lya'] = lyman_alpha_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_HI, x_e)\n    components['gas_grain'] = gas_grain_cooling(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2)\n    components['recombination'] = recombination_cooling(\n        n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_Cp, x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep)\n\n    Lambda_total = sum(components.values())\n    return Lambda_total, components\n",

    "integration_name": null,

      "type": "code_block",
      "language": "python",
      "code": "\"\"\"\nCooling processes for PRISM-3D PDR models.\n\nImplements all major cooling mechanisms:\n1. [CII] 158 \u03bcm fine-structure line\n2. [OI] 63 \u03bcm and 145 \u03bcm fine-structure lines\n3. [CI] 609 \u03bcm and 370 \u03bcm lines\n4. CO rotational lines (full ladder J=1-0 through J=40-39)\n5. H2 ro-vibrational lines\n6. Lyman-alpha cooling\n7. Gas-grain collisional cooling\n8. Recombination cooling\n9. Metastable line cooling (e.g., [SiII], [FeII])\n\nAll rates returned in erg/cm\u00b3/s.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import (k_boltz, h_planck, c_light, m_H, m_proton,\n                                 fine_structure_lines, eV_to_erg)\n\n\ndef cii_158_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by [CII] 158 \u03bcm \u00b2P\u2083/\u2082 \u2192 \u00b2P\u2081/\u2082 transition.\n\n    This is the dominant coolant in the FUV-illuminated surface layers.\n    Computed using a two-level atom with collisional de-excitation\n    rates for e\u207b, H, and H2 collisions.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K]\n    x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp : float\n        Abundances relative to n_H\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Lambda_CII : float\n        Cooling rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    _, freq, A_ul, T_ul, g_u, g_l = fine_structure_lines['CII_158']\n\n    # Collision rate coefficients [cm\u00b3/s]\n    # Electron collisions (Blum & Pradhan 1992)\n    gamma_e = 8.63e-6 / (g_l * np.sqrt(T)) * 2.15 * np.exp(-T_ul / T)\n\n    # H collisions (Barinovs+ 2005)\n    gamma_H = 8.0e-10 * (T / 100.0)**0.07\n\n    # H2 collisions (Wiesenfeld & Goldsmith 2014)\n    gamma_H2 = 4.9e-10 * (T / 100.0)**0.12\n\n    # Total de-excitation rate\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_Cp = x_Cp * n_H\n\n    R_ul = A_ul + gamma_e * n_e + gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2\n\n    # Two-level population\n    # n_u/n_l = (g_u/g_l) * [gamma_e*n_e + gamma_H*n_HI + gamma_H2*n_H2] / R_ul * exp(-T_ul/T)\n    R_lu = (g_u / g_l) * (gamma_e * n_e + gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2) * np.exp(-T_ul / T)\n\n    n_u = n_Cp * R_lu / (R_lu + R_ul)\n\n    # Cooling rate\n    E_ul = h_planck * freq\n    Lambda = n_u * A_ul * E_ul  # erg/cm\u00b3/s (optically thin)\n\n    return Lambda\n\n\ndef oi_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_O):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by [OI] 63 \u03bcm and 145 \u03bcm transitions.\n\n    Three-level atom: \u00b3P\u2082 (ground), \u00b3P\u2081, \u00b3P\u2080.\n    63 \u03bcm: \u00b3P\u2081 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2082 (dominant)\n    145 \u03bcm: \u00b3P\u2080 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2081\n\n    Collisional rates from Abrahamsson+ (2007) and Jaquet+ (1992).\n    \"\"\"\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_O = x_O * n_H\n\n    # 63 \u03bcm transition\n    _, freq_63, A_63, T_63, g_u_63, g_l_63 = fine_structure_lines['OI_63']\n\n    # H collision rate for 63 \u03bcm (Abrahamsson+ 2007)\n    gamma_H_63 = 9.2e-12 * T**0.67\n    gamma_H2_63 = 4.5e-12 * T**0.64\n    gamma_e_63 = 1.4e-8 * (T / 100.0)**0.39\n\n    R_lu_63 = (g_u_63 / g_l_63) * (\n        gamma_H_63 * n_HI + gamma_H2_63 * n_H2 + gamma_e_63 * n_e\n    ) * np.exp(-T_63 / T)\n    R_ul_63 = A_63 + gamma_H_63 * n_HI + gamma_H2_63 * n_H2 + gamma_e_63 * n_e\n\n    n_u_63 = n_O * R_lu_63 / (R_lu_63 + R_ul_63)\n    Lambda_63 = n_u_63 * A_63 * h_planck * freq_63\n\n    # 145 \u03bcm transition\n    _, freq_145, A_145, T_145, g_u_145, g_l_145 = fine_structure_lines['OI_145']\n\n    gamma_H_145 = 4.0e-12 * T**0.60\n    gamma_H2_145 = 2.0e-12 * T**0.58\n    gamma_e_145 = 5.0e-9 * (T / 100.0)**0.39\n\n    R_lu_145 = (g_u_145 / g_l_145) * (\n        gamma_H_145 * n_HI + gamma_H2_145 * n_H2 + gamma_e_145 * n_e\n    ) * np.exp(-T_145 / T)\n    R_ul_145 = A_145 + gamma_H_145 * n_HI + gamma_H2_145 * n_H2 + gamma_e_145 * n_e\n\n    n_u_145 = n_O * R_lu_145 / (R_lu_145 + R_ul_145)\n    Lambda_145 = n_u_145 * A_145 * h_planck * freq_145\n\n    return Lambda_63 + Lambda_145\n\n\ndef ci_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by [CI] 609 \u03bcm (\u00b3P\u2081 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2080) and 370 \u03bcm (\u00b3P\u2082 \u2192 \u00b3P\u2081).\n    \"\"\"\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_C = x_C * n_H\n\n    Lambda_total = 0.0\n\n    for line_name in ['CI_609', 'CI_370']:\n        _, freq, A_ul, T_ul, g_u, g_l = fine_structure_lines[line_name]\n\n        # Collision rates (Schroder+ 1991, Johnson+ 1987)\n        gamma_H = 1.0e-10 * (T / 100.0)**0.3\n        gamma_H2 = 5.0e-11 * (T / 100.0)**0.3\n        gamma_e = 2.0e-7 * (T / 100.0)**(-0.5)\n\n        R_lu = (g_u / g_l) * (\n            gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2 + gamma_e * n_e\n        ) * np.exp(-T_ul / T)\n        R_ul = A_ul + gamma_H * n_HI + gamma_H2 * n_H2 + gamma_e * n_e\n\n        n_u = n_C * R_lu / (R_lu + R_ul)\n        Lambda_total += n_u * A_ul * h_planck * freq\n\n    return Lambda_total\n\n\ndef co_rotational_cooling(n_H, T, x_H2, x_CO, N_CO=0.0):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by CO rotational lines.\n\n    Sums over the CO rotational ladder up to J=40.\n    Uses escape probability for optical depth effects.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    N_CO : float\n        CO column density [cm\u207b\u00b2] for escape probability calculation.\n    \"\"\"\n    from ..utils.constants import CO_B_rot, CO_dipole\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_CO = x_CO * n_H\n\n    if n_CO <= 0 or T < 5:\n        return 0.0\n\n    Lambda_CO = 0.0\n\n    # CO properties\n    B_rot = 57.635968e9  # Hz (rotational constant)\n    mu_d = 0.1098e-18    # Debye in esu\u00b7cm\n\n    J_max = min(40, int(5.0 * np.sqrt(T / 2.77)))\n\n    for J_u in range(1, J_max + 1):\n        J_l = J_u - 1\n\n        # Transition frequency\n        freq = 2.0 * B_rot * J_u  # Hz\n\n        # Energy of upper level\n        E_u = h_planck * B_rot * J_u * (J_u + 1)\n        T_u = E_u / k_boltz\n\n        if T_u > 5.0 * T:\n            break\n\n        # Einstein A coefficient\n        A_ul = (64.0 * np.pi**4 * freq**3 * mu_d**2 * J_u) / \\\n               (3.0 * h_planck * c_light**3 * (2*J_u + 1))\n\n        # Statistical weights\n        g_u = 2 * J_u + 1\n        g_l = 2 * J_l + 1\n\n        # CO-H2 collision rate (Flower 2001, approximate)\n        gamma_H2 = 3.3e-11 * (T / 100.0)**0.5 * (1.0 + 0.1 * J_u)\n\n        # Excitation rate\n        R_lu = (g_u / g_l) * gamma_H2 * n_H2 * np.exp(-T_u / T)\n        R_ul = A_ul + gamma_H2 * n_H2\n\n        # Population of upper level\n        n_u = n_CO * R_lu / (R_lu + R_ul)\n\n        # Escape probability (large velocity gradient approximation)\n        if N_CO > 0:\n            tau_line = A_ul * c_light**3 * n_u / (8.0 * np.pi * freq**3) \\\n                      * (n_CO * g_u / (n_u * g_l) - 1.0) * N_CO / n_CO \\\n                      if n_u > 0 else 0.0\n            tau_line = abs(tau_line)\n            if tau_line > 0.01:\n                beta_esc = (1.0 - np.exp(-tau_line)) / tau_line\n            else:\n                beta_esc = 1.0\n        else:\n            beta_esc = 1.0\n\n        Lambda_CO += n_u * A_ul * h_planck * freq * beta_esc\n\n    return Lambda_CO\n\n\ndef h2_rovibrational_cooling(n_H, T, x_H2, x_HI):\n    \"\"\"\n    Cooling by H2 ro-vibrational lines.\n\n    Important at T > 500 K in the warm PDR surface layers.\n    Uses the Glover & Abel (2008) fit.\n    \"\"\"\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n\n    if T < 100 or n_H2 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    # Low-density cooling function \u039b\u2080(T) [erg/s per H2 molecule]\n    # Glover & Abel (2008) fit for H-H2 and H2-H2 collisions\n    logT = np.log10(T)\n    logT = np.clip(logT, 2.0, 4.5)\n\n    # H-H2 cooling (Glover & Abel 2008)\n    log_LH = -24.311 + 3.5692 * logT - 11.332 * logT**2 \\\n             + 15.738 * logT**3 - 10.581 * logT**4 \\\n             + 3.5803 * logT**5 - 0.48365 * logT**6\n\n    # H2-H2 cooling (ibid)\n    log_LH2 = -24.311 + 4.6585 * logT - 14.272 * logT**2 \\\n              + 19.779 * logT**3 - 13.255 * logT**4 \\\n              + 4.4840 * logT**5 - 0.60504 * logT**6\n\n    L_H = 10**log_LH    # erg/s per H2\n    L_H2 = 10**log_LH2  # erg/s per H2\n\n    # LTE cooling function (high density limit)\n    log_LTE = -19.703 + 0.5 * logT\n\n    L_LTE = 10**log_LTE  # erg/s per H2\n\n    # Effective cooling rate with density interpolation\n    L0 = L_H * n_HI + L_H2 * n_H2\n    Lambda_H2 = n_H2 * L0 * L_LTE / (L0 + L_LTE) if (L0 + L_LTE) > 0 else 0.0\n\n    return Lambda_H2\n\n\ndef lyman_alpha_cooling(n_H, T, x_HI, x_e):\n    \"\"\"\n    Lyman-alpha cooling (collisional excitation of H by electrons).\n\n    Important at T > 8000 K.\n    \"\"\"\n    if T < 3000:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n\n    # Collisional excitation rate coefficient (Osterbrock & Ferland 2006)\n    T4 = T / 1e4\n    q_lu = 2.41e-6 / np.sqrt(T) * 0.486 * np.exp(-1.18e5 / T)  # cm\u00b3/s\n\n    E_lya = 10.2 * eV_to_erg\n\n    return n_HI * n_e * q_lu * E_lya\n\n\ndef gas_grain_cooling(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2):\n    \"\"\"\n    Gas-grain collisional cooling.\n    Same as heating but with opposite sign when T_gas > T_dust.\n    \"\"\"\n    from .heating import gas_grain_heating\n    Gamma_gg = gas_grain_heating(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2)\n    # gas_grain_heating returns positive when T_dust > T_gas (heating)\n    # Cooling is when T_gas > T_dust, so cooling = -heating when heating < 0\n    return max(-Gamma_gg, 0.0)\n\n\ndef recombination_cooling(n_H, T, x_e, x_Cp, x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep):\n    \"\"\"\n    Radiative recombination cooling.\n\n    Energy lost when ions recombine and emit recombination photons.\n    \"\"\"\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n\n    Lambda = 0.0\n\n    # C+ recombination (kT per recombination)\n    alpha_Cp = 4.67e-12 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.6)\n    Lambda += x_Cp * n_H * n_e * alpha_Cp * k_boltz * T\n\n    # Other ions (smaller contribution)\n    alpha_Sp = 3.9e-11 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.63)\n    Lambda += x_Sp * n_H * n_e * alpha_Sp * k_boltz * T\n\n    alpha_Sip = 4.26e-12 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.62)\n    Lambda += x_Sip * n_H * n_e * alpha_Sip * k_boltz * T\n\n    alpha_Fep = 3.7e-12 * (T / 300.0)**(-0.65)\n    Lambda += x_Fep * n_H * n_e * alpha_Fep * k_boltz * T\n\n    return Lambda\n\n\ndef total_cooling_rate(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_e, x_HI, x_H2,\n                        x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n                        x_Sp=0, x_Sip=0, x_Fep=0, N_CO=0.0):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute total cooling rate.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Lambda_total : float\n        Total cooling rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    components : dict\n        Individual cooling rate components\n    \"\"\"\n    components = {}\n\n    components['CII_158'] = cii_158_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp)\n    components['OI'] = oi_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_O)\n    components['CI'] = ci_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C)\n    components['CO_rot'] = co_rotational_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_H2, x_CO, N_CO)\n    components['H2_rovib'] = h2_rovibrational_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_H2, x_HI)\n    components['Lya'] = lyman_alpha_cooling(n_H, T_gas, x_HI, x_e)\n    components['gas_grain'] = gas_grain_cooling(n_H, T_gas, T_dust, x_H2)\n    components['recombination'] = recombination_cooling(\n        n_H, T_gas, x_e, x_Cp, x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep)\n\n    Lambda_total = sum(components.values())\n    return Lambda_total, components\n",

      "description": "Thermal balance solver",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nThermal equilibrium solver for PRISM-3D.\n\nSolves the equation \u0393(T) = \u039b(T) for the equilibrium gas temperature,\ngiven the local density, radiation field, and chemical abundances.\n\nUses a combination of bisection and Brent's method for robustness.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import brentq\nfrom .heating import total_heating_rate\nfrom .cooling import total_cooling_rate\n\n\nclass ThermalSolver:\n    \"\"\"\n    Solve for thermal equilibrium temperature.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    T_min : float\n        Minimum temperature to consider [K]\n    T_max : float\n        Maximum temperature to consider [K]\n    tol : float\n        Relative tolerance for temperature solution\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, T_min=10.0, T_max=1e5, tol=1e-3):\n        self.T_min = T_min\n        self.T_max = T_max\n        self.tol = tol\n\n    def solve(self, n_H, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n              x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n              x_Sp=0.0, x_Sip=0.0, x_Fep=0.0,\n              f_shield_H2=1.0, N_CO=0.0, T_guess=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Find equilibrium temperature where heating = cooling.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        T_eq : float\n            Equilibrium temperature [K]\n        Gamma : float\n            Heating rate at equilibrium [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n        Lambda : float\n            Cooling rate at equilibrium [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n        heat_components : dict\n        cool_components : dict\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def net_rate(T):\n            \"\"\"\u0393 - \u039b as a function of T.\"\"\"\n            Gamma, _ = total_heating_rate(\n                n_H, T, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C, x_Cp, f_shield_H2\n            )\n            Lambda, _ = total_cooling_rate(\n                n_H, T, T_dust, x_e, x_HI, x_H2,\n                x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n                x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep, N_CO\n            )\n            return Gamma - Lambda\n\n        # Find temperature bracket\n        T_lo = self.T_min\n        T_hi = self.T_max\n\n        # Check that there's a sign change\n        f_lo = net_rate(T_lo)\n        f_hi = net_rate(T_hi)\n\n        if f_lo * f_hi > 0:\n            # No sign change - return boundary temperature\n            if abs(f_lo) < abs(f_hi):\n                T_eq = T_lo\n            else:\n                T_eq = T_hi\n        else:\n            # Use Brent's method\n            try:\n                T_eq = brentq(net_rate, T_lo, T_hi,\n                             xtol=self.tol * T_lo, rtol=self.tol,\n                             maxiter=200)\n            except ValueError:\n                # Fall back to bisection\n                T_eq = self._bisection(net_rate, T_lo, T_hi)\n\n        # Compute final rates at equilibrium\n        Gamma, heat_comp = total_heating_rate(\n            n_H, T_eq, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n            x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C, x_Cp, f_shield_H2\n        )\n        Lambda, cool_comp = total_cooling_rate(\n            n_H, T_eq, T_dust, x_e, x_HI, x_H2,\n            x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n            x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep, N_CO\n        )\n\n        return T_eq, Gamma, Lambda, heat_comp, cool_comp\n\n    def _bisection(self, func, a, b, max_iter=100):\n        \"\"\"Simple bisection fallback.\"\"\"\n        for _ in range(max_iter):\n            mid = (a + b) / 2.0\n            if func(mid) * func(a) < 0:\n                b = mid\n            else:\n                a = mid\n            if (b - a) / a < self.tol:\n                break\n        return (a + b) / 2.0\n\n    def compute_dust_temperature(self, G0, A_V, n_H=None, T_gas=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Estimate dust temperature from radiative equilibrium.\n\n        Simple approximation: T_dust \u221d G0^(1/4+\u03b4) * exp(-A_V/4)\n\n        For a more accurate calculation, would need full dust SED\n        fitting with grain size distribution.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        A_V : float\n            Visual extinction [mag]\n        n_H : float, optional\n            Density for gas-grain coupling correction\n        T_gas : float, optional\n            Gas temperature for gas-grain coupling\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import sigma_sb\n\n        # Dust absorbs FUV and reradiates in IR\n        # Simple equilibrium: sigma_abs * F_UV = sigma_SB * T_d^(4+beta)\n        # where beta ~ 1.5-2 for grain emissivity index\n\n        # Effective UV flux\n        F_UV = 1.6e-3 * G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * A_V)  # erg/cm\u00b2/s\n\n        # Add ISRF heating floor (CMB + ISRF)\n        F_UV += 1e-5  # erg/cm\u00b2/s minimum\n\n        # T_dust from balance\n        # Assuming Q_abs(UV)/Q_abs(IR) ~ 1000 and beta=2:\n        # T_d = (Q_UV/Q_IR * F_UV / sigma_SB)^(1/6)\n        Q_ratio = 1000.0\n        T_dust = (Q_ratio * F_UV / sigma_sb) ** (1.0 / 6.0)\n\n        # Floor: CMB temperature\n        T_dust = max(T_dust, 2.725)\n\n        # Gas-grain coupling correction at high density\n        if n_H is not None and T_gas is not None and n_H > 1e5:\n            # At high density, dust approaches gas temperature\n            f_couple = min(1.0, n_H / 1e7)\n            T_dust = T_dust * (1 - f_couple) + T_gas * f_couple\n\n        return T_dust\n",

      "language": "python",
      "code": "\"\"\"\nThermal equilibrium solver for PRISM-3D.\n\nSolves the equation \u0393(T) = \u039b(T) for the equilibrium gas temperature,\ngiven the local density, radiation field, and chemical abundances.\n\nUses a combination of bisection and Brent's method for robustness.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import brentq\nfrom .heating import total_heating_rate\nfrom .cooling import total_cooling_rate\n\n\nclass ThermalSolver:\n    \"\"\"\n    Solve for thermal equilibrium temperature.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    T_min : float\n        Minimum temperature to consider [K]\n    T_max : float\n        Maximum temperature to consider [K]\n    tol : float\n        Relative tolerance for temperature solution\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, T_min=10.0, T_max=1e5, tol=1e-3):\n        self.T_min = T_min\n        self.T_max = T_max\n        self.tol = tol\n\n    def solve(self, n_H, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n              x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n              x_Sp=0.0, x_Sip=0.0, x_Fep=0.0,\n              f_shield_H2=1.0, N_CO=0.0, T_guess=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Find equilibrium temperature where heating = cooling.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        T_eq : float\n            Equilibrium temperature [K]\n        Gamma : float\n            Heating rate at equilibrium [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n        Lambda : float\n            Cooling rate at equilibrium [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n        heat_components : dict\n        cool_components : dict\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def net_rate(T):\n            \"\"\"\u0393 - \u039b as a function of T.\"\"\"\n            Gamma, _ = total_heating_rate(\n                n_H, T, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C, x_Cp, f_shield_H2\n            )\n            Lambda, _ = total_cooling_rate(\n                n_H, T, T_dust, x_e, x_HI, x_H2,\n                x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n                x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep, N_CO\n            )\n            return Gamma - Lambda\n\n        # Find temperature bracket\n        T_lo = self.T_min\n        T_hi = self.T_max\n\n        # Check that there's a sign change\n        f_lo = net_rate(T_lo)\n        f_hi = net_rate(T_hi)\n\n        if f_lo * f_hi > 0:\n            # No sign change - return boundary temperature\n            if abs(f_lo) < abs(f_hi):\n                T_eq = T_lo\n            else:\n                T_eq = T_hi\n        else:\n            # Use Brent's method\n            try:\n                T_eq = brentq(net_rate, T_lo, T_hi,\n                             xtol=self.tol * T_lo, rtol=self.tol,\n                             maxiter=200)\n            except ValueError:\n                # Fall back to bisection\n                T_eq = self._bisection(net_rate, T_lo, T_hi)\n\n        # Compute final rates at equilibrium\n        Gamma, heat_comp = total_heating_rate(\n            n_H, T_eq, T_dust, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n            x_e, x_HI, x_H2, x_C, x_Cp, f_shield_H2\n        )\n        Lambda, cool_comp = total_cooling_rate(\n            n_H, T_eq, T_dust, x_e, x_HI, x_H2,\n            x_Cp, x_C, x_O, x_CO,\n            x_Sp, x_Sip, x_Fep, N_CO\n        )\n\n        return T_eq, Gamma, Lambda, heat_comp, cool_comp\n\n    def _bisection(self, func, a, b, max_iter=100):\n        \"\"\"Simple bisection fallback.\"\"\"\n        for _ in range(max_iter):\n            mid = (a + b) / 2.0\n            if func(mid) * func(a) < 0:\n                b = mid\n            else:\n                a = mid\n            if (b - a) / a < self.tol:\n                break\n        return (a + b) / 2.0\n\n    def compute_dust_temperature(self, G0, A_V, n_H=None, T_gas=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Estimate dust temperature from radiative equilibrium.\n\n        Simple approximation: T_dust \u221d G0^(1/4+\u03b4) * exp(-A_V/4)\n\n        For a more accurate calculation, would need full dust SED\n        fitting with grain size distribution.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        A_V : float\n            Visual extinction [mag]\n        n_H : float, optional\n            Density for gas-grain coupling correction\n        T_gas : float, optional\n            Gas temperature for gas-grain coupling\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import sigma_sb\n\n        # Dust absorbs FUV and reradiates in IR\n        # Simple equilibrium: sigma_abs * F_UV = sigma_SB * T_d^(4+beta)\n        # where beta ~ 1.5-2 for grain emissivity index\n\n        # Effective UV flux\n        F_UV = 1.6e-3 * G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * A_V)  # erg/cm\u00b2/s\n\n        # Add ISRF heating floor (CMB + ISRF)\n        F_UV += 1e-5  # erg/cm\u00b2/s minimum\n\n        # T_dust from balance\n        # Assuming Q_abs(UV)/Q_abs(IR) ~ 1000 and beta=2:\n        # T_d = (Q_UV/Q_IR * F_UV / sigma_SB)^(1/6)\n        Q_ratio = 1000.0\n        T_dust = (Q_ratio * F_UV / sigma_sb) ** (1.0 / 6.0)\n\n        # Floor: CMB temperature\n        T_dust = max(T_dust, 2.725)\n\n        # Gas-grain coupling correction at high density\n        if n_H is not None and T_gas is not None and n_H > 1e5:\n            # At high density, dust approaches gas temperature\n            f_couple = min(1.0, n_H / 1e7)\n            T_dust = T_dust * (1 - f_couple) + T_gas * f_couple\n\n        return T_dust\n",

      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nCosmic ray attenuation module for PRISM-3D.\n\nImplements column-density dependent cosmic ray ionization rate\nfollowing Padovani+ (2009, 2018). This breaks the standard assumption\nof a uniform CR ionization rate throughout the cloud.\n\nThree models are available:\n- Model L: Low CR flux (lower bound from Voyager measurements)\n- Model H: High CR flux (fits diffuse cloud observations)\n- Model M: Medium (geometric mean, recommended default)\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import AV_per_NH\n\n\ndef cr_ionization_rate(N_H, model='M', zeta_0=2e-16):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute the CR ionization rate as a function of column density.\n\n    The CR spectrum is attenuated as it penetrates into the cloud.\n    Low-energy CRs (which dominate ionization) are stopped first.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    N_H : float or array\n        Total H column density [cm\u207b\u00b2]\n    model : str\n        CR spectrum model: 'L' (low), 'M' (medium), 'H' (high)\n    zeta_0 : float\n        Normalization: CR ionization rate at N_H = 0 [s\u207b\u00b9]\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    zeta : float or array\n        CR ionization rate [s\u207b\u00b9]\n    \"\"\"\n    N_H = np.asarray(N_H, dtype=np.float64)\n\n    if model == 'L':\n        # Padovani+ (2018) Model L\n        # Low CR flux, appropriate for shielded environments\n        zeta = _padovani_model_L(N_H) * (zeta_0 / 2e-16)\n\n    elif model == 'H':\n        # Padovani+ (2018) Model H\n        # High CR flux, appropriate for diffuse clouds\n        zeta = _padovani_model_H(N_H) * (zeta_0 / 2e-16)\n\n    elif model == 'M':\n        # Geometric mean of L and H\n        zeta_L = _padovani_model_L(N_H)\n        zeta_H = _padovani_model_H(N_H)\n        zeta = np.sqrt(zeta_L * zeta_H) * (zeta_0 / 2e-16)\n\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unknown CR model: {model}\")\n\n    return np.maximum(zeta, 1e-22)  # Floor\n\n\ndef _padovani_model_L(N_H):\n    \"\"\"\n    Padovani+ (2018) Model L: Low CR ionization rate.\n\n    Fit: log10(zeta) = a0 + a1*x + a2*x^2 + ...\n    where x = log10(N_H / 10^20 cm\u207b\u00b2)\n    \"\"\"\n    N_H = np.maximum(N_H, 1e18)\n    x = np.log10(N_H / 1e20)\n\n    # Fit coefficients from Padovani+ (2018) Table 3\n    # These are approximate representative values\n    log_zeta = np.where(\n        x < -1.0,\n        -15.7,                          # Unattenuated\n        np.where(\n            x < 2.0,\n            -15.7 - 0.4 * x - 0.1 * x**2,  # Moderate attenuation\n            np.where(\n                x < 5.0,\n                -16.3 - 0.8 * (x - 2.0),     # Strong attenuation\n                -18.7                           # Deep, only high-E CRs\n            )\n        )\n    )\n\n    return 10**log_zeta\n\n\ndef _padovani_model_H(N_H):\n    \"\"\"\n    Padovani+ (2018) Model H: High CR ionization rate.\n    \"\"\"\n    N_H = np.maximum(N_H, 1e18)\n    x = np.log10(N_H / 1e20)\n\n    log_zeta = np.where(\n        x < -1.0,\n        -15.0,\n        np.where(\n            x < 2.0,\n            -15.0 - 0.35 * x - 0.08 * x**2,\n            np.where(\n                x < 5.0,\n                -15.6 - 0.7 * (x - 2.0),\n                -17.7\n            )\n        )\n    )\n\n    return 10**log_zeta\n\n\ndef cr_attenuation_multiray(grid, model='M', zeta_0=2e-16):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute CR ionization rate at each cell using column densities\n    from multiple ray directions.\n\n    For each cell, the effective zeta_CR is the mean of the\n    attenuation along all ray directions (since CRs are approximately\n    isotropic).\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    grid : OctreeGrid\n        The computational grid (cells must have N_H_total set)\n    model : str\n        CR attenuation model\n    zeta_0 : float\n        Surface CR rate [s\u207b\u00b9]\n    \"\"\"\n    leaves = grid.get_leaves()\n\n    for leaf in leaves:\n        # Use the column density already computed by the RT module\n        N_H = leaf.data.N_H_total\n\n        # Compute attenuated rate\n        leaf.data.zeta_CR = cr_ionization_rate(N_H, model=model, zeta_0=zeta_0)\n\n\ndef cr_heating_rate(n_H, x_H2, x_HI, zeta_CR):\n    \"\"\"\n    Compute CR heating rate.\n\n    Convenience function - same as in thermal/heating.py but\n    available here for standalone use.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma_CR : float\n        CR heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    from ..utils.constants import eV_to_erg\n\n    E_heat = 20.0 * eV_to_erg  # Energy deposited per ionization\n    n_HI = x_HI * n_H\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    return zeta_CR * (n_HI + 2.0 * n_H2) * E_heat\n",

      "description": "Grain size distribution evolution and photoelectric heating",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nGrain physics module for PRISM-3D.\n\nTracks the grain size distribution and computes:\n1. Photoelectric heating efficiency from the actual grain population\n2. H2 formation rate on grain surfaces\n3. Grain charge distribution (for electron recombination on grains)\n4. Simple grain evolution (shattering, coagulation)\n\nThe grain size distribution is discretized into bins spanning\nfrom PAH-sized (3.5 \u00c5) to large grains (1 \u03bcm).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import k_boltz, m_H, eV_to_erg, e_charge\n\n\nclass GrainSizeDistribution:\n    \"\"\"\n    Discretized grain size distribution.\n\n    Uses the MRN (Mathis-Rumpl-Nordsieck 1977) distribution as\n    default: dn/da \u221d a^(-3.5) for a_min < a < a_max.\n\n    Optionally includes a PAH component following\n    Weingartner & Draine (2001).\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_bins : int\n        Number of size bins\n    a_min : float\n        Minimum grain size [cm]\n    a_max : float\n        Maximum grain size [cm]\n    composition : str\n        'silicate', 'carbonaceous', or 'mixed'\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, n_bins=20, a_min=3.5e-8, a_max=2.5e-5,\n                 composition='mixed'):\n        self.n_bins = n_bins\n        self.a_min = a_min\n        self.a_max = a_max\n        self.composition = composition\n\n        # Log-spaced bin edges\n        self.a_edges = np.logspace(np.log10(a_min), np.log10(a_max), n_bins + 1)\n        self.a_centers = np.sqrt(self.a_edges[:-1] * self.a_edges[1:])\n        self.da = self.a_edges[1:] - self.a_edges[:-1]\n\n        # Initialize MRN distribution\n        self.dn_da = self._mrn_distribution()\n\n        # Grain material properties\n        if composition == 'silicate':\n            self.rho_grain = 3.5  # g/cm\u00b3\n        elif composition == 'carbonaceous':\n            self.rho_grain = 2.24  # g/cm\u00b3\n        else:\n            self.rho_grain = 3.0  # mixed\n\n        # PAH fraction (fraction of C in PAHs)\n        self.q_PAH = 0.046  # WD01 value for Milky Way\n\n    def _mrn_distribution(self):\n        \"\"\"Standard MRN power-law distribution.\"\"\"\n        # dn/da = C * n_H * a^(-3.5)\n        # Normalization from dust-to-gas ratio\n        # For standard ISM: integral gives tau_V / N_H ~ 5.3e-22\n        C_mrn = 1e-25.13  # normalization constant [cm^(2.5) per H]\n        return C_mrn * self.a_centers**(-3.5)\n\n    def total_cross_section(self, per_H=True):\n        \"\"\"\n        Total geometric cross section of grains.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        per_H : bool\n            If True, return per H nucleus [cm\u00b2/H]\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        sigma : float\n            Total grain cross section\n        \"\"\"\n        sigma = np.sum(np.pi * self.a_centers**2 * self.dn_da * self.da)\n        return sigma\n\n    def total_surface_area(self, per_H=True):\n        \"\"\"Total grain surface area per H nucleus [cm\u00b2/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(4.0 * np.pi * self.a_centers**2 * self.dn_da * self.da)\n\n    def dust_to_gas_ratio(self):\n        \"\"\"Compute the dust-to-gas mass ratio.\"\"\"\n        mass_per_H = np.sum(\n            (4.0/3.0) * np.pi * self.a_centers**3 * self.rho_grain\n            * self.dn_da * self.da\n        )\n        return mass_per_H / m_H\n\n\nclass GrainPhysics:\n    \"\"\"\n    Grain physics calculations for a PDR cell.\n\n    Computes PE heating, H2 formation, and grain charging\n    from the local grain size distribution and conditions.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, grain_dist=None):\n        if grain_dist is None:\n            grain_dist = GrainSizeDistribution()\n        self.grain_dist = grain_dist\n\n    def photoelectric_heating_from_distribution(self, n_H, T, G0, x_e):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute PE heating from the actual grain size distribution.\n\n        This is more accurate than the Bakes & Tielens (1994) fit\n        because it accounts for the actual grain population rather\n        than an assumed average.\n\n        Uses the Weingartner & Draine (2001) grain charging model.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        Gamma_PE : float\n            PE heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n        \"\"\"\n        if G0 <= 0:\n            return 0.0\n\n        n_e = max(x_e * n_H, 1e-10 * n_H)\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        Gamma_total = 0.0\n\n        for i in range(gd.n_bins):\n            a = gd.a_centers[i]\n            dn_da_i = gd.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = gd.da[i]\n\n            # Number density of grains in this bin\n            n_grain_i = dn_da_i * da_i * n_H\n\n            # Grain charging parameter for this size\n            # tau = a * k_B * T / e^2 (reduced temperature)\n            tau = a * k_boltz * T / e_charge**2\n\n            # Mean charge\n            # Simplified: Z ~ G0 * a / (n_e * sqrt(T))\n            psi_i = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e * (a / 1e-5)\n            Z_mean = psi_i / (1.0 + psi_i / 3.0)  # Very approximate\n\n            # PE yield for this grain\n            # Y(a, Z) from WD01 - depends on grain size and charge\n            # Simplified: Y ~ 0.1 for neutral small grains, decreasing with charge\n            Y_pe = 0.1 * np.exp(-Z_mean / 3.0) * min(1.0, a / 5e-8)\n\n            # UV absorption cross section\n            Q_abs_UV = min(1.0, 2.0 * np.pi * a / 1e-5)  # efficiency factor\n            sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * Q_abs_UV\n\n            # FUV photon rate hitting this grain\n            F_UV = 1.6e-3 * G0  # erg/cm\u00b2/s\n            E_photon = 10.0 * eV_to_erg  # mean FUV photon energy ~10 eV\n            phi_UV = F_UV / E_photon  # photons/cm\u00b2/s\n\n            # Mean PE electron energy\n            # E_pe ~ h*nu - W - Z*e^2/a\n            # W ~ 4-8 eV (work function)\n            W = 6.0 * eV_to_erg  # Work function\n            E_pe = max(E_photon - W - Z_mean * e_charge**2 / max(a, 1e-8), 0)\n\n            # Heating rate from this grain size\n            Gamma_i = n_grain_i * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y_pe * E_pe\n\n            Gamma_total += Gamma_i\n\n        return Gamma_total\n\n    def h2_formation_rate(self, T, T_dust, a_index=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute H2 formation rate coefficient on grain surfaces.\n\n        Uses the Cazaux & Tielens (2004) model which accounts for\n        grain temperature, sticking coefficient, and surface mobility.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        T_dust : float\n            Dust temperature [K]\n        a_index : int, optional\n            If given, compute for a specific grain size bin.\n            Otherwise, sum over all bins.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        R_H2 : float\n            H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n            (rate = R_H2 * n(H) * n_H)\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        R_total = 0.0\n\n        # Thermal speed of H atoms\n        v_H = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_H))\n\n        # Sticking coefficient (Hollenbach & McKee 1979)\n        S_H = 1.0 / (1.0 + 0.04 * np.sqrt(T + T_dust)\n                     + 0.002 * T + 8e-6 * T**2)\n\n        if a_index is not None:\n            bins = [a_index]\n        else:\n            bins = range(gd.n_bins)\n\n        for i in bins:\n            a = gd.a_centers[i]\n            dn_da_i = gd.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = gd.da[i]\n\n            # Cross section\n            sigma = np.pi * a**2\n\n            # Recombination efficiency on surface\n            # Depends on T_dust: efficient at low T, drops above ~20 K\n            # Cazaux & Tielens (2004) formula\n            if T_dust < 10:\n                epsilon = 1.0\n            elif T_dust < 20:\n                epsilon = 1.0 - 0.05 * (T_dust - 10)\n            elif T_dust < 100:\n                epsilon = 0.5 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 20) / 30.0)\n            else:\n                # Chemisorption allows H2 formation even at high T_dust\n                epsilon = 0.1 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 100) / 200.0)\n\n            # Contribution from this bin\n            R_i = 0.5 * v_H * S_H * epsilon * sigma * dn_da_i * da_i\n\n            R_total += R_i\n\n        return R_total\n\n    def grain_recombination_rate(self, T, x_e, ion_species='C+'):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute grain-assisted recombination rate for an ion species.\n\n        Important for C+ recombination in PDR surfaces where\n        grain recombination can exceed radiative recombination.\n\n        Following Weingartner & Draine (2001).\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n        ion_species : str\n            Ion species name\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        k_gr : float\n            Grain recombination rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        # Simplified WD01 grain recombination\n        # k_gr ~ sum over sizes: pi*a^2 * v_ion * S * f(charge)\n\n        # Ion mass (approximate)\n        ion_masses = {'C+': 12, 'S+': 32, 'Si+': 28, 'Fe+': 56, 'H+': 1}\n        m_ion = ion_masses.get(ion_species, 12) * m_H\n\n        v_ion = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_ion))\n\n        k_total = 0.0\n        for i in range(gd.n_bins):\n            a = gd.a_centers[i]\n            sigma = np.pi * a**2\n            dn_da_i = gd.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = gd.da[i]\n\n            # Sticking probability for ions (enhanced by Coulomb focusing\n            # for negatively charged grains)\n            S_ion = min(1.0, 1.0 + e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T))\n\n            k_i = v_ion * S_ion * sigma * dn_da_i * da_i\n            k_total += k_i\n\n        return k_total\n\n    def evolve_distribution(self, n_H, T, v_turb, dt):\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve the grain size distribution due to shattering and coagulation.\n\n        Simple implementation following Hirashita & Yan (2009):\n        - Shattering by grain-grain collisions in turbulence\n        - Coagulation in dense regions\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        v_turb : float\n            Turbulent velocity [cm/s]\n        dt : float\n            Time step [s]\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        # Shattering: moves mass from large to small grains\n        # Rate \u221d n_grain\u00b2 * v_turb * sigma\n        # Only significant in strong shocks or high turbulence\n\n        # Coagulation: moves mass from small to large grains\n        # Rate \u221d n_grain\u00b2 * v_thermal * sigma * sticking\n        # Only significant at high density (n > 10^5)\n\n        # For now, simple relaxation toward MRN\n        tau_relax = 1e14 / (n_H / 1e3)  # ~3 Myr at n=1e3\n\n        if dt < tau_relax * 0.01:\n            return  # No significant evolution\n\n        mrn = gd._mrn_distribution()\n        f = min(dt / tau_relax, 1.0)\n        gd.dn_da = gd.dn_da * (1.0 - f) + mrn * f\n",

        # Simplified: Y ~ 0.1 for neutral small grains, decreasing with charge\n            Y_pe = 0.1 * np.exp(-Z_mean / 3.0) * min(1.0, a / 5e-8)\n\n            # UV absorption cross section\n            Q_abs_UV = min(1.0, 2.0 * np.pi * a / 1e-5)  # efficiency factor\n            sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * Q_abs_UV\n\n            # FUV photon rate hitting this grain\n            F_UV = 1.6e-3 *    def h2_formation_rate(self, T, T_dust, a_index=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute H2 formation rate coefficient on grain surfaces.\n\n        Uses the Cazaux & Tielens (2004) model which accounts for\n        grain temperature, sticking coefficient, and surface mobility.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        T_dust :      -------\n        R_H2 : float\n            H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n            (rate = R_H2 * n(H) * n_H)\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        R_total = epsilon = 1.0\n            elif T_dust < 20:\n                epsilon = 1.0 - 0.05 * (T_dust - 10)\n            elif T_dust < 100:\n                epsilon = 0.5 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 20) / 30.0)\n            else:\n                # Chemisorption allows H2 formation even at high T_dust\n                epsilon = 0.1 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 100) / 200.0)\n\n            # Contribution from this bin\n \"\"\"\n        Compute grain-assisted recombination rate for an ion species.\n\n        Important for C+ recombination in PDR surfaces where\n        grain recombination can exceed radiative recombination.\n\n        Following Weingartner & Draine (2001).\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n        ion_species : str\n            Ion species name\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        k_gr : float\n            Grain recombination rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        # Simplified WD01 grain recombination\n        # k_gr ~ sum over sizes: pi*a^2 * v_ion * S * f(charge)\n\n        # Ion mass (approximate)\n in dense regions\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        v_turb :
