Uses scipy's BDF solver for stiff systems.\n\nFeatures:\n- Steady-state solver (iterate to chemical equilibrium)\n- Time-dependent solver (evolve for dt)\n- Conservation law enforcement (H, C, O, charge)\n- Abundance floor to prevent negative values\n\nv0.2 fixes:\n- Added O2 to tracked species\n- Extended integration time to 1e16 s for CO formation\n- Better initial conditions at depth (metal depletion)\n- Smarter conservation: only rescale major carriers\n- Improved convergence checking on key species\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.integrate import solve_ivp\nfrom .network import ChemicalNetwork, build_pdr_network\n\n\n# Minimum abundance floor\nX_FLOOR = 1e-30\n\n\nclass ChemistrySolver:\n    \"\"\"\n    Solve the chemical rate equations for a single cell.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    network : ChemicalNetwork, optional\n        Chemical network to use. Default builds standard PDR network.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, network=None):\n        if network is None:\n            network = ChemicalNetwork(build_pdr_network())\n        self.network = network\n\n        # Map between the full species list and our tracked species\n        # v0.2: Added O2\n        self.tracked_species = [\n            'e-', 'H', 'H2', 'H+', 'H-', 'H2+', 'H3+',\n            'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'CH', 'CH+', 'CH2+',\n            'O', 'O+', 'OH', 'OH+', 'H2O', 'H2O+', 'H3O+',\n            'O2',\n            'CO+', 'HCO+',\n            'S', 'S+', 'Si', 'Si+', 'Fe', 'Fe+',\n            'He', 'He+',\n        ]\n        self.n_tracked = len(self.tracked_species)\n        self.idx = {s: i for i, s in enumerate(self.tracked_species)}\n\n    def _abundances_to_dict(self, x_arr):\n        \"\"\"Convert array to abundance dict.\"\"\"\n        return {self.tracked_species[i]: max(x_arr[i], X_FLOOR)\n                for i in range(self.n_tracked)}\n\n    def _dict_to_array(self, x_dict):\n        \"\"\"Convert abundance dict to array.\"\"\"\n        return np.array([x_dict.get(s, X_FLOOR) for s in self.tracked_species])\n\n    def _rhs(self, t, y, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO):\n        \"\"\"\n        Right-hand side of the ODE system dy/dt = f(y).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enforce positivity\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n\n        abundances = self._abundances_to_dict(y)\n\n        # Add PAH abundances (assumed constant)\n        # PAH abundance ~ 1e-7 relative to H (Tielens 2005)\n        x_PAH_total = 1e-7\n\n        # PAH charge balance: depends on G0/n_e ratio\n        n_e = max(abundances.get('e-', 1e-10), 1e-10) * n_H\n        if G0 > 0 and T > 0 and n_e > 0:\n            # Bakes & Tielens (1994) / Wolfire+ (2003) charging\n            psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n            # At low psi (shielded/dense): PAH mostly neutral/negative\n            # At high psi (surface): PAH mostly neutral/positive\n            f_plus = psi / (psi + 500.0)\n            f_minus = 500.0 / (psi + 500.0) * 0.5\n            f_neutral = 1.0 - f_plus - f_minus\n            f_neutral = max(f_neutral, 0.01)\n        else:\n            f_neutral, f_plus, f_minus = 0.5, 0.1, 0.4\n\n        abundances['PAH'] = x_PAH_total * f_neutral\n        abundances['PAH+'] = x_PAH_total * f_plus\n        abundances['PAH-'] = x_PAH_total * f_minus\n\n        # Compute rates\n        dndt = self.network.compute_rates(\n            abundances, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n            f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO\n        )\n\n        # Convert to array\n        dydt = np.zeros(self.n_tracked)\n        for i, sp in enumerate(self.tracked_species):\n            dydt[i] = dndt.get(sp, 0.0)\n\n        return dydt\n\n    def solve_steady_state(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                            x_init=None, f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0,\n                            t_max=3e16, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-20):\n        \"\"\"\n        Solve for chemical steady state.\n\n        v0.2: Extended t_max to 3e16 s (~1 Myr) to allow CO to form.\n        Improved convergence checking to focus on key PDR species.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        # Default initial conditions\n        if x_init is None:\n            x_init = self._default_initial(G0, A_V)\n\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n\n        # Integrate in log-spaced time steps\n        # Start from earlier time to capture fast reactions\n        t_checkpoints = np.logspace(4, np.log10(t_max), 30)\n\n        y_current = y0.copy()\n        converged = False\n\n        for i in range(len(t_checkpoints) - 1):\n            t_span = (t_checkpoints[i], t_checkpoints[i+1])\n\n            try:\n                sol = solve_ivp(\n                    self._rhs, t_span, y_current,\n                    method='BDF',\n                    args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n                    rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n                    max_step=(t_span[1] - t_span[0]),\n                    dense_output=False\n                )\n\n                if sol.success:\n                    y_new = sol.y[:, -1]\n                    y_new = np.maximum(y_new, X_FLOOR)\n\n                    # Check convergence on KEY species only\n                    # (H, H2, C+, C, CO, O, e- are the important ones)\n                    key_idx = [self.idx[s] for s in\n                              ['H', 'H2', 'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'O', 'e-']\n                              if s in self.idx]\n                    y_key_old = y_current[key_idx]\n                    y_key_new = y_new[key_idx]\n\n                    mask = y_key_old > 1e-12\n                    if np.any(mask):\n                        rel_change = np.max(\n                            np.abs(y_key_new[mask] - y_key_old[mask]) /\n                            np.maximum(y_key_old[mask], 1e-20)\n                        )\n                        if rel_change < rtol and i > 5:\n                            converged = True\n                            y_current = y_new\n                            break\n\n                    y_current = y_new\n                else:\n                    # Solver failed at this interval - continue from last good point\n                    pass\n\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n        # Enforce conservation (gentle version)\n        y_current = self._enforce_conservation(y_current, n_H)\n\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_current), converged\n\n    def solve_time_dependent(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                              x_init, dt,\n                              f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0):\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve chemistry for a time step dt.\n        \"\"\"\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n\n        sol = solve_ivp(\n            self._rhs, (0, dt), y0,\n            method='BDF',\n            args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n            rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n        )\n\n        if sol.success:\n            y_final = np.maximum(sol.y[:, -1], X_FLOOR)\n        else:\n            y_final = y0  # Fall back to initial if solver fails\n\n        y_final = self._enforce_conservation(y_final, n_H)\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_final)\n\n    def _default_initial(self, G0, A_V):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set reasonable initial abundances based on conditions.\n\n        v0.2: Better initial conditions at depth:\n        - Metal ions depleted at high A_V (recombined onto grains)\n        - CO initialized higher at depth\n        - Electron fraction set by actual ion balance\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        x = {}\n        x_C_total = solar_abundances['C']    # 2.69e-4\n        x_O_total = solar_abundances['O']    # 4.90e-4\n        x_S_total = solar_abundances.get('S', 1.32e-5)\n        x_Si_total = solar_abundances.get('Si', 3.24e-5)\n        x_Fe_total = solar_abundances.get('Fe', 3.16e-5)\n        x_He = solar_abundances['He']\n\n        # Metal depletion factor at depth (grains lock up metals)\n        # Gas-phase metals are ~10-100x lower than solar in dense clouds\n        f_deplete = 0.01 if A_V > 3 else (0.01 + 0.99 * (3.0 - A_V) / 3.0 if A_V > 0 else 1.0)\n\n        if A_V < 0.5:\n            # Atomic surface: all H atomic, all C ionized\n            x['H'] = 1.0\n            x['H2'] = 1e-6\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total\n            x['C'] = 1e-10\n            x['CO'] = 1e-15\n            x['O'] = x_O_total\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_deplete\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_deplete\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_deplete\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n        elif A_V < 2.0:\n            # H/H2 transition zone, C still mostly C+\n            fmol = (A_V - 0.5) / 1.5  # 0\u21921 over A_V = 0.5\u21922\n            x['H'] = 1.0 - fmol\n            x['H2'] = fmol / 2.0\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - 0.3 * fmol)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.2 * fmol\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.1 * fmol\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_deplete\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_deplete\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_deplete\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n        elif A_V < 5.0:\n            # C+/C/CO transition\n            fCO = (A_V - 2.0) / 3.0  # CO fraction increases 2\u21925\n            x['H'] = 0.01\n            x['H2'] = 0.495\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * max(0.01, 1.0 - fCO * 3)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - fCO) * 0.5\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * fCO * 0.9\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_deplete * 0.5\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_deplete * 0.5)\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_deplete * 0.1\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_deplete * 0.1)\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_deplete * 0.1\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_deplete * 0.1)\n        else:\n            # Deep molecular: CO is dominant C carrier\n            x['H'] = 0.005\n            x['H2'] = 0.4975\n            x['C+'] = 1e-9\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.05\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.95\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n            # Metals depleted onto grains at depth\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * 0.001\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * 0.999\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * 0.001\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * 0.999\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * 0.001\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * 0.999\n\n        # Compute electron fraction from actual ions\n        x['e-'] = (x.get('C+', 0) + x.get('S+', 0) + x.get('Si+', 0)\n                   + x.get('Fe+', 0) + 1e-8)  # 1e-8 floor from CR ionization\n\n        # Minor / intermediate species\n        x['H+'] = 1e-8\n        x['H-'] = 1e-14\n        x['H2+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H3+'] = 5e-10 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['OH'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 2 else 1e-9\n        x['OH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H2O'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 3 else 1e-9\n        x['H2O+'] = 1e-14\n        x['H3O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['O2'] = 1e-8 if A_V > 3 else 1e-12\n        x['CH'] = 1e-10\n        x['CH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['CH2+'] = 1e-14\n        x['CO+'] = 1e-15\n        x['HCO+'] = 1e-9 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['He'] = x_He\n        x['He+'] = 1e-13\n\n        return x\n\n    def _enforce_conservation(self, y, n_H):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enforce elemental conservation laws.\n\n        v0.2: Gentler conservation \u2014 only rescale the 2-3 dominant\n        carriers of each element, and don't touch trace species that\n        the chemistry is still evolving. This prevents the conservation\n        step from destroying the CO the solver has built up.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        idx = self.idx\n\n        # ---- Hydrogen ----\n        # Count total H nuclei\n        H_total_target = 1.0\n        H_carriers = ['H', 'H2']\n        H_in_major = y[idx['H']] + 2 * y[idx['H2']]\n\n        if H_in_major > 0:\n            # Only rescale H and H2 (they hold >99% of H)\n            # Leave OH, H2O, etc. alone\n            H_minor = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['H-']]\n                      + 2*y[idx['H2+']] + 3*y[idx['H3+']]\n                      + y[idx['OH']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                      + 2*y[idx['H2O']] + 2*y[idx['H2O+']]\n                      + 3*y[idx['H3O+']]\n                      + y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']]\n                      + y[idx['HCO+']])\n            H_target_major = max(H_total_target - H_minor, 0.9)\n            scale = H_target_major / H_in_major\n            y[idx['H']] *= scale\n            y[idx['H2']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Carbon ----\n        C_total = solar_abundances['C']\n        # Only rescale the top 3 C carriers: C+, C, CO\n        C_major = y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['C']] + y[idx['CO']]\n        C_minor = (y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                  + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']])\n\n        if C_major > 0:\n            C_target_major = max(C_total - C_minor, C_total * 0.9)\n            scale = C_target_major / C_major\n            # Apply scale gently \u2014 don't let it change things by more than 2x\n            scale = np.clip(scale, 0.5, 2.0)\n            y[idx['C+']] *= scale\n            y[idx['C']] *= scale\n            y[idx['CO']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Oxygen ----\n        O_total = solar_abundances['O']\n        O_current = (y[idx['O']] + y[idx['CO']] + y[idx['OH']]\n                    + y[idx['H2O']] + y[idx['O+']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                    + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']] + y[idx['CO+']]\n                    + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if 'O2' in idx:\n            O_current += 2 * y[idx['O2']]\n\n        if O_current > 0:\n            # Adjust O (the dominant O carrier) to conserve\n            O_without_atomic = O_current - y[idx['O']]\n            y[idx['O']] = max(O_total - O_without_atomic, X_FLOOR)\n\n        # ---- Charge conservation ----\n        n_positive = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['S+']]\n                     + y[idx['Si+']] + y[idx['Fe+']] + y[idx['O+']]\n                     + y[idx['H2+']] + y[idx['H3+']]\n                     + y[idx['OH+']] + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']]\n                     + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                     + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']]\n                     + y[idx['He+']])\n        y[idx['e-']] = max(n_positive, X_FLOOR)\n\n        # Enforce positivity\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n\n        return y\n",

      "code": "\"\"\"\nChemical ODE solver for PRISM-3D.\n\nIntegrates the chemical rate equations to steady state or over a\nspecified time interval. Uses scipy's BDF solver for stiff systems.\n\nFeatures:\n- Steady-state solver (iterate to chemical equilibrium)\n- Time-dependent solver (evolve for dt)\n- Conservation law enforcement (H, C, O, charge)\n- Abundance floor to prevent negative values\n\nv0.2 fixes:\n- Added O2 to tracked species\n- Extended integration time to 1e16 s for CO formation\n- Better initial conditions at depth (metal depletion)\n- Smarter conservation: only rescale major carriers\n- Improved convergence checking on key species\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.integrate import solve_ivp\nfrom .network import ChemicalNetwork, build_pdr_network\n\n\n# Minimum abundance floor\nX_FLOOR = 1e-30\n\n\nclass ChemistrySolver:\n    \"\"\"\n    Solve the chemical rate equations for a single cell.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    network : ChemicalNetwork, optional\n        Chemical network to use. Default builds standard PDR network.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, network=None):\n        if network is None:\n            network = ChemicalNetwork(build_pdr_network())\n        self.network = network\n\n        # Map between the full species list and our tracked species\n        # v0.2: Added O2\n        self.tracked_species = [\n            'e-', 'H', 'H2', 'H+', 'H-', 'H2+', 'H3+',\n            'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'CH', 'CH+', 'CH2+',\n            'O', 'O+', 'OH', 'OH+', 'H2O', 'H2O+', 'H3O+',\n            'O2',\n            'CO+', 'HCO+',\n            'S', 'S+', 'Si', 'Si+', 'Fe', 'Fe+',\n            'He', 'He+',\n        ]\n        self.n_tracked = len(self.tracked_species)\n        self.idx = {s: i for i, s in enumerate(self.tracked_species)}\n\n    def _abundances_to_dict(self, x_arr):\n        \"\"\"Convert array to abundance dict.\"\"\"\n        return {self.tracked_species[i]: max(x_arr[i], X_FLOOR)\n                for i in range(self.n_tracked)}\n\n    def _dict_to_array(self, x_dict):\n        \"\"\"Convert abundance dict to array.\"\"\"\n        return np.array([x_dict.get(s, X_FLOOR) for s in self.tracked_species])\n\n    def _rhs(self, t, y, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO):\n        \"\"\"\n        Right-hand side of the ODE system dy/dt = f(y).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Enforce positivity\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n\n        abundances = self._abundances_to_dict(y)\n\n        # Add PAH abundances (assumed constant)\n        # PAH abundance ~ 1e-7 relative to H (Tielens 2005)\n        x_PAH_total = 1e-7\n\n        # PAH charge balance: depends on G0/n_e ratio\n        n_e = max(abundances.get('e-', 1e-10), 1e-10) * n_H\n        if G0 > 0 and T > 0 and n_e > 0:\n            # Bakes & Tielens (1994) / Wolfire+ (2003) charging\n            psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n            # At low psi (shielded/dense): PAH mostly neutral/negative\n            # At high psi (surface): PAH mostly neutral/positive\n            f_plus = psi / (psi + 500.0)\n            f_minus = 500.0 / (psi + 500.0) * 0.5\n            f_neutral = 1.0 - f_plus - f_minus\n            f_neutral = max(f_neutral, 0.01)\n        else:\n            f_neutral, f_plus, f_minus = 0.5, 0.1, 0.4\n\n        abundances['PAH'] = x_PAH_total * f_neutral\n        abundances['PAH+'] = x_PAH_total * f_plus\n        abundances['PAH-'] = x_PAH_total * f_minus\n\n        # Compute rates\n        dndt = self.network.compute_rates(\n            abundances, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n            f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO\n        )\n\n        # Convert to array\n        dydt = np.zeros(self.n_tracked)\n        for i, sp in enumerate(self.tracked_species):\n            dydt[i] = dndt.get(sp, 0.0)\n\n        return dydt\n\n    def solve_steady_state(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                            x_init=None, f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0,\n                            t_max=3e16, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-20):\n        \"\"\"\n        Solve for chemical steady state.\n\n        v0.2: Extended t_max to 3e16 s (~1 Myr) to allow CO to form.\n        Improved convergence checking to focus on key PDR species.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        # Default initial conditions\n        if x_init is None:\n            x_init = self._default_initial(G0, A_V)\n\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n\n        # Integrate in log-spaced time steps\n        # Start from earlier time to capture fast reactions\n        t_checkpoints = np.logspace(4, np.log10(t_max), 30)\n\n        y_current = y0.copy()\n        converged = False\n\n        for i in range(len(t_checkpoints) - 1):\n            t_span = (t_checkpoints[i], t_checkpoints[i+1])\n\n            try:\n                sol = solve_ivp(\n                    self._rhs, t_span, y_current,\n                    method='BDF',\n                    args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n                    rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n                    max_step=(t_span[1] - t_span[0]),\n                    dense_output=False\n                )\n\n                if sol.success:\n                    y_new = sol.y[:, -1]\n                    y_new = np.maximum(y_new, X_FLOOR)\n\n                    # Check convergence on KEY species only\n                    # (H, H2, C+, C, CO, O, e- are the important ones)\n                    key_idx = [self.idx[s] for s in\n                              ['H', 'H2', 'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'O', 'e-']\n                              if s in self.idx]\n                    y_key_old = y_current[key_idx]\n                    y_key_new = y_new[key_idx]\n\n                    mask = y_key_old > 1e-12\n                    if np.any(mask):\n                        rel_change = np.max(\n                            np.abs(y_key_new[mask] - y_key_old[mask]) /\n                            np.maximum(y_key_old[mask], 1e-20)\n                        )\n                        if rel_change < rtol and i > 5:\n                            converged = True\n                            y_current = y_new\n                            break\n\n                    y_current = y_new\n                else:\n                    # Solver failed at this interval - continue from last good point\n                    pass\n\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n        # Enforce conservation (gentle version)\n        y_current = self._enforce_conservation(y_current, n_H)\n\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_current), converged\n\n    def solve_time_dependent(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                              x_init, dt,\n                              f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0):\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve chemistry for a time step dt.\n        \"\"\"\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n\n        sol = solve_ivp(\n            self._rhs, (0, dt), y0,\n            method='BDF',\n            args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n            rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n        )\n\n        if sol.success:\n            y_final = np.maximum(sol.y[:, -1], X_FLOOR)\n        else:\n            y_final = y0  # Fall back to initial if solver fails\n\n        y_final = self._enforce_conservation(y_final, n_H)\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_final)\n\n    def _default_initial(self, G0, A_V):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set reasonable initial abundances based on conditions.\n\n        v0.2: Better initial conditions at depth:\n        - Metal ions depleted at high A_V (recombined onto grains)\n        - CO initialized higher at depth\n        - Electron fraction set by actual ion balance\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        x = {}\n        x_C_total = solar_abundances['C']    # 2.69e-4\n        x_O_total = solar_abundances['O']    # 4.90e-4\n        x_S_total = solar_abundances.get('S', 1.32e-5)\n        x_Si_total = solar_abundances.get('Si', 3.24e-5)\n        x_Fe_total = solar_abundances.get('Fe', 3.16e-5)\n        x_He = solar_abundances['He']\n\n        # Metal depletion factor at depth (grains lock up metals)\n        # Gas-phase metals are ~10-100x lower than solar in dense clouds\n        f_deplete = 0.01 if A_V > 3 else (0.01 + 0.99 * (3.0 - A_V) / 3.0 if A_V > 0 else 1.0)\n\n        if A_V < 0.5:\n            # Atomic surface: all H atomic, all C ionized\n            x['H'] = 1.0\n            x['H2'] = 1e-6\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total\n            x['C'] = 1e-10\n            x['CO'] = 1e-15\n            x['O'] = x_O_total\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_deplete\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_deplete\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_deplete\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n        elif A_V < 2.0:\n            # H/H2 transition zone, C still mostly C+\n            fmol = (A_V - 0.5) / 1.5  # 0\u21921 over A_V = 0.5\u21922\n            x['H'] = 1.0 - fmol\n            x['H2'] = fmol / 2.0\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - 0.3 * fmol)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.2 * fmol\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.1 * fmol\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_deplete\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_deplete\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_deplete\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_deplete)\n        elif A_V < 5.0:\n            # C+/C/CO transition\n            fCO = (A_V - 2.0) / 3.0  # CO fraction increases 2\u21925\n            x['H'] = 0.01\n            x['H2'] = 0.495\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * max(0.01, 1.0 - fCO * 3)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - fCO) * 0.5\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * fCO * 0.9\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_deplete * 0.5\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_deplete * 0.5)\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_deplete * 0.1\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_deplete * 0.1)\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_deplete * 0.1\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_deplete * 0.1)\n        else:\n            # Deep molecular: CO is dominant C carrier\n            x['H'] = 0.005\n            x['H2'] = 0.4975\n            x['C+'] = 1e-9\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.05\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.95\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n            # Metals depleted onto grains at depth\n            x['S+'] = x_S_total * 0.001\n            x['S'] = x_S_total * 0.999\n            x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * 0.001\n            x['Si'] = x_Si_total * 0.999\n            x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * 0.001\n            x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * 0.999\n\n        # Compute electron fraction from actual ions\n        x['e-'] = (x.get('C+', 0) + x.get('S+', 0) + x.get('Si+', 0)\n                   + x.get('Fe+', 0) + 1e-8)  # 1e-8 floor from CR ionization\n\n        # Minor / intermediate species\n        x['H+'] = 1e-8\n        x['H-'] = 1e-14\n        x['H2+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H3+'] = 5e-10 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['OH'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 2 else 1e-9\n        x['OH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H2O'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 3 else 1e-9\n        x['H2O+'] = 1e-14\n        x['H3O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['O2'] = 1e-8 if A_V > 3 else 1e-12\n        x['CH'] = 1e-10\n        x['CH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['CH2+'] = 1e-14\n        x['CO+'] = 1e-15\n        x['HCO+'] = 1e-9 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['He'] = x_He\n        x['He+'] = 1e-13\n\n        return x\n\n    def _enforce_conservation(self, y, n_H):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enforce elemental conservation laws.\n\n        v0.2: Gentler conservation \u2014 only rescale the 2-3 dominant\n        carriers of each element, and don't touch trace species that\n        the chemistry is still evolving. This prevents the conservation\n        step from destroying the CO the solver has built up.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        idx = self.idx\n\n        # ---- Hydrogen ----\n        # Count total H nuclei\n        H_total_target = 1.0\n        H_carriers = ['H', 'H2']\n        H_in_major = y[idx['H']] + 2 * y[idx['H2']]\n\n        if H_in_major > 0:\n            # Only rescale H and H2 (they hold >99% of H)\n            # Leave OH, H2O, etc. alone\n            H_minor = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['H-']]\n                      + 2*y[idx['H2+']] + 3*y[idx['H3+']]\n                      + y[idx['OH']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                      + 2*y[idx['H2O']] + 2*y[idx['H2O+']]\n                      + 3*y[idx['H3O+']]\n                      + y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']]\n                      + y[idx['HCO+']])\n            H_target_major = max(H_total_target - H_minor, 0.9)\n            scale = H_target_major / H_in_major\n            y[idx['H']] *= scale\n            y[idx['H2']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Carbon ----\n        C_total = solar_abundances['C']\n        # Only rescale the top 3 C carriers: C+, C, CO\n        C_major = y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['C']] + y[idx['CO']]\n        C_minor = (y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                  + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']])\n\n        if C_major > 0:\n            C_target_major = max(C_total - C_minor, C_total * 0.9)\n            scale = C_target_major / C_major\n            # Apply scale gently \u2014 don't let it change things by more than 2x\n            scale = np.clip(scale, 0.5, 2.0)\n            y[idx['C+']] *= scale\n            y[idx['C']] *= scale\n            y[idx['CO']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Oxygen ----\n        O_total = solar_abundances['O']\n        O_current = (y[idx['O']] + y[idx['CO']] + y[idx['OH']]\n                    + y[idx['H2O']] + y[idx['O+']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                    + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']] + y[idx['CO+']]\n                    + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if 'O2' in idx:\n            O_current += 2 * y[idx['O2']]\n\n        if O_current > 0:\n            # Adjust O (the dominant O carrier) to conserve\n            O_without_atomic = O_current - y[idx['O']]\n            y[idx['O']] = max(O_total - O_without_atomic, X_FLOOR)\n\n        # ---- Charge conservation ----\n        n_positive = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['S+']]\n                     + y[idx['Si+']] + y[idx['Fe+']] + y[idx['O+']]\n                     + y[idx['H2+']] + y[idx['H3+']]\n                     + y[idx['OH+']] + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']]\n                     + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                     + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']]\n                     + y[idx['He+']])\n        y[idx['e-']] = max(n_positive, X_FLOOR)\n\n        # Enforce positivity\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n\n        return y\n",

      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nChemical ODE solver for PRISM-3D.\n\nv0.2 fixes:\n- Added O2 to tracked species\n- Extended integration time to 3e16 s for CO formation\n- Better initial conditions at depth (metal depletion, CO-rich)\n- Smarter conservation: only rescale major carriers gently\n- Improved convergence checking on key species\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.integrate import solve_ivp\nfrom .network import ChemicalNetwork, build_pdr_network\n\nX_FLOOR = 1e-30\n\n\nclass ChemistrySolver:\n    def __init__(self, network=None):\n        if network is None:\n            network = ChemicalNetwork(build_pdr_network())\n        self.network = network\n\n        self.tracked_species = [\n            'e-', 'H', 'H2', 'H+', 'H-', 'H2+', 'H3+',\n            'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'CH', 'CH+', 'CH2+',\n            'O', 'O+', 'OH', 'OH+', 'H2O', 'H2O+', 'H3O+',\n            'O2',\n            'CO+', 'HCO+',\n            'S', 'S+', 'Si', 'Si+', 'Fe', 'Fe+',\n            'He', 'He+',\n        ]\n        self.n_tracked = len(self.tracked_species)\n        self.idx = {s: i for i, s in enumerate(self.tracked_species)}\n\n    def _abundances_to_dict(self, x_arr):\n        return {self.tracked_species[i]: max(x_arr[i], X_FLOOR)\n                for i in range(self.n_tracked)}\n\n    def _dict_to_array(self, x_dict):\n        return np.array([x_dict.get(s, X_FLOOR) for s in self.tracked_species])\n\n    def _rhs(self, t, y, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO):\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n        abundances = self._abundances_to_dict(y)\n\n        # PAH charge balance\n        x_PAH_total = 1e-7\n        n_e = max(abundances.get('e-', 1e-10), 1e-10) * n_H\n        if G0 > 0 and T > 0 and n_e > 0:\n            psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n            f_plus = psi / (psi + 500.0)\n            f_minus = 500.0 / (psi + 500.0) * 0.5\n            f_neutral = max(1.0 - f_plus - f_minus, 0.01)\n        else:\n            f_neutral, f_plus, f_minus = 0.5, 0.1, 0.4\n\n        abundances['PAH'] = x_PAH_total * f_neutral\n        abundances['PAH+'] = x_PAH_total * f_plus\n        abundances['PAH-'] = x_PAH_total * f_minus\n\n        dndt = self.network.compute_rates(\n            abundances, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO\n        )\n\n        dydt = np.zeros(self.n_tracked)\n        for i, sp in enumerate(self.tracked_species):\n            dydt[i] = dndt.get(sp, 0.0)\n        return dydt\n\n    def solve_steady_state(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                            x_init=None, f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0,\n                            t_max=3e16, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-20):\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        if x_init is None:\n            x_init = self._default_initial(G0, A_V)\n\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n        t_checkpoints = np.logspace(4, np.log10(t_max), 30)\n        y_current = y0.copy()\n        converged = False\n\n        for i in range(len(t_checkpoints) - 1):\n            t_span = (t_checkpoints[i], t_checkpoints[i+1])\n            try:\n                sol = solve_ivp(\n                    self._rhs, t_span, y_current,\n                    method='BDF',\n                    args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n                    rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n                    max_step=(t_span[1] - t_span[0]),\n                )\n                if sol.success:\n                    y_new = np.maximum(sol.y[:, -1], X_FLOOR)\n                    key_idx = [self.idx[s] for s in\n                              ['H', 'H2', 'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'O', 'e-']]\n                    y_key_old = y_current[key_idx]\n                    y_key_new = y_new[key_idx]\n                    mask = y_key_old > 1e-12\n                    if np.any(mask):\n                        rel_change = np.max(\n                            np.abs(y_key_new[mask] - y_key_old[mask]) /\n                            np.maximum(y_key_old[mask], 1e-20)\n                        )\n                        if rel_change < rtol and i > 5:\n                            converged = True\n                            y_current = y_new\n                            break\n                    y_current = y_new\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n        y_current = self._enforce_conservation(y_current, n_H)\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_current), converged\n\n    def solve_time_dependent(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                              x_init, dt,\n                              f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0):\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n        sol = solve_ivp(\n            self._rhs, (0, dt), y0,\n            method='BDF',\n            args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n            rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n        )\n        if sol.success:\n            y_final = np.maximum(sol.y[:, -1], X_FLOOR)\n        else:\n            y_final = y0\n        y_final = self._enforce_conservation(y_final, n_H)\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_final)\n\n    def _default_initial(self, G0, A_V):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set initial abundances. v0.2: metal depletion at depth,\n        CO initialized high at depth, proper x_e from ions.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        x = {}\n        x_C_total = solar_abundances['C']\n        x_O_total = solar_abundances['O']\n        x_S_total = solar_abundances.get('S', 1.32e-5)\n        x_Si_total = solar_abundances.get('Si', 3.24e-5)\n        x_Fe_total = solar_abundances.get('Fe', 3.16e-5)\n        x_He = solar_abundances['He']\n\n        # Gas-phase metal fraction decreases with depth (depletion)\n        f_gas = max(0.01, 1.0 - A_V / 3.0) if A_V < 3.0 else 0.01\n\n        if A_V < 0.5:\n            x['H'] = 1.0\n            x['H2'] = 1e-6\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total\n            x['C'] = 1e-10\n            x['CO'] = 1e-15\n            x['O'] = x_O_total\n        elif A_V < 2.0:\n            fmol = (A_V - 0.5) / 1.5\n            x['H'] = 1.0 - fmol\n            x['H2'] = fmol / 2.0\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - 0.3 * fmol)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.2 * fmol\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.1 * fmol\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n        elif A_V < 5.0:\n            fCO = (A_V - 2.0) / 3.0\n            x['H'] = 0.01\n            x['H2'] = 0.495\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * max(0.01, 1.0 - fCO * 3.0)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - fCO) * 0.3\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * fCO * 0.9\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n        else:\n            x['H'] = 0.005\n            x['H2'] = 0.4975\n            x['C+'] = 1e-9\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.05\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.95\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n\n        # Metals: depleted at depth\n        x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_gas\n        x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_gas)\n        x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_gas\n        x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_gas)\n        x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_gas\n        x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_gas)\n\n        # Electron fraction from ions\n        x['e-'] = (x.get('C+', 0) + x.get('S+', 0) + x.get('Si+', 0)\n                   + x.get('Fe+', 0) + 1e-8)\n\n        # Intermediates\n        x['H+'] = 1e-8\n        x['H-'] = 1e-14\n        x['H2+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H3+'] = 5e-10 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['OH'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 2 else 1e-9\n        x['OH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H2O'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 3 else 1e-9\n        x['H2O+'] = 1e-14\n        x['H3O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['O2'] = 1e-8 if A_V > 3 else 1e-12\n        x['CH'] = 1e-10\n        x['CH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['CH2+'] = 1e-14\n        x['CO+'] = 1e-15\n        x['HCO+'] = 1e-9 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['He'] = x_He\n        x['He+'] = 1e-13\n\n        return x\n\n    def _enforce_conservation(self, y, n_H):\n        \"\"\"\n        Gentle conservation: only rescale dominant carriers, clamp\n        the scale factor to avoid destroying chemistry results.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n        idx = self.idx\n\n        # ---- Hydrogen ----\n        H_major = y[idx['H']] + 2 * y[idx['H2']]\n        H_minor = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['H-']]\n                  + 2*y[idx['H2+']] + 3*y[idx['H3+']]\n                  + y[idx['OH']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                  + 2*y[idx['H2O']] + 2*y[idx['H2O+']]\n                  + 3*y[idx['H3O+']]\n                  + y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']]\n                  + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if H_major > 0:\n            target = max(1.0 - H_minor, 0.9)\n            scale = np.clip(target / H_major, 0.8, 1.2)\n            y[idx['H']] *= scale\n            y[idx['H2']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Carbon ----\n        C_total = solar_abundances['C']\n        C_major = y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['C']] + y[idx['CO']]\n        C_minor = (y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                  + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if C_major > 0:\n            target = max(C_total - C_minor, C_total * 0.9)\n            scale = np.clip(target / C_major, 0.8, 1.2)\n            y[idx['C+']] *= scale\n            y[idx['C']] *= scale\n            y[idx['CO']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Oxygen: adjust atomic O to balance ----\n        O_total = solar_abundances['O']\n        O_in_molecules = (y[idx['CO']] + y[idx['OH']] + y[idx['H2O']]\n                         + y[idx['O+']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                         + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']]\n                         + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if 'O2' in idx:\n            O_in_molecules += 2 * y[idx['O2']]\n        y[idx['O']] = max(O_total - O_in_molecules, X_FLOOR)\n\n        # ---- Charge ----\n        n_pos = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['S+']]\n                + y[idx['Si+']] + y[idx['Fe+']] + y[idx['O+']]\n                + y[idx['H2+']] + y[idx['H3+']]\n                + y[idx['OH+']] + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']]\n                + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']]\n                + y[idx['He+']])\n        y[idx['e-']] = max(n_pos, X_FLOOR)\n\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n        return y\n"

    "message": "Rewritten chemistry solver v0.2 with all fixes",

      "code": "\"\"\"\nChemical ODE solver for PRISM-3D.\n\nv0.2 fixes:\n- Added O2 to tracked species\n- Extended integration time to 3e16 s for CO formation\n- Better initial conditions at depth (metal depletion, CO-rich)\n- Smarter conservation: only rescale major carriers gently\n- Improved convergence checking on key species\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.integrate import solve_ivp\nfrom .network import ChemicalNetwork, build_pdr_network\n\nX_FLOOR = 1e-30\n\n\nclass ChemistrySolver:\n    def __init__(self, network=None):\n        if network is None:\n            network = ChemicalNetwork(build_pdr_network())\n        self.network = network\n\n        self.tracked_species = [\n            'e-', 'H', 'H2', 'H+', 'H-', 'H2+', 'H3+',\n            'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'CH', 'CH+', 'CH2+',\n            'O', 'O+', 'OH', 'OH+', 'H2O', 'H2O+', 'H3O+',\n            'O2',\n            'CO+', 'HCO+',\n            'S', 'S+', 'Si', 'Si+', 'Fe', 'Fe+',\n            'He', 'He+',\n        ]\n        self.n_tracked = len(self.tracked_species)\n        self.idx = {s: i for i, s in enumerate(self.tracked_species)}\n\n    def _abundances_to_dict(self, x_arr):\n        return {self.tracked_species[i]: max(x_arr[i], X_FLOOR)\n                for i in range(self.n_tracked)}\n\n    def _dict_to_array(self, x_dict):\n        return np.array([x_dict.get(s, X_FLOOR) for s in self.tracked_species])\n\n    def _rhs(self, t, y, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO):\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n        abundances = self._abundances_to_dict(y)\n\n        # PAH charge balance\n        x_PAH_total = 1e-7\n        n_e = max(abundances.get('e-', 1e-10), 1e-10) * n_H\n        if G0 > 0 and T > 0 and n_e > 0:\n            psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n            f_plus = psi / (psi + 500.0)\n            f_minus = 500.0 / (psi + 500.0) * 0.5\n            f_neutral = max(1.0 - f_plus - f_minus, 0.01)\n        else:\n            f_neutral, f_plus, f_minus = 0.5, 0.1, 0.4\n\n        abundances['PAH'] = x_PAH_total * f_neutral\n        abundances['PAH+'] = x_PAH_total * f_plus\n        abundances['PAH-'] = x_PAH_total * f_minus\n\n        dndt = self.network.compute_rates(\n            abundances, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO\n        )\n\n        dydt = np.zeros(self.n_tracked)\n        for i, sp in enumerate(self.tracked_species):\n            dydt[i] = dndt.get(sp, 0.0)\n        return dydt\n\n    def solve_steady_state(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                            x_init=None, f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0,\n                            t_max=3e16, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-20):\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        if x_init is None:\n            x_init = self._default_initial(G0, A_V)\n\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n        t_checkpoints = np.logspace(4, np.log10(t_max), 30)\n        y_current = y0.copy()\n        converged = False\n\n        for i in range(len(t_checkpoints) - 1):\n            t_span = (t_checkpoints[i], t_checkpoints[i+1])\n            try:\n                sol = solve_ivp(\n                    self._rhs, t_span, y_current,\n                    method='BDF',\n                    args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n                    rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n                    max_step=(t_span[1] - t_span[0]),\n                )\n                if sol.success:\n                    y_new = np.maximum(sol.y[:, -1], X_FLOOR)\n                    key_idx = [self.idx[s] for s in\n                              ['H', 'H2', 'C+', 'C', 'CO', 'O', 'e-']]\n                    y_key_old = y_current[key_idx]\n                    y_key_new = y_new[key_idx]\n                    mask = y_key_old > 1e-12\n                    if np.any(mask):\n                        rel_change = np.max(\n                            np.abs(y_key_new[mask] - y_key_old[mask]) /\n                            np.maximum(y_key_old[mask], 1e-20)\n                        )\n                        if rel_change < rtol and i > 5:\n                            converged = True\n                            y_current = y_new\n                            break\n                    y_current = y_new\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n\n        y_current = self._enforce_conservation(y_current, n_H)\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_current), converged\n\n    def solve_time_dependent(self, n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR,\n                              x_init, dt,\n                              f_shield_H2=1.0, f_shield_CO=1.0):\n        y0 = self._dict_to_array(x_init)\n        sol = solve_ivp(\n            self._rhs, (0, dt), y0,\n            method='BDF',\n            args=(n_H, T, G0, A_V, zeta_CR, f_shield_H2, f_shield_CO),\n            rtol=1e-5, atol=1e-25,\n        )\n        if sol.success:\n            y_final = np.maximum(sol.y[:, -1], X_FLOOR)\n        else:\n            y_final = y0\n        y_final = self._enforce_conservation(y_final, n_H)\n        return self._abundances_to_dict(y_final)\n\n    def _default_initial(self, G0, A_V):\n        \"\"\"\n        Set initial abundances. v0.2: metal depletion at depth,\n        CO initialized high at depth, proper x_e from ions.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n\n        x = {}\n        x_C_total = solar_abundances['C']\n        x_O_total = solar_abundances['O']\n        x_S_total = solar_abundances.get('S', 1.32e-5)\n        x_Si_total = solar_abundances.get('Si', 3.24e-5)\n        x_Fe_total = solar_abundances.get('Fe', 3.16e-5)\n        x_He = solar_abundances['He']\n\n        # Gas-phase metal fraction decreases with depth (depletion)\n        f_gas = max(0.01, 1.0 - A_V / 3.0) if A_V < 3.0 else 0.01\n\n        if A_V < 0.5:\n            x['H'] = 1.0\n            x['H2'] = 1e-6\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total\n            x['C'] = 1e-10\n            x['CO'] = 1e-15\n            x['O'] = x_O_total\n        elif A_V < 2.0:\n            fmol = (A_V - 0.5) / 1.5\n            x['H'] = 1.0 - fmol\n            x['H2'] = fmol / 2.0\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - 0.3 * fmol)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.2 * fmol\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.1 * fmol\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n        elif A_V < 5.0:\n            fCO = (A_V - 2.0) / 3.0\n            x['H'] = 0.01\n            x['H2'] = 0.495\n            x['C+'] = x_C_total * max(0.01, 1.0 - fCO * 3.0)\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * (1.0 - fCO) * 0.3\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * fCO * 0.9\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n        else:\n            x['H'] = 0.005\n            x['H2'] = 0.4975\n            x['C+'] = 1e-9\n            x['C'] = x_C_total * 0.05\n            x['CO'] = x_C_total * 0.95\n            x['O'] = x_O_total - x['CO']\n\n        # Metals: depleted at depth\n        x['S+'] = x_S_total * f_gas\n        x['S'] = x_S_total * (1.0 - f_gas)\n        x['Si+'] = x_Si_total * f_gas\n        x['Si'] = x_Si_total * (1.0 - f_gas)\n        x['Fe+'] = x_Fe_total * f_gas\n        x['Fe'] = x_Fe_total * (1.0 - f_gas)\n\n        # Electron fraction from ions\n        x['e-'] = (x.get('C+', 0) + x.get('S+', 0) + x.get('Si+', 0)\n                   + x.get('Fe+', 0) + 1e-8)\n\n        # Intermediates\n        x['H+'] = 1e-8\n        x['H-'] = 1e-14\n        x['H2+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H3+'] = 5e-10 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['OH'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 2 else 1e-9\n        x['OH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['H2O'] = 1e-7 if A_V > 3 else 1e-9\n        x['H2O+'] = 1e-14\n        x['H3O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['O2'] = 1e-8 if A_V > 3 else 1e-12\n        x['CH'] = 1e-10\n        x['CH+'] = 1e-13\n        x['CH2+'] = 1e-14\n        x['CO+'] = 1e-15\n        x['HCO+'] = 1e-9 if A_V > 2 else 1e-12\n        x['O+'] = 1e-13\n        x['He'] = x_He\n        x['He+'] = 1e-13\n\n        return x\n\n    def _enforce_conservation(self, y, n_H):\n        \"\"\"\n        Gentle conservation: only rescale dominant carriers, clamp\n        the scale factor to avoid destroying chemistry results.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.constants import solar_abundances\n        idx = self.idx\n\n        # ---- Hydrogen ----\n        H_major = y[idx['H']] + 2 * y[idx['H2']]\n        H_minor = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['H-']]\n                  + 2*y[idx['H2+']] + 3*y[idx['H3+']]\n                  + y[idx['OH']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                  + 2*y[idx['H2O']] + 2*y[idx['H2O+']]\n                  + 3*y[idx['H3O+']]\n                  + y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']]\n                  + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if H_major > 0:\n            target = max(1.0 - H_minor, 0.9)\n            scale = np.clip(target / H_major, 0.8, 1.2)\n            y[idx['H']] *= scale\n            y[idx['H2']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Carbon ----\n        C_total = solar_abundances['C']\n        C_major = y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['C']] + y[idx['CO']]\n        C_minor = (y[idx['CH']] + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                  + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if C_major > 0:\n            target = max(C_total - C_minor, C_total * 0.9)\n            scale = np.clip(target / C_major, 0.8, 1.2)\n            y[idx['C+']] *= scale\n            y[idx['C']] *= scale\n            y[idx['CO']] *= scale\n\n        # ---- Oxygen: adjust atomic O to balance ----\n        O_total = solar_abundances['O']\n        O_in_molecules = (y[idx['CO']] + y[idx['OH']] + y[idx['H2O']]\n                         + y[idx['O+']] + y[idx['OH+']]\n                         + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']]\n                         + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']])\n        if 'O2' in idx:\n            O_in_molecules += 2 * y[idx['O2']]\n        y[idx['O']] = max(O_total - O_in_molecules, X_FLOOR)\n\n        # ---- Charge ----\n        n_pos = (y[idx['H+']] + y[idx['C+']] + y[idx['S+']]\n                + y[idx['Si+']] + y[idx['Fe+']] + y[idx['O+']]\n                + y[idx['H2+']] + y[idx['H3+']]\n                + y[idx['OH+']] + y[idx['H2O+']] + y[idx['H3O+']]\n                + y[idx['CH+']] + y[idx['CH2+']]\n                + y[idx['CO+']] + y[idx['HCO+']]\n                + y[idx['He+']])\n        y[idx['e-']] = max(n_pos, X_FLOOR)\n\n        y = np.maximum(y, X_FLOOR)\n        return y\n",

      "code": "    parameter psi = G0 * sqrt(T) / (n_e), which controls the\n    balance between UV photoemission and electron recapture.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_H : float\n        H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n    T : float\n        Gas temperature [K]\n    G0 : float\n        FUV field [Habing units]\n    x_e : float\n        Electron fraction n_e/n_H\n    grain_param : dict, optional\n        Grain parameters. If None, uses standard ISM values.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Gamma_PE : float\n        PE heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    \"\"\"\n    if G0 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_e = x_e * n_H\n    if n_e <= 0:\n        n_e = 1e-10 * n_H  # floor\n\n    # Charging parameter\n    psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n\n    # Bakes & Tielens (1994) Eq. 41-42\n    # Heating efficiency epsilon(psi)\n    epsilon = 0.049 / (1.0 + (psi / 1925.0)**0.73) \\\n            + 0.037 * (T / 1e4)**0.7 / (1.0 + psi / 5000.0)\n\n    # Total PE heating rate\n    # Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n    Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H\n\n    return Gamma_PE\n\n\ndef photoelectric_heating_wd01(n_H, T, G0, x_e, R_V=3.1):\n    \"\"\"\n    PE heating from Weingartner & Draine (2001) - more accurate than BT94.\n\n    Uses their fit for the standard ISM grain size distribution.\n    \"\"\"\n    if G0 <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    n_e = max(x_e * n_H, 1e-10 * n_H)\n\n    # WD01 charging parameter (their Eq. 2)\n    phi_pah = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / (n_e * 1.0)  # for PAH fraction = 1\n\n    # WD01 heating rate (their Eq. 1 and Table 2 for R_V=3.1)\n    C0 = 5.22e-26  # erg/s\n    C1 = 3.46e-2\n    C2 = 5.11e-1\n    C3 = 1.83e4\n    C4 = 1.39e1\n",

At G0=5700 and n_H=1e4, BT94 gives:\n# Gamma_PE = 1.3e-24 * epsilon * G0 * n_H\n# With G0=5700, n_H=1e4: base = 1.3e-24 * 5700 * 1e4 = 7.41e-17\n# epsilon should be ~0.01-0.05 so Gamma ~ 7e-19 to 4e-18\n# We're getting 6e-19 which is LOW because epsilon is low\n\n# The problem: the n_e in the charging parameter is n_e = x_e * n_H\n# With x_e = 3e-4 (from C+) and n_H = 1e4: n_e = 3\n# psi = G0 * sqrt(T) / n_e = 5700 * 10 / 3 = 19000\n# At psi = 19000, epsilon is very small because grains are positively charged!\n\n# But the Bakes & Tielens formula is correct for these conditions.\n# The real fix is that in a true Orion Bar:\n# 1. n_H should produce a certain column to the star\n# 2. CellData.\"\"\"\n        d.x_HI = abundances.get('H', d.x_HI)\n        d.x_H2 = abundances.get('H2', d.x_H2)\n        d.x_Hp = abundances.get('H+', d.x_Hp)\n        d.x_Cp = abundances.get('C+', d.x_Cp)\n        d.x_C = abundances.get('C', d.x_C)\n        d.x_CO = abundances.get('CO', d.x_CO)\n        d.x_O = abundances.get('O', d.x_O)\n        d.x_OH = abundances.get('OH', d.x_OH)\n        d.x_H2O = abundances.get('H2O', d.x_H2O)\n        d.x_CHx = abundances.get('CH', d.x_CHx)\n        d.x_HCOp = abundances.get('HCO+', d.x_HCOp)\n        d.x_S = abundances.get('S', d.x_S)\n        d.x_Sp = abundances.get('S+', d.x_Sp)\n        d.x_Si = abundances.get('Si', d.x_Si)\n        d.x_Sip = abundances.get('Si+', d.x_Sip)\n        d.x_Fe = abundances.get('Fe', d.x_Fe)\n        d.x_Fep = abundances.get('Fe+', d.x_Fep)\n        d.x_Hep = abundances.get('He+', d.x_Hep)\n\n        # Electron fraction from charge balance\n        d.x_e = (d.x_Cp + d.x_Sp + d.x_Sip + d.x_Fep + d.x_Hp\n                + abundances.get('HCO+', 0) + abundances.get('H3+', 0))\n\n    def _print_timing(self):\n        \"\"\"Print timing breakdown.\"\"\"\n        total = sum(self.timing.values())\n The solution converges to T=100 because the\n# solver oscillates: high T -> destroy H2 -> no pumping -> low T.\n# \n# FIX: The H2 pumping heating should use the ATTENUATED pumping rate\n# properly. Also need to make the H2 destruction self-consistent.\n# For now, let's cap the pumping rate energy to be no more than\n# the PE heating at the surface (physically, H2 pumping shouldn't\n# dominate at x_H2 < 0.01 on the surface).\n\nprint()\nprint('FIX: Cap H2 pumping heating to not exceed PE heating at surface')\nprint('Also: surface T oscillation between hot (destroy H2) and cold')\nprint('This is a well-known issue in PDR codes - needs damping.')\n\" 2>&1",

      "description": "Fix H2 UV pumping heating rate - use correct fluorescence rate and add density-dependent efficiency",
      "new_str": "def h2_uv_pumping_heating(n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2, T, n_cr=1e4):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 UV fluorescent pumping.\n\n    FUV photons excite H2 to excited electronic states, which cascade\n    back producing vibrationally excited H2 in the ground state.\n    Collisional de-excitation of these levels heats the gas.\n\n    The FUV pumping rate is ~9x the photodissociation rate (since ~10%\n    of absorptions lead to dissociation, ~90% to fluorescence).\n    But the effective heating depends on the competition between\n    collisional de-excitation (heating) and radiative cascade (no heating).\n\n    Following Burton, Hollenbach & Tielens (1990) and\n    R\u00f6llig+ (2006) Eq. 1.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pumping rate: ~9x the photodissociation rate\n    # Photodissociation rate: 3.3e-11 * G0 * exp(-3.74*A_V) * f_shield\n    # Pumping rate: ~9 * 3.3e-11 = 3.0e-10 per H2 molecule\n    k_pump = 3.0e-10 * G0 * np.exp(-3.74 * A_V) * f_shield_H2  # s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    if n_H2 <= 0 or k_pump <= 0:\n        return 0.0\n\n    # Average energy of vibrationally excited H2: ~2 eV per pump event\n    # But only a fraction goes to heating (rest re-radiated)\n    E_vib = 2.0 * eV_to_erg\n\n    # Heating efficiency: fraction of pumped energy that heats the gas\n    # At low density (n << n_cr): energy radiated away (IR fluorescence)\n    # At high density (n >> n_cr): collisional de-excitation heats gas\n    # n_cr for v=1 J=0: ~1e4 cm\u207b\u00b3 for H-H2 collisions\n    # For higher v levels: n_cr can be >1e5\n    # Use effective n_cr that accounts for the distribution of v levels\n    n_cr_eff = 3e4  # effective critical density\n\n    f_heat = n_H / (n_H + n_cr_eff)\n\n    return k_pump * n_H2 * E_vib * f_heat",
      "old_str": "def h2_uv_pumping_heating(n_H, x_H2, G0, A_V, f_shield_H2, T, n_cr=1e4):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heating from H2 UV fluorescent pumping.\n\n    FUV photons excite H2 to excited electronic states, which cascade\n    back producing vibrationally excited H2 in the ground state.\n    Collisional de-excitation of these levels heats the gas.\n\n    The efficiency depends on whether the gas is above or below the\n    critical density for vibrational de-excitation (~1e4 cm\u207b\u00b3).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pumping rate (similar to photodissociation but ~10x more absorptions)\n    k_pump = 3.3e-10 * G0 * np.exp(-3.74 * A_V) * f_shield_H2  # s\u207b\u00b9\n\n    n_H2 = x_H2 * n_H\n\n    # Energy per de-excitation event: ~2 eV if collisionally de-excited\n    E_vib = 2.0 * eV_to_erg\n\n    # Fraction that goes to heating (depends on density vs critical density)\n    f_heat = n_H / (n_H + n_cr)\n\n    return k_pump * n_H2 * E_vib * f_heat",

G0 = 1e4; zeta_CR = 2e-16\nN_H_total = 10.0 * NH_per_AV; depth = N_H_total / n_H\ncell_size = depth / n_cells\n\ngrid = OctreeGrid(box_size=depth, n_base=1, max_level=0)\ngrid.root_nodes = []\nhalf_depth = depth / 2.0\nfor i in range(n_cells):\n    center = np.array([-half_depth + (i + 0.5) * cell_size, 0.0, 0.0])\n    node = OctreeNode(center, cell_size, level=0)\n    node.data = CellData(); node.data.n_H = n_H\n    grid.root_nodes.append(node)\ngrid.n_base = 1; grid.base_cell_size = depth; grid.invalidate_cache()\n\nfor i, node in enumerate(grid.root_nodes):\n    x = (i + 0.5) * cell_size; N_H_col = n_H * x\n    node.data.A_V = N_H_col * AV_per_NH\n    node.data.G0 = G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * node.data.A_V)\n    node.data.N_H_total = N_H_col\n    A = node.data.A_V\n    if A < 0.5:\n        node.data.x_HI=1.0; node.data.x_H2=1e-6; node.data.x_Cp=2.69e-4\n        node.data.x_C=1e-10; node.data.x_CO=1e-15; node.data.x_O=4.9e-4; node.data.T_gas=500\n    elif A < 2:\n        f=(A-0.5)/1.5; node.data.x_HI=1-f; node.data.x_H2=f/2\n        node.data.x_Cp=2.69e-4*(1-0.3*f); node.data.x_C=2.69e-4*0.2*f\n        node.data.x_CO=2.69e-4*0.1*f; node.data.x_O=4.9e-4-node.data.x_CO; node.data.T_gas=300\n    elif A < 5:\n        fCO=(A-2)/3; node.data.x_HI=0.01; node.data.x_H2=0.495\n        node.data.x_Cp=max(2.69e-4*(1-3*fCO),1e-9); node.data.x_C=2.69e-4*(1-fCO)*0.3\n        node.data.x_CO=2.69e-4*fCO*0.9; node.data.x_O=4.9e-4-node.data.x_CO; node.data.T_gas=80\n    else:\n        node.data.x_HI=0.005; node.data.x_H2=0.4975; node.data.x_Cp=1e-9\n        node.data.x_C=2.69e-4*0.05; node.data.x_CO=2.69e-4*0.95\n        node.data.x_O=4.9e-4-node.data.x_CO; node.data.T_gas=20\n    node.data.x_e = node.data.x_Cp + 1e-5\n\nclass Simple1DRT:\n    def __init__(s, grid, G0): s.grid=grid; s.G0_external=G0\n    def compute_1d_column_densities(s, axis=0, direction=1):\n        leaves=sorted(s.grid.get_leaves(), key=lambda l:l.center[0])\n        N_H=N_H2=N_CO=0\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            ds=leaf.size\n            leaf.data.N_H_total=N_H+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*ds\n            leaf.data.N_H2=N_H2+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_H2*2*ds\n            leaf.data.N_CO=N_CO+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_CO*ds\n            leaf.data.A_V=leaf.data.N_H_total*AV_per_NH\n            leaf.data.G0=s.G0_external*np.exp(-1.8*leaf.data.A_V)\n            N_H+=leaf.data.n_H*ds; N_H2+=leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_H2*2*ds\n            N_CO+=leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_CO*ds\n\nsolver = PDRSolver(grid, G0_external=G0, zeta_CR_0=zeta_CR, use_1d_rt=True, cr_model='M')\nsolver.rt = Simple1DRT(grid, G0)\nt0 = time.time()\nconverged = solver.run(max_iterations=8, convergence_tol=0.05, verbose=True)\nprint(f'Total: {time.time()-t0:.0f}s')\n\nleaves = sorted(grid.get_leaves(), key=lambda l: l.center[0])\nprint(f'\\n{\\\"=\\\"*105}')\nprint(f'{\\\"A_V\\\":>6s} {\\\"T_gas\\\":>7s} {\\\"T_d\\\":>5s} {\\\"x(H)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(H2)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(C+)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(C)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(CO)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(O)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(e)\\\":>9s} {\\\"x(OH)\\\":>9s} {\\\"G0\\\":>7s}')\nprint(f'{\\\"=\\\"*105}')\nfor leaf in leaves:\n    d = leaf.data\n    print(f'{d.A_V:6.2f} {d.T_gas:7.1f} {d.T_dust:5.1f} '\n          f'{d.x_HI:9.2e} {d.x_H2:9.2e} '\n          f'{d.x_Cp:9.2e} {d.x_C:9.2e} {d.x_CO:9.2e} '\n          f'{d.x_O:9.2e} {d.x_e:9.2e} {d.x_OH:9.2e} {d.G0:7.1f}')\n\" 2>&1",

The photodissociation rate at A_V=0.21 is:\n# k_pd = 3.3e-11 * 6873 * exp(-3.74*0.21) * f_shield ~ 1e-7 s-1\n# H2 formation rate: 3e-17 * sqrt(T/100) * x_H * n_H = 3e-17 * 2.2 * 1 * 1e4 = 6.6e-13 s-1\n# Equilibrium: x_H2 = R_form / (2*k_pd) = 6.6e-13/(2e-7) = 3.3e-6\n#\n# So x_H2 should be 3.3e-6, not 8.5e-4 (250x too high!)\n# The chemistry solver is not destroying H2 fast enough at the surface.\n# The issue is that the chemistry uses f_shield_H2 = 1 for the first cell,\n# but self-shielding at N_H2 ~ a few x 10^14 is already strong.\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom prism3d.radiative_transfer.shielding import f_shield_H2\n\n# N_H2 at first cell midpoint: ~8.5e-4 * 1e4 * 0.5*cell = \n# cell_size ~ 1.87e18/24 = 7.8e16 cm\n# N_H2 ~ 8.5e-4 * 1e4 * 3.9e16 = 3.3e17 cm-2\nN_H2 = 3.3e17\nf = f_shield_H2(N_H2, T=500)\nprint(f'f_shield(N_H2={N_H2:.1e}) = {f:.4e}')\n# So f_shield ~ 0.0013. With this, k_pd = 1e-7 * 0.0013 = 1.3e-10\n# x_H2_eq = 6.6e-13 / (2*1.3e-10) = 2.5e-3\n# Still high because the COLUMN density counts self-shielding.\n# The problem is subtle: the surface cell has H2 that is self-shielded \n# by its own column. This is correct! The surface temperature should\n# indeed be set by the balance including this H2.\n# \n# For a real PDR code, the surface is at T~500K determined by PE heating.\n# The pumping heating DOES contribute but its effect is usually less than PE\n# because the H2 at the surface is in the UV-pumped state (radiating).\n#\n# The fix: PE heating needs to be STRONGER. n_H = 1e4; G0 = 1e4; zeta_CR = 2e-16\nN_H_total = 10.0 * NH_per_AV; depth = N_H_total / n_H; cell_size = depth / n_cells\nx_C = gas_phase_abundances['C']; x_O = gas_phase_abundances['O']\nx_S = gas_phase_abundances['S']; half_depth = depth / 2.0\nfor i in range(n_cells):\n    center = np.array([-half_depth + (i + 0.5) * cell_size, 0.0, 0.0])\n    node = OctreeNode(center, cell_size, level=0); node.data = CellData(); node.data.n_H = n_H\n    grid.root_nodes.append(node)\ngrid.n_base = 1; A = node.data.A_V\n    if A < 0.5:\n        node.data.x_HI=1.0; node.data.x_H2=1e-6; node.data.x_Cp=x_C; node.data.x_C=1e-10\n        node.data.x_CO=1e-15; node.data.x_O=x_O; node.data.T_gas=500\n    elif A < 2:\n        f=(A-0.5)/1.5; node.data.x_HI=1-f; node.data.x_H2=f/2; node.data.x_Cp=x_C*(1-0.3*f)\n        node.data.x_C=x_C*0.2*f; node.data.x_CO=x_C*0.1*f; node.data.x_O=x_O-node.data.x_CO; node.data.T_gas=300\n    elif A < 5:\n        fCO=(A-2)/3; s.G0_external=G0\n    def compute_1d_column_densities(s, axis=0, direction=1):\n        leaves=sorted(s.grid.get_leaves(), key=lambda l:l.center[0])\n        N_H=N_H2=N_CO=0\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            ds=leaf.size\n            leaf.data.N_H_total=N_H+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*ds\n            leaf.data.N_H2=N_H2+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_H2*2*ds\n            leaf.data.N_CO=N_CO+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_CO*ds\n            leaf.data.A_V=leaf.data.N_H_total*AV_per_NH\n            leaf.data.G0=s.G0_external*np.exp(-1.8*leaf.data.A_V)\n            N_H+=leaf.data.n_H*ds; half_depth = depth / 2.0\nfor i in range(n_cells):\n    center = np.array([-half_depth + (i + 0.5) * cell_size, 0.0, 0.0])\n    node = OctreeNode(center, cell_size, level=0); node.data = CellData(); node.data.n_H = n_H\n    grid.root_nodes.append(node)\ngrid.n_base = 1; node.data = CellData(); node.data.n_H = n_H\n    grid.root_nodes.append(node)\ngrid.n_base = 1; grid.base_cell_size = depth; grid.invalidate_cache()\n\nfor i, node in enumerate(grid.root_nodes):\n    x = (i + 0.5) * cell_size; s.G0_external=G0\n    def compute_1d_column_densities(s, axis=0, direction=1):\n        leaves=sorted(s.grid.get_leaves(), key=lambda l:l.center[0])\n        N_H=N_H2=N_CO=0\n        for leaf in leaves:\n            ds=leaf.size\n            leaf.data.N_H_total=N_H+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*ds\n            leaf.data.N_H2=N_H2+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_H2*2*ds\n            leaf.data.N_CO=N_CO+0.5*leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_CO*ds\n            leaf.data.A_V=leaf.data.N_H_total*AV_per_NH\n            leaf.data.G0=s.G0_external*np.exp(-1.8*leaf.data.A_V)\n            N_H+=leaf.data.n_H*ds; N_H2+=leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_H2*2*ds\n            N_CO+=leaf.data.n_H*leaf.data.x_CO*ds\n\nsolver = PDRSolver(grid, G0_external=G0, zeta_CR_0=zeta_CR, use_1d_rt=True, cr_model='M')\nsolver.rt = Simple1DRT(grid, G0)\nconverged = solver.run(max_iterations=12, convergence_tol=0.05, verbose=True)\n\n# Save results for plotting\nleaves = sorted(grid.get_leaves(), key=lambda l: l.center[0])\nresult = {k: [] for k in ['A_V','T_gas','T_dust','x_HI','x_H2','x_Cp','x_C','x_CO','x_O','x_e','G0']}\nfor leaf in leaves:\n    d = leaf.data\n    result['A_V'].append(d.A_V);     "text": "## v0.2 Results \u2014 What Changed and Where We Are\n\n### Fixes Applied\n\n**Chemistry network** \u2014 expanded from 51 to ~75 reactions, adding the critical missing pieces: CR-induced photodissociation of CO/OH/H\u2082O/C (the dominant destruction mechanism in shielded gas), O\u2082 chemistry (C + O\u2082 \u2192 CO + O formation pathway), additional recombination channels (OH\u207a, H\u2082O\u207a, CH\u2082\u207a), and PAH-assisted recombination for Si\u207a and Fe\u207a.\n\n**Gas-phase depletion** \u2014 Added realistic ISM depletions (Jenkins 2009): C at 40%, O at 60%, Si at 3%, Fe at 0.6% of solar in gas phase. This dramatically reduces x(e\u207b) at the surface from ~10\u207b\u00b3 to ~10\u207b\u2074, which controls the PE heating efficiency.\n\n**Chemistry solver** \u2014 Extended integration time from 10\u00b9\u2075 to 3\u00d710\u00b9\u2076 s (~1 Myr), improved initial conditions at depth (CO-rich, metal-depleted), and gentler conservation enforcement that doesn't destroy the CO the solver builds up.\n\n**H\u2082 pumping heating** \u2014 Fixed critical density to n_cr=3\u00d710\u2074 (appropriate for the vibrational level distribution) which prevents pumping from overwhelming PE at the surface.\n\n**Temperature damping** \u2014 Added 50% damping on T updates to suppress the classic H/H\u2082 front oscillation.\n\n### What's Working Well\n\nThe **C\u207a\u2192C\u2192CO transition** is now clear with CO peaking at ~10\u207b\u2074 around A_V=2.7, and **C dominates at depth** (x_C \u2248 10\u207b\u2074) with a thin CO layer in between \u2014 this is physically reasonable for these conditions. The **H\u2192H\u2082 transition** is sharp at A_V\u22481. The **temperature profile** drops smoothly from 100K at surface \u2192 10K at depth.\n\n### Remaining Issues to Tackle Next\n\nThe surface temperature is ~100K instead of ~500K (PE heating efficiency is still marginal for these conditions \u2014 the Bakes & Tielens formula underestimates at high G\u2080/n). CO doesn't fully dominate C at high A_V because the ion-molecule chain (O + H\u2083\u207a \u2192 OH\u207a \u2192 ... \u2192 OH \u2192 CO) isn't producing enough OH at T=10K. And there's still a mild oscillation at the H/H\u2082 front.\n\nWant me to push on the **surface temperature** (switch to WD01 PE heating), **CO at depth** (full water chemistry network), or pivot to the **3D turbulent cloud** and **ML chemistry** features?",

J.; Le Bourlot, J.; Le Petit, F.; Meijerink, R.; Morata, O.; Ossenkopf, V.; Roueff, E.; Shaw, G.; Spaans, M.; Sternberg, A.; Stutzki, J.; Thi, W. -F.; van Dishoeck, E. F.; van Hoof, P. A. M.; Viti, S.; Wolfire, M. G., Astronomy and Astrophysics, Volume 467, Issue 1, May III 2007, pp.187-206\n\nPhoton Dominated Regions (PDRs) play an important role in modern astrophysics as they are responsible for many emission characteristics of the ISM, and dominate the infrared and sub-millimeter spectra of star forming regions and galaxies as a whole. Theoretical models addressing the structure of PDRs have been available for approximately 30 years and have evolved into advanced computer codes accounting for a growing number of physical effects with increasing accuracy. These codes have been developed with different goals in mind: some are geared to efficiently model a particular type of region, e.g. HII regions, protoplanetary disks, planetary nebulae, diffuse clouds, etc.; others emphasize a strict handling of the micro-physical processes in full detail (e.g. wavelength dependent absorption), but at the cost of increased computing time. Yet others aim at efficient and rapid calculation of large model grids for comparison with observational data, which comes at the cost of pragmatic app\n\nAs a result, the different models have focused on the detailed simulation of different processes determining the structure in the different regions while using only rough approximations for other processes. The model setups vary greatly among different model codes. This includes the assumed model geometry, their physical and chemical structure, the choice of free parameters, and other details. Consequently it is not always straightforward to directly compare the results from different PDR codes. Taking into account that there are multiple ways of implementing physical effects in numerical codes, it is obvious that the model output of different PDR codes can differ from each other. As a result, significant variations in the physical and chemical PDR structure predicted by the various PDR codes can occur. This divergency would prevent a unique interpretation of observed data in terms of the parameters of the observed clouds.",

        }

        "text": "We identified a number of key processes governing the chemical network which have been treated differently in the various codes such as the effect of PAHs on the electron density or the temperature dependence of the dissociation of CO by cosmic ray induced secondary photons, and defined a proper common treatment. We established a comprehensive set of reference models for ongoing and future PDR model bench-marking and were able to increase the agreement in model predictions for all benchmark models significantly. Nevertheless, the remaining spread in the computed observables such as the atomic fine-structure line intensities serves as a warning that there is still a considerable uncertainty when interpreting astronomical data with our models. ... Published in Astronomy &amp; Astrophysics, v. 467, no. 1, p. 187-206. Reproduced with permission from Astronomy &amp; Astrophysics, \u00a9 ESO 2007 ... R\u00f6llig, M.; Abel, Nicholas Paul; Bell, T.; Bensch, F.; Black, J.; Ferland, Gary J.; Jonkheid, B.; Kamp, I\n\nWe identified a number of key processes governing the chemical network which have been treated differently in the various codes such as the effect of PAHs on the electron density or the temperature dependence of the dissociation of CO by cosmic ray induced secondary photons, and defined a proper common treatment. We established a comprehensive set of reference models for ongoing and future PDR model bench-marking and were able to increase the agreement in model predictions for all benchmark models significantly. Nevertheless, the remaining spread in the computed observables such as the atomic fine-structure line intensities serves as a warning that there is still a considerable uncertainty when interpreting astronomical data with our models. ... Published in Astronomy & Astrophysics, v. 467, no. 1, p. 187-206. Reproduced with permission from Astronomy & Astrophysics, \u00a9 ESO 2007 ... R\u00f6llig, M.; Abel, Nicholas Paul; Bell, T.; Bensch, F.; Black, J.; Ferland, Gary J.; Jonkheid, B.; Kamp, I\n\nAims. We present a comparison between independent computer codes, modeling the physics and chemistry of interstellar photon dominated regions (PDRs). Our goal was to understand the mutual differences in the PDR codes and their effects on the physical and chemical structure of the model clouds, and to converge the output of different codes to a common solution. Methods. A number of benchmark models have been created, covering low and high gas densities n = 103,105.5 cm-3 and far ultraviolet intensities \u03c7 = 10, 105 in units of the Draine field (FUV: 6\n\nWe present a comparison between independent computer codes, modeling the physics and chemistry of interstellar photon dominated regions (PDRs). Our goal was to understand the mutual differences in the PDR codes and their effects on the physical and chemical structure of the model clouds, and to converge the output of different codes to a common solution. Methods. A number of benchmark models have been created, covering low and high gas densities n = 103,105.5 cm-3 and far ultraviolet intensities \u03c7 = 10, 105 in units of the Draine field (FUV: 6 < h\u03bd < 13.6 eV). The benchmark models were computed in two ways: one set assuming constant temperatures, thus testing the consistency of the chemical network and photo-processes, and a second set determining the temperature self consistently by solving the thermal balance, thus testing the modeling of the heating and cooling mechanisms accounting for the detailed energy balance throughout the clouds.\n\nM. From: Markus Roellig [view email] [v1] Thu, 8 Feb 2007 14:59:26 UTC (927 KB) ... View a PDF of the paper titled A PDR-Code Comparison Study, by M. Roellig and 23 other authors ... arXivLabs is a framework that allows collaborators to develop and share new arXiv features directly on our website. Both individuals and organizations that work with arXivLabs have embraced and accepted our values of openness, community, excellence, and user data privacy. arXiv is committed to these values and only works with partners that adhere to them.\n\nWe present a comparison between independent computer codes, modeling the physics and chemistry of interstellar photon dominated regions (PDRs). Our goal was to understand the mutual differences in the PDR codes and their effects on the physical and chemical structure of the model clouds, and to converge the output of different codes to a common solution. A number of benchmark models have been created, covering low and high gas densities and far ultraviolet intensities. The benchmark models were computed in two ways: one set assuming constant temperatures, thus testing the consistency of the chemical network and photo-processes, and a second set determining the temperature selfconsistently. We investigated the impact of PDR geometry and agreed on the comparison of results from spherical and plane-parallel PDR models. We identified a number of key processes governing the chemical network which have been treated differently in the various codes, and defined a proper common treatment.\n\nView a PDF of the paper titled A PDR-Code Comparison Study, by M.         "text": "PDR modeling is my main focus. I am responsible for supporting and developing the KOSMA-\u03c4 PDR code and its applications. KOSMA-\u03c4 features a spherical geometry with isotropic FUV illumination and was part of the large PDR Comparison Benchmark (R\u00f6llig et al. 2007).\n\nPDR modeling is my main focus. I am responsible for supporting and developing the KOSMA-\u03c4 PDR code and its applications. KOSMA-\u03c4 features a spherical geometry with isotropic FUV illumination and was part of the large PDR Comparison Benchmark (R\u00f6llig et al. 2007). The code is a further development of the plane-parallel PDR code from A. Sternberg, Tel Aviv, and has originally been built to study the C+ - C - CO stratification in PDRs. We constantly update the code and expand its capabilities. Present development projects include the inclusion of a much more refined dust treatment by coupling KOSMA-\u03c4 with a full dust radiative transfer code by R. Szczerba, Torun. This will provide the full continuum properties of the model clouds including observable SED\u2019s given any dust composition. Additionally, we will also include the stochastic photo-heating of the very small grains (VSG\u2019s), which is bealived to be an important contribution to the total energy balance of the cloud.\n\nThe main work goes into the improved modelling of the microphysical and chemical processes in the KOSMA-tau PDR model. This means in particular accounting for non-stationary effects: to include the full time-dependent chemistry to the model. This will allow to model the impact of : ... Markus R\u00f6llig and Yoko Okada are Co-Investigators of the ERS project \u201cRadiative Feedback from Massive Stars as Traced by Multiband Imaging and Spectroscopic Mosaics\u201d. As preparation for the launch of JWST, we are updating KOSMA-tau to solve the full H2 problem (full ro-vib structure). In addition we need to account for non-stationary PAH heating. This is work in progress. KOSMA-tau results will be made available on the PDR model database ISMDB of the Meudon group in order to allow a comparison between both models. ... Schneider, N. ; R\u00f6llig, M. ; Polehampton, E.\n\nSchulz, A.; Henkel, C.; Muders, D.; Mao, R. Q.; R\u00f6llig, M.; Mauersberger, R., Astronomy and Astrophysics, Volume 466, Issue 2, May I 2007, pp.467-479 \u00b7\n\nMolecular cloud and star formation (SF) are the fundamental processes in the Cosmic Cycle of Matter. This cycle has shaped the evolution of the universe from the formation of the first generation of stars to its present appearance. The interaction of star formation with the interstellar medium (ISM) is most prominently marked by so-called photon dominated regions (PDR). My research goal is to contribute to a better understanding of the physics and the chemistry in PDRs. If we really want to understand planet and star formation we need to be able to reliably interpret the emission from PDRs. One of my main instruments of choice to achieve this goal is numerical modelling and I am the leading German expert on the numerical modelling of PDRs. Models are not just a tool to better understand your data. They can be a tool to better understand the physics of the universe.",

      {
        "type": "knowledge",
        "title": "A photon dominated region code comparison study | Astronomy & Astrophysics (A&A)",
        "url": "https://www.aanda.org/articles/aa/abs/2007/19/aa5918-06/aa5918-06.html",
        "metadata": {
          "type": "webpage_metadata",
          "site_domain": "aanda.org",
          "favicon_url": "https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?sz=64&domain=aanda.org",
          "site_name": "Astronomy & Astrophysics"
